# Grasshopper.Kernel

Auto-generated from vendor docs for grasshopper 8.0. 214 types in this namespace.

## GH_ActiveObject (class)

Basic implementation of IGH_ActiveObject interface. Derive from this class rather than implementing IGH_ActiveObject to make your life easier.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ActiveObject.htm)

### Constructors
- `protected GH_ActiveObject(IGH_InstanceDescription Tag)` — Initializes a new instance of the GH_ActiveObject class
- `protected GH_ActiveObject(string sName, string sAbbreviation, string sDescription, string sCategory, string sSubCategory)` — Initializes a new instance of the GH_ActiveObject class

### Methods
#### `public virtual void AddRuntimeMessage(GH_RuntimeMessageLevel level, string text)`

Add a new message to this object. Valid message type flags are Warning and Error. If the Message string is empty or zero-length no message is added.

**Parameters:**
- `level` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_RuntimeMessageLevel) — Type of message. Only Warnings and Errors are recorded.
- `text` (System.String) — Content of message.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ActiveObject_AddRuntimeMessage.htm)

#### `public virtual void AddedToDocument(GH_Document document)`

This method will be called when an object is added to a document. Override this method if you want to handle such events.

- `document` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_Document) — Document that now owns this object.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_DocumentObject_AddedToDocument.htm)

#### `public virtual void AppendAdditionalMenuItems(ToolStripDropDown menu)`

Override this function if you want to add additional items to the object context menu. Default items already included in the menu toolstrip are: 1. Name2. Preview3. Warnings4. Errors5. [additional items will be inserted here]6. Help

**Parameters:**
- `menu` (System.Windows.Forms.ToolStripDropDown)

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ActiveObject_AppendAdditionalMenuItems.htm)

#### `public override bool AppendMenuItems(ToolStripDropDown menu)`

This function is called when a context menu is about to be displayed. Override it to set custom items. GH_ActiveObject will already populate the menu with default items, if you merely wish to insert object-specific menu item, consider overriding AppendAdditionalMenuItems instead.

**Parameters:**
- `menu` (System.Windows.Forms.ToolStripDropDown) — Menu object to populate.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — If true, the menu will be displayed, if false the menu will be supressed.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ActiveObject_AppendMenuItems.htm)

#### `public virtual void ExpirePreview(bool redraw)`

Call this function when you suspect that the preview has expired for this object. This will cause the display cache to be eradicated.

**Parameters:**
- `redraw` (System.Boolean)

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_DocumentObject_ExpirePreview.htm)

#### `public override void ExpireSolution(bool recompute)`

Informs the document that owns this object that the solution has expired. The current object will be set to BLANK as a result. This method is recursive, it will also expire any and all objects which depend on this object. If you want a less destructive expiration, consider using ClearData(). If this object is already Blank, you should consider not expiring it.

**Parameters:**
- `recompute` (System.Boolean) — If True, the document will be instructed to solve the new state.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ActiveObject_ExpireSolution.htm)

#### `protected internal virtual string HtmlHelp_Source()`

Return a String which contains HTML formatted source for the help topic. If you want to pass a URL that points to a remote page, then prefix the URL with a GOTO: tag, like so: GOTO:http://www.YourWebAddressHere.com

**Remarks:** You can use the GUI.GH_HtmlFormatter class to generate an HTML page which conforms to Grasshopper Help standards

**Returns:** `String` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_DocumentObject_HtmlHelp_Source.htm)

#### `public virtual bool ReadFull(GH_IReader reader)`

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success, false on failure.

#### `public void TriggerAutoSave(GH_AutoSaveTrigger trigger)`

Triggers the AutoSave function on the owner document with a custom flag.

**Parameters:**
- `trigger` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_AutoSaveTrigger) — Reason for the autosave operation. It is possible that a user has decided to avoid autosave events for specific types, so if you can, try and provide a correct trigger flag.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_DocumentObject_TriggerAutoSave_1.htm)

#### `public void TriggerAutoSave(GH_AutoSaveTrigger trigger, Guid id)`

Triggers the AutoSave function on the owner document with a custom flag.

**Parameters:**
- `trigger` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_AutoSaveTrigger) — Reason for the autosave operation. It is possible that a user has decided to avoid autosave events for specific types, so if you can, try and provide a correct trigger flag.
- `id` (System.Guid) — ID of autosave event. Consecutive autosave requests with the same ID will be ignored.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_DocumentObject_TriggerAutoSave_2.htm)

#### `public void TriggerAutoSave(Guid id)`

Triggers the AutoSave function on the owner document with the object_changed flag.

**Parameters:**
- `id` (System.Guid) — ID of autosave event. Consecutive autosave requests with the same ID will be ignored.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_DocumentObject_TriggerAutoSave_3.htm)

#### `protected virtual void ValuesChanged()`

Override this method if you want to respond to changes to the value table. The base implementation is empty, so you don't have to call it.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_DocumentObject_ValuesChanged.htm)

#### `public override bool Write(GH_IWriter writer)`

(Overrides GH_DocumentObject.Write(GH_IWriter).)

**Parameters:**
- `writer` (GH_IO.Serialization.GH_IWriter)

**Returns:** `Boolean` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ActiveObject_Write.htm)

#### `public virtual bool WriteFull(GH_IWriter writer)`

GH_InstanceDescription does not by default serialize all fields. Use this function to write all fields to the archive. This method is compatible with the default Write()/Read() operations.

**Parameters:**
- `writer` (GH_IO.Serialization.GH_IWriter) — Writer for serialization.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_InstanceDescription_WriteFull.htm)

### Properties
- `Attributes` (IGH_Attributes, get/set) — Gets or sets the attributes that are associated with this object. Only set custom attributes if you know what you are doing.
- `Category` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the Category in which this object belongs. If HasCategory() returns false, this field has no meaning.
- `ComponentGuid` (Guid, get) — Returns a consistent ID for this object type. Every object must supply a unique and unchanging ID that is used to identify objects of the same type.
- `Description` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the description of the object. This field typically remains fixed during the lifetime of an object.
- `Exposure` (GH_Exposure, get) — Gets the exposure of this object in the Graphical User Interface. The default is to expose everywhere.
- `HasCategory` (Boolean, get) — Gets whether or not the Category field has been set.
- `HasSubCategory` (Boolean, get) — Gets whether or not the SubCategory field has been set.
- `Icon` (Bitmap, get) — Override this function to supply a custom icon (24x24 pixels). The result of this property is cached, so don't worry if icon retrieval is not very fast.
- `Icon_24x24` (Bitmap, get) — The icon associated with this object.
- `Icon_24x24_Locked` (Bitmap, get) — The greyscale icon of this object.
- `IconCapableUI` (Boolean, get) — By default the NickName menu item supports the Icon Mode override toggle. If your UI is not capable of displaying icons, then override this property and return False.
- `IconDisplayMode` (GH_IconDisplayMode, get/set) — Gets the current display mode of the object.
- `InstanceDescription` (String, get) — Gets a description of the current state of the object. This field is usually the same as the Description() field, but it might be variable when overridden.
- `InstanceGuid` (Guid, get) — Gets the ID of this runtime instance.
- `IsDataProvider` (Boolean, get) — Returns a boolean indicating whether or not the current object can seed data.
- `Keywords` (IEnumerable<String>, get) — Gets a list of additional keywords that describe the object. Typically this list is empty but you can override this property to aid in object searches.
- `Locked` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets the enabled flag of this object. Disabled objects are ignored during solutions.
- `MutableNickName` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value that enables Nick name changes through the menu. The default is TRUE.
- `Name` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the name of the object. This field typically remains fixed during the lifetime of an object.
- `NickName` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the nickname of the object. This field can be changed by the user.
- `Obsolete` (Boolean, get) — Gets whether this object is obsolete. Default implementation returns true if the class name contains the string "OBSOLETE" or if this class has been decorated with the ObsoleteAttribute. You are free to override this if you want, but I suggest adding the ObsoleteAttribute instead.
- `Phase` (GH_SolutionPhase, get/set) — Gets or sets the solution phase this object is currenly in.
- `ProcessorTime` (TimeSpan, get) — Gets the most recent processor time profiler value.
- `RuntimeMessageLevel` (GH_RuntimeMessageLevel, get) — Returns the worst case flag for the current object If the object contains at least 1 error, the result is Error.If the object contains at least 1 warning, the result is Warning.If the object contains at least 1 message, the result is Remark.If the object contains no errors, no warnings and no messages, the result is Blank.
- `SubCategory` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the SubCategory in which this object belongs. If HasSubCategory() returns false, this field has no meaning.
- `m_attributes` (IGH_Attributes, get) — (Inherited from GH_DocumentObject.)

**Signature:** `public event IGH_DocumentObject.PreviewExpiredEventHandler PreviewExpired`

Raised whenever the display (in the Rhino viewports) of a certain object becomes invalid.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/E_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_DocumentObject_PreviewExpired.htm)

## GH_Arrange (enum)

Enumerates possible types of object arrangement.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Arrange.htm)

### Values
- `MoveToFront` = `0` — Put the objects on the top of the stack.
- `MoveForwards` = `1` — Objects are inserted above the first occluding object.
- `MoveToBack` = `2` — Put the objects on the bottom of the stack.
- `MoveBackwards` = `3` — Objects are inserted below the first occluded object.

## GH_AssemblyFolderInfo (struct)

Represents a folder from which GHA files are loaded.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_AssemblyFolderInfo.htm)

### Constructors

### Methods
#### `public override string ToString()`

(Overrides ValueType.ToString().)

**Returns:** `String` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_AssemblyFolderInfo_ToString.htm)

### Properties
- `SearchOption` (SearchOption, get) — Gets the search option to use when looking for GHA files in this folder.
- `Folder` (String, get) — The actual full path to the folder.
- `Type` (GH_PluginFolderType, get) — Identifies the type of assembly folder.

## GH_AssemblyInfo (class)

Implement this interface once (and once only) in a GHA project if you wish to add additional information about your library to Grasshopper. Note you must supply an empty constructor.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_AssemblyInfo.htm)

### Constructors
- `protected GH_AssemblyInfo()` — Initializes a new instance of the GH_AssemblyInfo class

### Properties
- `Assembly` (Assembly, get) — Gets the actual assembly that this info object describes.
- `AuthorContact` (String, get) — Gets the contact information for the author. This can be email, phone, fax, address or all of the above. Return null if you do not wish to associate contact details with this assembly.
- `AuthorName` (String, get) — Gets the name of the individual/company/organisation that is responsible for this assembly. Return null if you do not wish to associate an author with this assembly.
- `Description` (String, get) — Gets the human readable description of this assembly. Return null if you do not wish to associate a description with this assembly.
- `Icon` (Bitmap, get) — Gets a 24x24 icon that represents this assembly. Return null if you do not wish to associate an icon with this assembly.
- `Id` (Guid, get) — Gets the Id of this assembly. By default the Id is a hash of the name.
- `IsCoreLibrary` (Boolean, get) — Gets whether this library is a Grasshopper core library. Core libraries are installed along with Grasshopper and thus should always be available anywhere.
- `License` (GH_LibraryLicense, get) — Gets the license type for this assembly.
- `LoadingMechanism` (GH_LoadingMechanism, get) — Gets the mechanism used to load this assembly.
- `Location` (String, get) — Gets the location this assembly was loaded from.
- `Name` (String, get) — Gets the human readable name of this assembly. Return null if you do not wish to associate a name with this assembly.
- `Version` (String, get) — Gets the version code for this assembly.

## GH_AssemblyInfoStub (class)

Provides basic properties for a GHA assembly that does not expose a type that derives from GH_AssemblyInfo. This type is created automatically when a GH_AssemblyInfo type is not found in an assembly.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_AssemblyInfoStub.htm)

### Constructors
- `public GH_AssemblyInfoStub(Assembly assembly)` — Initializes a new instance of the GH_AssemblyInfoStub class

### Properties
- `Assembly` (Assembly, get) — Gets the actual assembly that this info object describes.
- `AssemblyName` (String, get) — (Overrides GH_AssemblyInfo.AssemblyName.)
- `AssemblyVersion` (String, get) — (Overrides GH_AssemblyInfo.AssemblyVersion.)
- `AuthorContact` (String, get) — Gets the contact information for the author. This can be email, phone, fax, address or all of the above. Return null if you do not wish to associate contact details with this assembly.
- `AuthorName` (String, get) — Gets the name of the individual/company/organisation that is responsible for this assembly. Return null if you do not wish to associate an author with this assembly.
- `Description` (String, get) — Gets the human readable description of this assembly. Return null if you do not wish to associate a description with this assembly.
- `Icon` (Bitmap, get) — Gets a 24x24 icon that represents this assembly. Return null if you do not wish to associate an icon with this assembly.
- `Id` (Guid, get) — Gets the Id of this assembly. By default the Id is a hash of the name.
- `IsCoreLibrary` (Boolean, get) — Gets whether this library is a Grasshopper core library. Core libraries are installed along with Grasshopper and thus should always be available anywhere.
- `License` (GH_LibraryLicense, get) — Gets the license type for this assembly.
- `LoadingMechanism` (GH_LoadingMechanism, get) — Gets the mechanism used to load this assembly.
- `Location` (String, get) — Gets the location this assembly was loaded from.
- `Name` (String, get) — Gets the human readable name of this assembly. Return null if you do not wish to associate a name with this assembly.
- `Version` (String, get) — Gets the version code for this assembly.

## GH_AssemblyPriority (class)

Derive from this class if you wish to perform additional steps before any of your components are loaded. Any class in your project which inherits from GH_AssemblyPriority and which has an empty constructor will be instantiated prior to any GH_Component or IGH_DocumentObject classes.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_AssemblyPriority.htm)

### Constructors
- `protected GH_AssemblyPriority()` — Initializes a new instance of the GH_AssemblyPriority class

### Methods
#### `public abstract GH_LoadingInstruction PriorityLoad()`

This method will be called exactly once before any of the Components in your project are loaded.

**Returns:** `GH_LoadingInstruction` — Loading instruction. If you wish to abort the loading of your project, then return GH_LoadingInstruction.Abort.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_AssemblyPriority_PriorityLoad.htm)

Expires the entire layout of the attributes. When overridden, implementer must place a call to the base class ExpireLayout().

#### `public virtual bool InvalidateCanvas(GH_Canvas canvas, GH_CanvasMouseEvent e)`

If the mouse location should cause a canvas invalidation then return true. You only need to override this function if you draw objects that are dependant on cursor positions outside the bounds of the attributes.

- `canvas` (Grasshopper.GUI.Canvas.GH_Canvas) — Canvas to potentially invalidate.
- `e` (Grasshopper.GUI.GH_CanvasMouseEvent) — Event arguments for canvas mousemove.

#### `public virtual void SetupTooltip(PointF point, GH_TooltipDisplayEventArgs e)`

Populates the Grasshopper tooltip with all relevant data. If this function returns True, it is assumed that all possible fields have been filled out and the tooltip is ready for display.

**Parameters:**
- `point` (System.Drawing.PointF) — The pixel coordinate on the canvas of the cursor.
- `e` (Grasshopper.GUI.GH_TooltipDisplayEventArgs) — Tooltip event arguments.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Attributes_1_SetupTooltip.htm)

Utility function for testing window selections with freeform Graphics paths.

**Parameters:**
- `path` (System.Drawing.Drawing2D.GraphicsPath) — Path to test.
- `box` (System.Drawing.RectangleF) — Window selection box.
- `fuzz` (System.Single) — Fuzzy pixels around object.
- `mode` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_PickBox) — Selection mode to apply.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if box contains the path.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Attributes_1_SolvePathBoxPick.htm)

#### `public virtual bool Write(GH_IWriter writer)`



**Parameters:**
- `writer` (GH_IO.Serialization.GH_IWriter)

**Returns:** `Boolean` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Attributes_1_Write.htm)

### Properties
- `AllowMessageBalloon` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether these attributes allow warning and error balloons to be drawn on top of them.
- `Bounds` (RectangleF, get/set) — Gets the rectangle that contains the active content of the attributes. Typically the Contents determine the active area for menus, tooltips etc. Attributes are not supposed to draw objects beyond the Bounds.
- `DocObject` (IGH_DocumentObject, get) — Gets the owner object of these attributes.
- `GetTopLevel` (IGH_Attributes, get) — Gets the top-level attributes of the attribute stack these attributes belong to.
- `HasInputGrip` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether or not these attributes have an input grip.
- `HasOutputGrip` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether or not these attributes have an output grip.
- `InputGrip` (PointF, get) — Gets the input grip location for these attributes. If HasInputGrip equals False, this point is meaningless.
- `InstanceGuid` (Guid, get) — Gets the instance ID of the document object that owns these attributes.
- `IsTopLevel` (Boolean, get) — Gets whether these attributes are top_level (i.e. no Parent attributes)
- `OutputGrip` (PointF, get) — Gets the output grip location for these attributes. If HasOutputGrip equals False, this point is meaningless.
- `Owner` (T, get) — Gets the type-safe owner object of these attributes. This property is identical to the DocObject property.
- `Parent` (IGH_Attributes, get/set) — Gets or sets the parent attributes. Top level attributes do not have parents.
- `PathName` (String, get) — Get a description of the location of these attributes within the local attribute stack.
- `Pivot` (PointF, get/set) — Gets or sets the pivot for these attributes. The pivot controls the general placement of the attributes. If you want to move the attributes, change the pivot location.
- `Selected` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets the selected state of the top-level attributes.
- `TooltipEnabled` (Boolean, get) — Gets the tooltip enabled value. If False, no further tooltip functions will be called.

## GH_AutoSaveTrigger (enum)

Different types of events that can cause an AutoSave.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_AutoSaveTrigger.htm)

### Values
- `undefined` = `0` — Generic autosave trigger, this flag is not filtered.
- `doc_unload` = `1` — Autosave was triggered by a document being unloaded from a Canvas Context.
- `object_added` = `2` — Autosave was triggered by the addition of an object to the document.
- `object_removed` = `3` — Autosave was triggered by the removal of an object from the document.
- `wire_event` = `4` — Autosave was triggered by a change in connector wires.
- `data_matching_event` = `5` — Autosave was triggered by a change to Data Matching settings.
- `data_modification_event` = `6` — Autosave was triggered by a change to Data modifier settings.
- `object_generic` = `7` — Autosave was triggered by a non-trivial change to object data or settings.

## GH_BakeUtility (class)

A utility class for baking. We heavily recommend you use this for any baking, as it handles invalid objects correctly.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_BakeUtility.htm)

### Constructors
- `public GH_BakeUtility(GH_Document document)` — Create a new baker.

### Methods
#### `public bool BakeObject(Object obj, ObjectAttributes att, RhinoDoc doc)`

Try and bake a single object.

**Parameters:**
- `obj` (System.Object) — Object to bake, must at least implement IGH_BakeAwareData.
- `att` (ObjectAttributes) — Object attributes.
- `doc` (RhinoDoc)

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if object was baked.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_BakeUtility_BakeObject.htm)

#### `public int BakeObjects(IEnumerable objects, ObjectAttributes att, RhinoDoc doc)`

Bake a bunch of objects.

**Parameters:**
- `objects` (System.Collections.IEnumerable) — Objects to bake.
- `att` (ObjectAttributes) — Attributes.
- `doc` (RhinoDoc) — Document.

**Returns:** `Int32` — Number of successful bake operations.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_BakeUtility_BakeObjects.htm)

### Properties
- `BakedCount` (Int32, get) — Gets the number of successful baking operations.
- `BakedIds` (Guid[], get) — Gets all collected object ids.
- `InvalidCount` (Int32, get) — Gets the number of invalid objects encountered.

## GH_BitmapChannel (enum)

Lists all possible channels for 24 and 32 bit bitmaps.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_BitmapChannel.htm)

### Values
- `A` = `1` — Alpha channel, not all bitmaps support this.
- `R` = `2` — Red channel.
- `G` = `4` — Green channel.
- `B` = `8` — Blue channel.
- `RGB` = `14` — Red, Green and Blue channel mashup.
- `ARGB` = `15` — Alpha, Red, Green and Blue channel mashup.

## GH_ClipboardType (enum)

Enumerates all possible clipboards for GH_DocumentIO operations.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ClipboardType.htm)

### Values
- `System` = `0` — Identifies the Operating System clipboard shared amongst all applications.
- `Local` = `1` — Identifies the DocumentIO clipboard which is only accessible from within a single instance of GH_DocumentIO.
- `Global` = `2` — Identifies the DocumentIO clipboard which is accessible from all instances of GH_DocumentIO in this AppDomain.

## GH_Component (class)

Inherit from this class if you wish to create a custom component. Note that you must provide a public, empty constructor which calls the base class constructor.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Component.htm)

### Methods
#### `public virtual void AddRuntimeMessage(GH_RuntimeMessageLevel level, string text)`

Add a new message to this object. Valid message type flags are Warning and Error. If the Message string is empty or zero-length no message is added.

**Parameters:**
- `level` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_RuntimeMessageLevel) — Type of message. Only Warnings and Errors are recorded.
- `text` (System.String) — Content of message.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ActiveObject_AddRuntimeMessage.htm)

#### `public override void AddedToDocument(GH_Document document)`

Overrides the AddedToDocument method and delegates the call to all parameters.

**Parameters:**
- `document` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_Document) — Document that now owns this object.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Component_AddedToDocument.htm)

#### `protected virtual void AfterSolveInstance()`

Override this method if you want to be called after the last call to SolveInstance.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Component_AfterSolveInstance.htm)

#### `protected virtual void AppendAdditionalComponentMenuItems(ToolStripDropDown menu)`

Override this function if you want to insert some custom menu items in your derived Component class. Items will be added between List Matching items and parameter menus.

**Parameters:**
- `menu` (System.Windows.Forms.ToolStripDropDown)

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Component_AppendAdditionalComponentMenuItems.htm)

#### `public override void AppendAdditionalMenuItems(ToolStripDropDown menu)`

Adds typical component type items to the context menu: 1. Data comparison types 2. Custom (overridden) items 3. Nested input parameter context menu items 4. Nested output parameter context menu items

**Parameters:**
- `menu` (System.Windows.Forms.ToolStripDropDown)

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Component_AppendAdditionalMenuItems.htm)

#### `public override bool AppendMenuItems(ToolStripDropDown menu)`

This function is called when a context menu is about to be displayed. Override it to set custom items. GH_ActiveObject will already populate the menu with default items, if you merely wish to insert object-specific menu item, consider overriding AppendAdditionalMenuItems instead.

**Parameters:**
- `menu` (System.Windows.Forms.ToolStripDropDown) — Menu object to populate.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — If true, the menu will be displayed, if false the menu will be supressed.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ActiveObject_AppendMenuItems.htm)

#### `protected bool AssignInitCodeToInputParameter(int paramIndex, string code)`

Utility function for parsing and assigning init codes. This only works for standard parameter types.

**Parameters:**
- `paramIndex` (System.Int32) — Index of input parameter to assign.
- `code` (System.String) — Code to parse and assign.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the code was assigned, false if otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Component_AssignInitCodeToInputParameter.htm)

#### `public override sealed bool DependsOn(IGH_ActiveObject potentialSource)`

**Parameters:**
- `redraw` (System.Boolean)

#### `public override void ExpireSolution(bool recompute)`

Informs the document that owns this object that the solution has expired. The current object will be set to BLANK as a result. This method is recursive, it will also expire any and all objects which depend on this object. If you want a less destructive expiration, consider using ClearData(). If this object is already Blank, you should consider not expiring it.

**Parameters:**
- `recompute` (System.Boolean) — If True, the document will be instructed to solve the new state.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ActiveObject_ExpireSolution.htm)

#### `public void TriggerAutoSave(GH_AutoSaveTrigger trigger)`

Triggers the AutoSave function on the owner document with a custom flag.

**Parameters:**
- `trigger` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_AutoSaveTrigger) — Reason for the autosave operation. It is possible that a user has decided to avoid autosave events for specific types, so if you can, try and provide a correct trigger flag.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_DocumentObject_TriggerAutoSave_1.htm)

#### `public void TriggerAutoSave(GH_AutoSaveTrigger trigger, Guid id)`

Triggers the AutoSave function on the owner document with a custom flag.

**Parameters:**
- `trigger` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_AutoSaveTrigger) — Reason for the autosave operation. It is possible that a user has decided to avoid autosave events for specific types, so if you can, try and provide a correct trigger flag.
- `id` (System.Guid) — ID of autosave event. Consecutive autosave requests with the same ID will be ignored.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_DocumentObject_TriggerAutoSave_2.htm)

#### `public void TriggerAutoSave(Guid id)`

Triggers the AutoSave function on the owner document with the object_changed flag.

**Parameters:**
- `id` (System.Guid) — ID of autosave event. Consecutive autosave requests with the same ID will be ignored.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_DocumentObject_TriggerAutoSave_3.htm)

#### `protected virtual void ValuesChanged()`

Override this method if you want to respond to changes to the value table. The base implementation is empty, so you don't have to call it.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_DocumentObject_ValuesChanged.htm)

#### `public override bool Write(GH_IWriter writer)`

Write all required data for deserialization to an IO archive.

**Parameters:**
- `writer` (GH_IO.Serialization.GH_IWriter) — Object to write with.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Component_Write.htm)

#### `public virtual bool WriteFull(GH_IWriter writer)`

GH_InstanceDescription does not by default serialize all fields. Use this function to write all fields to the archive. This method is compatible with the default Write()/Read() operations.

**Parameters:**
- `writer` (GH_IO.Serialization.GH_IWriter) — Writer for serialization.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_InstanceDescription_WriteFull.htm)

### Properties
- `Attributes` (IGH_Attributes, get/set) — Gets or sets the attributes that are associated with this object. Only set custom attributes if you know what you are doing.
- `Category` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the Category in which this object belongs. If HasCategory() returns false, this field has no meaning.
- `ClippingBox` (BoundingBox, get) — Gets the clipping box for all preview geometry drawn by this component and all associated parameters.
- `ComponentGuid` (Guid, get) — Returns a consistent ID for this object type. Every object must supply a unique and unchanging ID that is used to identify objects of the same type.
- `Description` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the description of the object. This field typically remains fixed during the lifetime of an object.
- `Exposure` (GH_Exposure, get) — Gets the exposure of this object in the Graphical User Interface. The default is to expose everywhere.
- `HasCategory` (Boolean, get) — Gets whether or not the Category field has been set.
- `HasSubCategory` (Boolean, get) — Gets whether or not the SubCategory field has been set.
- `HelpDescription` (String, get) — Override this method if you want the help topic content to be more than the Component description.
- `Hidden` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets the hidden flag for this component. Does not affect Hidden flags on parameters associated with this component.
- `Icon` (Bitmap, get) — Override this function to supply a custom icon (24x24 pixels). The result of this property is cached, so don't worry if icon retrieval is not very fast.
- `Icon_24x24` (Bitmap, get) — The icon associated with this object.
- `Icon_24x24_Locked` (Bitmap, get) — The greyscale icon of this object.
- `IconCapableUI` (Boolean, get) — By default the NickName menu item supports the Icon Mode override toggle. If your UI is not capable of displaying icons, then override this property and return False.
- `IconDisplayMode` (GH_IconDisplayMode, get/set) — Gets the current display mode of the object.
- `InConstructor` (Boolean, get) — Gets the constructor flag for this instance. If InConstructor = True, it means the component is still registering parameters and the layout is not yet complete.
- `InstanceDescription` (String, get) — (Overrides GH_InstanceDescription.InstanceDescription.)
- `InstanceGuid` (Guid, get) — Gets the ID of this runtime instance.
- `IsBakeCapable` (Boolean, get) — 
- `IsDataProvider` (Boolean, get) — Components are never Data providers. Only the output parameters can be considered to be providers.
- `IsPreviewCapable` (Boolean, get) — If a single parameter is PreviewCapable, so is the component. Override this property if you need special Preview flags.
- `IsValidPrincipalParameterIndex` (Boolean, get) — Gets whether the MasterParameterIndex property identifies a valid parameter.
- `Keywords` (IEnumerable<String>, get) — Gets a list of additional keywords that describe the object. Typically this list is empty but you can override this property to aid in object searches.
- `Locked` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets the Locked state for this Component. This also sets locked states for all input and output parameters.
- `Message` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets a custom message to be displayed underneath the component. This message is not serialized and should be assigned on every solution anew.
- `MutableNickName` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value that enables Nick name changes through the menu. The default is TRUE.
- `Name` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the name of the object. This field typically remains fixed during the lifetime of an object.
- `NickName` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the nickname of the object. This field can be changed by the user.
- `Obsolete` (Boolean, get) — Gets whether this object is obsolete. Default implementation returns true if the class name contains the string "OBSOLETE" or if this class has been decorated with the ObsoleteAttribute. You are free to override this if you want, but I suggest adding the ObsoleteAttribute instead.
- `Params` (GH_ComponentParamServer, get) — Gets the parameter manager object for this component.
- `Phase` (GH_SolutionPhase, get/set) — Gets or sets the solution phase this object is currenly in.
- `PrincipalParameterIndex` (Int32, get/set) — Gets or sets the principal parameter index override. Negative indices or indices larger than or equal to the input parameter count will be ignored and regular principal parameter detection will kick in.
- `ProcessorTime` (TimeSpan, get) — Gets the total processor time (profiler) spend by this component and all input parameters.
- `RunCount` (Int32, get) — Gets a value indicating how many times the SolveInstance method was called on this component during the last solution. This property will return -1 if no valid runcount is available.
- `RuntimeMessageLevel` (GH_RuntimeMessageLevel, get) — Returns the worst case runtime warning level of me and all my parameters
- `SubCategory` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the SubCategory in which this object belongs. If HasSubCategory() returns false, this field has no meaning.
- `m_attributes` (IGH_Attributes, get) — (Inherited from GH_DocumentObject.)

### Events
#### `AttributesChanged` (`Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_DocumentObject.AttributesChangedEventHandler`)

**Signature:** `public event IGH_DocumentObject.AttributesChangedEventHandler AttributesChanged`

Raised whenever the number or kind of attributes changes. This event is handled by GH_Documents who subsequently wipe their attribute caches.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/E_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_DocumentObject_AttributesChanged.htm)

**Signature:** `public event IGH_DocumentObject.DisplayExpiredEventHandler DisplayExpired`

Raised whenever the display (on the Canvas) of a certain object becomes invalid.

Raised whenever an object needs to know which GH_Document it belongs to.

#### `PreviewExpired` (`Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_DocumentObject.PreviewExpiredEventHandler`)

**Signature:** `public event IGH_DocumentObject.PreviewExpiredEventHandler PreviewExpired`

Raised whenever the display (in the Rhino viewports) of a certain object becomes invalid.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/E_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_DocumentObject_PreviewExpired.htm)

#### `SolutionExpired` (`Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_DocumentObject.SolutionExpiredEventHandler`)

**Signature:** `public event IGH_DocumentObject.SolutionExpiredEventHandler SolutionExpired`

Raised whenever the solution of a certain object becomes invalid.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/E_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_DocumentObject_SolutionExpired.htm)

## GH_Component.GH_InputParamManager (class)

This class is used during Component construction to add input parameters.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Component_GH_InputParamManager.htm)

### Methods
#### `public int AddAngleParameter(string name, string nickname, string description, GH_ParamAccess access)`

Add a floating point Number parameter to the input list of this component. The parameter will be aware that it is representing angles.

**Parameters:**
- `name` (System.String) — The name of the parameter. Keep it short, single words are best.
- `nickname` (System.String) — The nickname of the parameter. Keep it short, single characters are best.
- `description` (System.String) — The description of the parameter. Be succinct but clear, single sentences are best.
- `access` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_ParamAccess) — Parameter access type. You must provide a correct access code in order to use GetData(), GetDataList() or GetDataTree() respectively.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Component_GH_InputParamManager_AddAngleParameter.htm)

- `access` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_ParamAccess) — Parameter access type. You must provide a correct access code in order to use GetData(), GetDataList() or GetDataTree() respectively.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Component_GH_InputParamManager_AddAngleParameter_2.htm)

- `name` (System.String) — The name of the parameter. Keep it short, single words are best.
- `nickname` (System.String) — The nickname of the parameter. Keep it short, single characters are best.
- `description` (System.String) — The description of the parameter. Be succinct but clear, single sentences are best.
- `access` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_ParamAccess) — Parameter access type. You must provide a correct access code in order to use GetData(), GetDataList() or GetDataTree() respectively.
- `default` (System.Globalization.CultureInfo) — A default value to store in the parameter Persistent Data.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Component_GH_InputParamManager_AddCultureParameter_1.htm)

- `access` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_ParamAccess) — Parameter access type. You must provide a correct access code in order to use GetData(), GetDataList() or GetDataTree() respectively.
- `default` (Rectangle3d) — A default value to store in the parameter Persistent Data.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Component_GH_InputParamManager_AddRectangleParameter_1.htm)

#### `public override void HideParameter(int index)`

Hide a specific input parameter. If the parameter at the given index implements IGH_PreviewObject then the Hidden flag will be set to True. Otherwise, nothing will happen.

**Parameters:**
- `index` (System.Int32) — Index of parameter to hide.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Component_GH_InputParamManager_HideParameter.htm)

### Properties
- `Param` (IGH_Param, get) — Gets the input parameter at the given index.
- `ParamCount` (Int32, get) — Returns the number of input parameters already part of this component.

## GH_Component.GH_OutputParamManager (class)

This class is used during Component construction to add output parameters.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Component_GH_OutputParamManager.htm)

Add a topological MeshFace parameter to the output list of this component.

**Parameters:**
- `name` (System.String) — The name of the parameter. Keep it short, single words are best.
- `nickname` (System.String) — The nickname of the parameter. Keep it short, single characters are best.
- `description` (System.String) — The description of the parameter. Be succinct but clear, single sentences are best.
- `access` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_ParamAccess) — Parameter access type. You must provide a correct access code in order to use SetData(), SetDataList() or SetDataTree() respectively.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Component_GH_OutputParamManager_AddMeshFaceParameter.htm)

#### `public override void HideParameter(int index)`

Hide a specific output parameter. If the parameter at the given index implement IGH_PreviewObject then the Hidden flag will be set to True. Otherwise, nothing will happen.

**Parameters:**
- `index` (System.Int32) — Index of parameter to hide.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Component_GH_OutputParamManager_HideParameter.htm)

- `description` (System.String) — The description of the parameter. Be succinct but clear.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Component_GH_OutputParamManager_Register_TransformParam.htm)

#### `public void Register_TransformParam(string name, string nickname, string description, GH_ParamAccess access)`

Register a new Transform parameter

**Parameters:**
- `name` (System.String) — The name of the parameter. Keep it short, single words are best.
- `nickname` (System.String) — The nickname of the parameter. Keep it short, single characters are best.
- `description` (System.String) — The description of the parameter. Be succinct but clear.
- `access` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_ParamAccess) — Parameter access, you should set this field if you intend to output lists here.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Component_GH_OutputParamManager_Register_TransformParam_1.htm)

#### `public GH_ComponentParamServer.IGH_SyncObject EmitSyncObject()`

Create a synch object that stores a shallow representation of this server. Cache this object if you're planning to make sweeping changes to this param-server. Then, once you are finished call the Sync() function to perform cleanup of removed parameters. This is a utility function and you can choose to perform cleanup yourself.

**Returns:** `GH_ComponentParamServer.IGH_SyncObject` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ComponentParamServer_EmitSyncObject.htm)

#### `public void Sync(GH_ComponentParamServer.IGH_SyncObject sync_data)`

Sync changes from a prerecorded state. All param references in the sync object which are no longer present in the current server will be properly deleted. The sync object will be reset in the process, so you can only call this function with a specific Sync object once.

**Parameters:**
- `sync_data` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_ComponentParamServer.IGH_SyncObject) — Sync object. Should be obtained by calling EmitSyncObject()

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ComponentParamServer_Sync.htm)

#### `public void Sync(GH_ComponentParamServer.IGH_SyncObject sync_data, out int cleanup_count)`

Sync changes from a prerecorded state. All param references in the sync object which are no longer present in the current server will be properly deleted. The sync object will be reset in the process, so you can only call this function with a specific Sync object once.

**Parameters:**
- `sync_data` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_ComponentParamServer.IGH_SyncObject) — Sync object. Should be obtained by calling EmitSyncObject()
- `cleanup_count` (System.Int32) — Number of parameters that were cleaned up.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ComponentParamServer_Sync_1.htm)

#### `public void Sync(Object sync_data)`

Sync changes from a prerecorded state. All param references in the sync object which are no longer present in the current server will be properly deleted. The sync object will be reset in the process, so you can only call this function with a specific Sync object once.

**Parameters:**
- `sync_data` (System.Object) — Sync object. Should be obtained by calling EmitSyncObject()

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ComponentParamServer_Sync_2.htm)

#### `public static void WriteParamHashData(BinaryWriter writer, IGH_Param param, GH_ParamHashFields fields)`

Write parameter data to a binary writer.

**Remarks:** This is useful for hashing purposes, there is no way to deserialize data written with this method.

**Parameters:**
- `writer` (System.IO.BinaryWriter) — Writer to write with.
- `param` (Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_Param) — Parameter to serialize.
- `fields` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_ParamHashFields) — Fields of parameter to serialize.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ComponentParamServer_WriteParamHashData.htm)

### Properties
- `Input` (List<IGH_Param>, get) — Gets or sets the input list of parameters. Do not insert or remove parameters from this list, use the dedicated functions instead.
- `InputWidth` (Single, get) — Gets the width of the largest input parameter.
- `OnlyTreeAndListParameters` (Boolean, get) — Returns True if every input parameter is either a list or a tree parameter.
- `OnlyTreeParameters` (Boolean, get) — Returns True if every input parameter is a tree parameter.
- `Output` (List<IGH_Param>, get) — Gets or sets the output list of parameters. Do not insert or remove parameters from this list, use the dedicated functions instead.
- `OutputWidth` (Single, get) — Gets the width of the largest output parameter.
- `Owner` (IGH_Component, get) — Gets the owner component of this instance

### Events
#### `ParameterChanged` (`Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_ComponentParamServer.ParameterChangedEventHandler`)

**Signature:** `public event GH_ComponentParamServer.ParameterChangedEventHandler ParameterChanged`

Raised whenever there is a change to any parameter. If you only care about NickName or Source changes, handle the ParameterNickNameChanged or ParameterSourcesChanged events instead.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/E_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ComponentParamServer_ParameterChanged.htm)

#### `ParameterNickNameChanged` (`Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_ComponentParamServer.ParameterNickNameChangedEventHandler`)

**Signature:** `public event GH_ComponentParamServer.ParameterNickNameChangedEventHandler ParameterNickNameChanged`

Raised whenever a parameter nickname change is accepted.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/E_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ComponentParamServer_ParameterNickNameChanged.htm)

#### `ParameterSourcesChanged` (`Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_ComponentParamServer.ParameterSourcesChangedEventHandler`)

#### `public virtual bool InvalidateCanvas(GH_Canvas canvas, GH_CanvasMouseEvent e)`

If the mouse location should cause a canvas invalidation then return true. You only need to override this function if you draw objects that are dependant on cursor positions outside the bounds of the attributes.

**Parameters:**
- `canvas` (Grasshopper.GUI.Canvas.GH_Canvas) — Canvas to potentially invalidate.
- `e` (Grasshopper.GUI.GH_CanvasMouseEvent) — Event arguments for canvas mousemove.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the canvas ought to be invalidated.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Attributes_1_InvalidateCanvas.htm)

#### `public virtual bool IsMenuRegion(PointF point)`

Determines whether a point is available for context menu popups. By default, IsMenuRegion calls IsPickRegion(PointF).

**Parameters:**
- `point` (System.Drawing.PointF) — Point location to test.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Attributes_1_IsMenuRegion.htm)

#### `public virtual bool IsPickRegion(PointF point)`

Determines whether a point is within the pickable region for this object. By default, any point inside the Bounds is treated as pickable.

**Parameters:**
- `point` (System.Drawing.PointF) — Point location to test.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Attributes_1_IsPickRegion.htm)

#### `public virtual bool IsPickRegion(RectangleF box, GH_PickBox method)`

Determines whether a rectangle is a valid pick region for this object. By default, the picking rectangle is intersected with the Bounds rectangle.

**Parameters:**
- `box` (System.Drawing.RectangleF) — Selection box.
- `method` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_PickBox) — Selection method.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Attributes_1_IsPickRegion_1.htm)

#### `public virtual bool IsTooltipRegion(PointF point)`

Determines whether a point is available for tooltip popups. By default, IsMenuRegion calls IsTooltipRegion(PointF).

- `point` (System.Drawing.PointF) — Point location to test.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Attributes_1_IsTooltipRegion.htm)

Populates the Grasshopper tooltip with all relevant data. If this function returns True, it is assumed that all possible fields have been filled out and the tooltip is ready for display.

- `e` (Grasshopper.GUI.GH_TooltipDisplayEventArgs) — Tooltip event arguments.

- `writer` (GH_IO.Serialization.GH_IWriter)

**Returns:** `Boolean` —

### Properties
- `AllowMessageBalloon` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether these attributes allow warning and error balloons to be drawn on top of them.
- `Bounds` (RectangleF, get/set) — Gets the rectangle that contains the active content of the attributes. Typically the Contents determine the active area for menus, tooltips etc. Attributes are not supposed to draw objects beyond the Bounds.
- `DocObject` (IGH_DocumentObject, get) — Gets the owner object of these attributes.
- `GetTopLevel` (IGH_Attributes, get) — Gets the top-level attributes of the attribute stack these attributes belong to.
- `HasInputGrip` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether or not these attributes have an input grip.
- `HasOutputGrip` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether or not these attributes have an output grip.
- `InputGrip` (PointF, get) — Gets the input grip location for these attributes. If HasInputGrip equals False, this point is meaningless.
- `InstanceGuid` (Guid, get) — Gets the instance ID of the document object that owns these attributes.
- `IsTopLevel` (Boolean, get) — Gets whether these attributes are top_level (i.e. no Parent attributes)
- `OutputGrip` (PointF, get) — Gets the output grip location for these attributes. If HasOutputGrip equals False, this point is meaningless.
- `Owner` (T, get) — Gets the type-safe owner object of these attributes. This property is identical to the DocObject property.
- `Parent` (IGH_Attributes, get/set) — Gets or sets the parent attributes. Top level attributes do not have parents.
- `PathName` (String, get) — Get a description of the location of these attributes within the local attribute stack.
- `Pivot` (PointF, get/set) — Gets or sets the pivot for these attributes. The pivot controls the general placement of the attributes. If you want to move the attributes, change the pivot location.
- `Selected` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets the selected state of the top-level attributes.
- `TooltipEnabled` (Boolean, get) — Gets the tooltip enabled value. If False, no further tooltip functions will be called.

## GH_ComponentServer (class)

Maintains proxies for all components, parameters and other object types as defined in all grasshopper assemblies.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ComponentServer.htm)

### Constructors
- `public GH_ComponentServer()` — Initializes a new instance of the GH_ComponentServer class

### Methods
#### `public void AddAlias(string alias, Guid target)`

Add an alias to a target.

**Parameters:**
- `alias` (System.String) — Alias to add. If the alias string contains spaces or commas, those will be used to harvest partial aliases.
- `target` (System.Guid) — Component ID of object that is targeted by the alias.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ComponentServer_AddAlias.htm)

#### `public void AddCategoryIcon(string fullCategoryName, Bitmap icon)`

Add an icon for a tab category. 16x16 images are required.

**Parameters:**
- `fullCategoryName` (System.String) — The full name of the category.
- `icon` (System.Drawing.Bitmap) — the icon to display on the tab (has to be a 16x16 image).

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ComponentServer_AddCategoryIcon.htm)

#### `public void AddCategoryShortName(string fullCategoryName, string categoryAbbreviation)`

Add an abbreviation for a tab category. 2~4 letter abbreviations are best.

**Parameters:**
- `fullCategoryName` (System.String) — The full name of the category.
- `categoryAbbreviation` (System.String) — The abbreviation to display on the tab.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ComponentServer_AddCategoryShortName.htm)

#### `public void AddCategorySymbolName(string fullCategoryName, char symbol)`

Add a single char category description.

**Parameters:**
- `fullCategoryName` (System.String) — The full name of the category.
- `symbol` (System.Char) — The single char representing the category.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ComponentServer_AddCategorySymbolName.htm)

#### `public List<Guid> AliasTargets(string alias)`

Get all the target Component IDs for a given alias.

**Parameters:**
- `alias` (System.String) — Alias to search for.

**Returns:** `List<Guid>` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ComponentServer_AliasTargets.htm)

#### `public void Clear()`

Clear all caches.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ComponentServer_Clear.htm)

#### `public void ClearStaleUserObjects()`

Remove all user objects that no longer point to existing files.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ComponentServer_ClearStaleUserObjects.htm)

#### `public static int CompareProxies(IGH_ObjectProxy A, IGH_ObjectProxy B)`

Compare two object proxies. Names and exposures are taken into account.

**Parameters:**
- `A` (Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_ObjectProxy) — First proxy to compare.
- `B` (Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_ObjectProxy) — Second proxy to compare.

**Returns:** `Int32` — -1 if A is valued lower than B, 0 if A and B are considered equal, +1 if A is valued higher than B.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ComponentServer_CompareProxies.htm)

#### `public static string CopyFileToAppropriateFolder(string filepath, bool moveOriginalToBin)`

Copy a GHA or GHUSER file into the appropriate Roaming Application Data folder. Of the file is already in the top-level roaming folder it will not be copied.

**Parameters:**
- `filepath` (System.String) — The file to copy.
- `moveOriginalToBin` (System.Boolean) — If true, the original file will be placed in the recycle bin upon a successful copy.

**Returns:** `String` — The path of the copied file.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ComponentServer_CopyFileToAppropriateFolder.htm)

#### `public static List<GH_ExternalFile> ExternalFiles(bool testGrasshopperCollisions, bool testExternalCollisions)`

Get lists of all the external files (GHA, GHUSER and GHCLUSTER) that will be loaded by Grasshopper. Note: this does not equal the files that may already have been loaded, just the files that we'll attempt to load if we were asked to load all plugins now. The list contains no duplicate file paths, but it may contain identical files which won't be filtered until actual loading.

**Parameters:**
- `testGrasshopperCollisions` (System.Boolean) — If true, invalid duplicates of Grasshopper.dll will be tested for and warned about.
- `testExternalCollisions` (System.Boolean) — If true, duplicates of GHA, GHUSER and GHCLUSTER files will be tested for and warned about.

**Returns:** `List<GH_ExternalFile>` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ComponentServer_ExternalFiles.htm)

#### `public IGH_ObjectProxy FindObjectByName(string name, bool ignoreWhiteSpace, bool ignoreCapitalisation)`

Find an object by name. Obsolete and hidden objects are ignored. Only exact matches are included.

**Parameters:**
- `name` (System.String) — Name to search for. Name may be prefixed by Category and Subcategory.
- `ignoreWhiteSpace` (System.Boolean) — If true, whitespace is ignored during matching.
- `ignoreCapitalisation` (System.Boolean) — If true, capitalisation is ignored during matching.

**Returns:** `IGH_ObjectProxy` — The proxy that matches the name, or null if no such object could be found.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ComponentServer_FindObjectByName.htm)

#### `public List<IGH_ObjectProxy> FindObjects(Guid libraryID)`

Find all the objects that are part of a certain library.

**Parameters:**
- `libraryID` (System.Guid) — ID of library to search for.

**Returns:** `List<IGH_ObjectProxy>` — A list of all the objects that are defined in the specified library.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ComponentServer_FindObjects.htm)

#### `public int FindObjects(string[] terms, int maximumResults, ref IGH_ObjectProxy[] results, ref double[] resultWeights)`

Searches the list of cached object proxies for objects that might be implied by a search term.

**Parameters:**
- `terms` (System.String[]) — Search terms to use.
- `maximumResults` (System.Int32) — Maximum number of allowed hits.
- `results` (Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_ObjectProxy[])
- `resultWeights` (System.Double[])

**Returns:** `Int32` — A sorted list of search results.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ComponentServer_FindObjects_1.htm)

#### `public Bitmap GetCategoryIcon(string fullName)`

Sets the aliases for a given target.

**Parameters:**

### Properties
- `Aliases` (List<String>, get) — Gets a list of all the defined aliases.
- `CompleteRibbonLayout` (GH_Layout, get) — Gets the default component layout featuring all non-hidden components and parameters.
- `DiscardedProxies` (Int32, get) — Returns the total number of IGH_DocumentObjects that were discarded during assembly parsing. This includes both skipped and replaced proxies.
- `GraphProxies` (IList<GH_GraphProxy>, get) — Returns a list of all cached GH_GraphProxy instances.
- `Libraries` (ReadOnlyCollection<GH_AssemblyInfo>, get) — Returns a list of Assembly Info objects describing all loaded assemblies.
- `LoadingExceptions` (List<GH_LoadingException>, get) — Gets a list of all the exceptions that were recorded during the most recent loading sequence.
- `NoLoadExtension` (String, get) — 
- `ObjectProxies` (IList<IGH_ObjectProxy>, get) — Returns a list of all cached GH_ObjectProxy instances.
- `ObjectProxyNames` (AutoCompleteStringCollection, get) — Returns a specialized string collection of all component names.
- `TypeHints` (IList<Type>, get) — Gets a list of all IGH_TypeHint types in all loaded assemblies.
- `Upgraders` (IList<IGH_UpgradeObject>, get) — Gets a list of all the upgraders that have been cached.

### Events

## GH_ConnectivityDiagram (class)

Represents a complete connectivity diagram (topology) of a GH_Document. A connectivity diagram is basically an undirected network of all the wires in a document.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ConnectivityDiagram.htm)

### Constructors
- `public GH_ConnectivityDiagram(GH_ConnectivityDiagram other)` — Create a duplicate of another Connectivity Diagram.
- `public GH_ConnectivityDiagram(GH_Document doc)` — Create a new Connectivity Diagram from a GH_Document.

### Methods
#### `public bool ContainsID(Guid id)`

Test the diagram for a given Node ID.

**Parameters:**
- `id` (System.Guid) — ID to search for.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the ID is part of this diagram, false if otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ConnectivityDiagram_ContainsID.htm)

#### `public List<Guid> GetAllInputs(Guid id)`

Get all the Nodes that are part of the specified node input.

**Parameters:**
- `id` (System.Guid) — ID of node to query.

**Returns:** `List<Guid>` — A list of input nodes.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ConnectivityDiagram_GetAllInputs.htm)

#### `public List<Guid> GetAllOutputs(Guid id)`

Get all the Nodes that are part of the specified node output.

**Parameters:**
- `id` (System.Guid) — ID of node to query.

**Returns:** `List<Guid>` — A list of output nodes.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ConnectivityDiagram_GetAllOutputs.htm)

### Properties
- `Node` (GH_ConnectivityNode, get) — Get the connectivity node with the specified ID.
- `NodeCount` (Int32, get) — Gets the number of connectivity nodes in this diagram.
- `Nodes` (ReadOnlyCollection<Guid>, get) — Gets a proxy for all the Nodes in this diagram.

## GH_ConnectivityDisparity (class)

Maintains two connectivity diagrams, one of which is a subset of the other.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ConnectivityDisparity.htm)

### Constructors
- `public GH_ConnectivityDisparity(GH_ConnectivityDiagram topset, GH_ConnectivityDiagram subset)` — Create a new instance of the Connectivity Disparity utility class.

### Methods
#### `public List<Guid> ExternalInputs(Guid id)`

Find all external sources for a given id in the subset.

**Parameters:**
- `id` (System.Guid) — ID of parameter.

**Returns:** `List<Guid>` — A list containing all sources that are not present in the subset topology.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ConnectivityDisparity_ExternalInputs.htm)

#### `public List<Guid> ExternalOutputs(Guid id)`

Find all external recipients for a given id in the subset.

**Parameters:**
- `id` (System.Guid) — ID of parameter.

**Returns:** `List<Guid>` — A list containing all recipients that are not present in the subset topology.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ConnectivityDisparity_ExternalOutputs.htm)

#### `public List<Guid> InternalInputs(Guid id)`

Find all internal sources for a given id in the subset.

**Parameters:**
- `id` (System.Guid) — ID of parameter.

**Returns:** `List<Guid>` — A list containing all sources that are present in the subset topology.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ConnectivityDisparity_InternalInputs.htm)

#### `public List<Guid> InternalOutputs(Guid id)`

Find all external recipients for a given id in the subset.

**Parameters:**
- `id` (System.Guid) — ID of parameter.

**Returns:** `List<Guid>` — A list containing all recipients that are present in the subset topology.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ConnectivityDisparity_InternalOutputs.htm)

### Properties
- `SubNodes` (ReadOnlyCollection<Guid>, get) — Gets a proxy for all the Nodes in the Subset diagram.
- `SubSet` (GH_ConnectivityDiagram, get) — Gets a pointer to the subset diagram stored in this disparity class.
- `TopNodes` (ReadOnlyCollection<Guid>, get) — Gets a proxy for all the Nodes in the Top Level diagram.
- `TopSet` (GH_ConnectivityDiagram, get) — Gets a pointer to the complete diagram stored in this disparity class.

## GH_ConnectivityNode (class)

Represents a single node in a Grasshopper connectivity diagram. A single node represents a single IGH_Param instance.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ConnectivityNode.htm)

### Constructors
- `public GH_ConnectivityNode(GH_ConnectivityNode other)` — Initializes a new instance of the GH_ConnectivityNode class

### Properties
- `NodeID` (Guid, get) — Gets the InstanceID of the Parameter that this node represents.
- `NodeIn` (ReadOnlyCollection<Guid>, get) — Gets the list of IDs that are connected to the input of this node.
- `NodeOut` (ReadOnlyCollection<Guid>, get) — Gets the list of IDs that are connected to the input of this node.

## GH_Conversion (enum)

Level flag for conversion algorithms.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Conversion.htm)

### Values
- `Primary` = `0` — Primary conversions only entail direct casts from identical or similar data types.
- `Secondary` = `1` — Secondary conversions entail all implied conversions with the exception of direct casts.
- `Both` = `2` — Indicates both Primary and Secondary

## GH_Convert (class)

Provides static converters for different data types inside Grasshopper. This class both has specific and generic converters.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Convert.htm)

### Methods
#### `public static bool BackSolveExpression(string exp, string variable, double target, double minimum, double maximum, int steps, out double t, out double v)`

Try and backsolve an expression

**Parameters:**
- `exp` (System.String) — Expression to back solve.
- `variable` (System.String) — Name of input variable.
- `target` (System.Double) — Result to look for.
- `minimum` (System.Double) — Lowest input to try.
- `maximum` (System.Double) — Highest input to try.
- `steps` (System.Int32) — Number of steps per iteration.
- `t` (System.Double) — Value for variable that closest approximates the target value.
- `v` (System.Double) — Value of expression for the best approximation.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success, false on failure..

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Convert_BackSolveExpression.htm)

**Returns:** `T` — A RhinoCommon object instance or null on failure.

#### `public static DateTime CreatePureDate(DateTime date)`

Convert a datetime structure into a pure date, i.e. remove the time portion. As per convention, this leaves 1 tick into the day in question intact.

**Parameters:**
- `date` (System.DateTime)

**Returns:** `DateTime` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Convert_CreatePureDate.htm)

#### `public static DateTime CreatePureTime(DateTime date)`

Convert a datetime structure into a pure time, i.e. remove the date portion. As per convention, this means setting the year, month and day to 1.

**Parameters:**
- `date` (System.DateTime)

**Returns:** `DateTime` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Convert_CreatePureTime.htm)

#### `public static DateTime CreatePureTime(int hours, int minutes, int seconds)`

Create a pure time instance. If the total number of hours, minutes and seconds exceeds a full year then it is not possible to create a pure time and the largest possible pure time will be returned instead.

**Parameters:**
- `hours` (System.Int32)
- `minutes` (System.Int32)
- `seconds` (System.Int32)

**Returns:** `DateTime` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Convert_CreatePureTime_1.htm)

#### `public static GH_TimeKind GetTimeKind(DateTime time)`

Gets the kind of time implied by a System.DateTime value.

**Parameters:**
- `time` (System.DateTime) — Time to evaluate.

**Returns:** `GH_TimeKind` — GH_TimeKind.PureTime, GH_TimeKind.PureDate or GH_TimeKind.DateAndTime.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Convert_GetTimeKind.htm)

#### `public static GH_TimeKind GetTimeKind(DateTime A, DateTime B)`

Gets the kind of time implied by two System.DateTime values.

**Parameters:**
- `A` (System.DateTime) — First time to evaluate.
- `B` (System.DateTime) — Second time to evaluate.

**Returns:** `GH_TimeKind` — GH_TimeKind.PureTime, GH_TimeKind.PureDate or GH_TimeKind.DateAndTime if both kinds are the same. GH_TimeKind.DateAndTime is one is a pure date and the other a DateAndTime value. GH_TimeKind.Mixed is one is a pure time and the other one is not.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Convert_GetTimeKind_1.htm)

#### `public static bool IsPureDate(DateTime potentialDate)`

Tests whether a DateTime instance represents a pure date (i.e. that the time portion is undefined). Pure dates are a Grasshopper convention, basically a pure date is 1 tick past midnight.

**Parameters:**
- `potentialDate` (System.DateTime) — Date to test.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the date is considered pure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Convert_IsPureDate.htm)

#### `public static bool IsPureTime(DateTime potentialTime)`

Tests whether a DateTime instance represents a pure time (i.e. that the date portion is undefined). Pure times are a Grasshopper convention, basically a pure time is 2 ticks past any second.

**Parameters:**
- `potentialTime` (System.DateTime) — Date to test.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the time is considered undefined.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Convert_IsPureTime.htm)

#### `public static IGH_GeometricGoo ObjRefToGeometry(ObjRef in)`

Convert a Rhino Object Reference into Grasshopper IGH_GeometricGoo.

**Parameters:**
- `in` (ObjRef) — Object reference to convert.

**Returns:** `IGH_GeometricGoo` — An instance that implements IGH_GeometricGoo on success or false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Convert_ObjRefToGeometry.htm)

#### `public static GH_Variant ParseExpression(string exp, bool recursive)`

Attempts to parse an expression.

**Parameters:**
- `exp` (System.String) — Expression to parse.
- `recursive` (System.Boolean) — If True, and the result of the expression is a String, the result is parsed again until the result is no longer a String or until the expression fails.

**Returns:** `GH_Variant` — The result of the (recursive) expression.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Convert_ParseExpression.htm)

#### `public static bool StringToDate(string text, ref DateTime date)`

Convert a string representing a date into an actual date. If a pure date is possible it will be returned. If the input is a pure time this method will fail, you need to use StringToTime instead.

**Parameters:**
- `text` (System.String) — String to parse.
- `date` (System.DateTime) — If the string could be parsed, the pure date will be returned here.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if string was successfully parsed, false if not.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Convert_StringToDate.htm)

#### `public static Guid StringToGuid(string text)`

Create a Guid based on SHA256 hashing of a string.

**Parameters:**
- `text` (System.String) — Text to hash.

**Returns:** `Guid` — A guid representing the string.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Convert_StringToGuid.htm)

#### `public static bool StringToTime(string text, ref DateTime time)`

Convert a string representing a time into an pure time. Allowed separators of hour, minute and second portions are colons, semi-colons, points and commas. Grasshopper itself uses colons when formatting times. A PM or AM symbol is allowed provided it does not border on any alphabetical characters. A time can be prefixed by a "X day[s] + " string which defines the total number of 24-hour periods to include.

**Parameters:**
- `text` (System.String) — String to parse.
- `time` (System.DateTime) — If the string could be parsed, the pure time will be returned here.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if string was successfully parsed, false if not.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Convert_StringToTime.htm)

**Returns:** `Boolean` —

#### `public static bool ToGHAngularDimension_Primary(Object data, ref GH_AngularDimension target)`

Converts similar data into a GH_AngularDimension instance. Data is not guaranteed to be duplicated.

**Parameters:**
