### Properties
- `CultureTable` (SortedDictionary<CultureInfo,List<String>>, get) — Gets the cached culture table. Do not modify this dictionary unless you know what you are doing.
- `PureDateTicks` (Int64, get) — Represents the total number of ticks past midnight that (by Grasshopper convention) identifies a pure date.
- `PureTimeTicks` (Int64, get) — Represents the total number of ticks past a whole second that (by Grasshopper convention) identifies a pure time.

## GH_CustomEventServer (class)

RhinoCommon only event server.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_CustomEventServer.htm)

### Constructors
- `public GH_CustomEventServer(IGH_EventConsumer consumer)` — Create and register a new event server.

### Properties
- `Enabled` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets the enabled state flag for this event watcher.
- `GuidTable` (SortedDictionary<Guid,UInt32>, get) — Gets direct access to the internal guid table. The table contains pairs of object Ids and runtime serial numbers.
- `GuidTableCount` (Int32, get) — Gets the number of stored ids in the Guid Table.

## GH_DataComparison (enum)

Enumerates all possible Data Matching algorithms for Components.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_DataComparison.htm)

### Values
- `ShortestList` = `0` — Matches data in all lists until the shortest list runs dry. This flag will omit data entries if lists have a different length
- `LongestList` = `1` — Matches data in all lists until the longest list runs dry. This is the default behaviour
- `CrossReference` = `2` — Matches data in all lists in all possible combinations. Careful, since this might results in stupendous amounts of data

## GH_DataMapping (enum)

Enumerates all parameter data mapping types.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_DataMapping.htm)

### Values
- `None` = `0` — Data is not mapped.
- `Flatten` = `1` — Data is flattened inside this parameter.
- `Graft` = `2` — Data is grafted inside this parameter.

## GH_DocContextEventArgs (class)

These arguments are passed along with SettingsChanged events on GH_Document.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_DocContextEventArgs.htm)

### Properties
- `Context` (GH_DocumentContext, get) — Gets the value of the document context flag at the time the event was raised, it may have been changed in between by other event handlers.
- `Document` (GH_Document, get) — Gets the document that raised the event.

## GH_DocEnabledEventArgs (class)

These arguments are passed along with SettingsChanged events on GH_Document.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_DocEnabledEventArgs.htm)

### Properties
- `Document` (GH_Document, get) — Gets the document that raised the event.
- `Enabled` (Boolean, get) — Gets the value of the document Enabled flag at the time the event was raised, it may have been changed in between by other event handlers.

## GH_DocFilePathEventArgs (class)

These arguments are passed along with FilePathChanged events on GH_Document.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_DocFilePathEventArgs.htm)

### Constructors
- `public GH_DocFilePathEventArgs(GH_Document document, string oldPath, string newPath)` — Create a new instance of the GH_DocFilePathEventArgs class.

### Properties
- `Document` (GH_Document, get) — Gets the document that raised this event.
- `NewFilePath` (String, get) — Gets the new filepath, the one currently assigned to this document.
- `OldFilePath` (String, get) — Gets the old filepath, prior to the change that triggered this event.

## GH_DocModifiedEventArgs (class)

These arguments are passed along with ModifiedChanged events on GH_Document.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_DocModifiedEventArgs.htm)

### Properties
- `Document` (GH_Document, get) — Gets the document that raised the event.
- `Modified` (Boolean, get) — Gets the value of the document Modified flag at the time the event was raised, it may have been changed in between by other event handlers.

## GH_DocObjectEventArgs (class)

These arguments are passed along with ObjectsAdded and ObjectsRemoved events on GH_Document.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_DocObjectEventArgs.htm)

### Properties
- `Attributes` (List<IGH_Attributes>, get) — Gets all the attributes that are associated with all objects in the event.
- `Document` (GH_Document, get) — Gets the document that raised the event.
- `Object` (IGH_DocumentObject, get) — Gets the object to which this event pertains.
- `ObjectCount` (Int32, get) — Gets the number of objects in this event.
- `Objects` (ReadOnlyCollection<IGH_DocumentObject>, get) — Gets a readonly list of the objects to which this event pertains.

## GH_DocSettingsEventArgs (class)

These arguments are passed along with SettingsChanged events on GH_Document.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_DocSettingsEventArgs.htm)

### Properties
- `Document` (GH_Document, get) — Gets the document that raised the event.
- `Kind` (GH_DocumentSettings, get) — Gets the type of setting that was changed.

## GH_DocUndoEventArgs (class)

These arguments are passed along with UndoStateChanged events on GH_Document.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_DocUndoEventArgs.htm)

### Properties
- `Document` (GH_Document, get) — Gets the document that raised the event.
- `Operation` (GH_UndoOperation, get) — Gets the type of undo operation that caused this event.
- `Record` (GH_UndoRecord, get) — Gets the UndoRecord pertaining to this event. Not every Event type has a single record associated with it so be sure to test for null.

#### `public bool AutoSave(GH_AutoSaveTrigger trigger)`

Creates a new autosave file on the disk. The previous autosave file (assuming it exists) will be temporarily renamed and eventually deleted entirely. Note: AutoSave is only possible on Documents that have TopLevel Rank and a valid FilePath.

**Parameters:**
- `trigger` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_AutoSaveTrigger) — Reason for autosave. User can choose to ignore certain autosave reasons.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Document_AutoSave.htm)

#### `public bool AutoSave(GH_AutoSaveTrigger trigger, Guid id)`

Creates a new autosave file on the disk. The previous autosave file (assuming it exists) will be temporarily renamed and eventually deleted entirely. Note: AutoSave is only possible on Documents that have TopLevel Rank and a valid FilePath.

**Parameters:**
- `trigger` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_AutoSaveTrigger) — Reason for autosave. User can choose to ignore certain autosave reasons.
- `id` (System.Guid) — Guid of autosave operation, consecutive calls to autosave with the same Guid will be ignored.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Document_AutoSave_1.htm)

Returns the boundingbox of all the normal attributes. If the document contains no normal attributes an Empty RectangleF structure is returned.

**Parameters:**
- `limitToSelection` (System.Boolean) — If true, only selected attributes are included in the boundingbox computation.

**Returns:** `RectangleF` — The boundingbox of the requested objects or RectangleF.Empty if no objects could be found.

Add an input hook for every non-optional input parameter with no persistent data and no sources. Then add an output hook for every floating parameter without recipients, and for every first component output parameter where the parent component has no recipients.

#### `public void DefineConstant(string name, GH_Variant value)`

Add a new constant to the document. Constants are not case-sensitive, when you define a constant with an existing name, the old one will be replaced. Note: constants are as of yet unused. Eventually they are supposed to become accessible to all expressions running inside a document.

**Parameters:**
- `name` (System.String) — Name of constant.
- `value` (Grasshopper.Kernel.Expressions.GH_Variant) — Value of constant.

Destroys the Attribute cache. Call this function whenever you do something which might affect attributes.

#### `public void DestroyAutoSaveFiles()`

Destroys all autosave files and backup files that are associated with this document. Only call this function when you are certain that the document has been properly saved and no changes have occured since.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Document_DestroyAutoSaveFiles.htm)

#### `public void DestroyObjectTable()`

Erase the Object fast lookup table. You must call this whenever you add or remove objects to or from the document while circumventing the regular channels.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Document_DestroyObjectTable.htm)

Attempts to destroy all the preview meshes in this document. It might not work for all objects, but this is still your best bet.

#### `public static bool IsEscapeKeyDown()`

Gets whether the escape key was pressed since the last time this function was called. Though in reality the actual escape state is checked no more than once every 50 milliseconds or so, which should be sufficient for a smooth user experience.

**Remarks:** If GetAsyncKeyState is called by some other application, it will mess with the value returned here, as GetAsyncKeyState is a system wide static method.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if escape was/is pressed, false if otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Document_IsEscapeKeyDown.htm)

**Parameters:**
- `other` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_Document) — The other document. Cannot be Null or
- `resolveProxies` (System.Boolean) — If True, an attempt is made to repair proxy sources after insertion.

**Parameters:**
- `other` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_Document) — The other document. Cannot be null.
- `resolveProxies` (System.Boolean) — If True, an attempt is made to repair proxy sources after insertion.

#### `public GH_RelevantObjectData RelevantObjectAtPoint(PointF pt)`

**Parameters:**
- `pt` (System.Drawing.PointF) — The point to test.

**Returns:** `GH_RelevantObjectData` — Information about the most relevant object or Null if no relevant object could be found at the given point.

- `searchFilter` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_RelevantObjectFilter)

**Returns:** `GH_RelevantObjectData` — Information about the most relevant object or Null if no relevant object could be found at the given point.

Set the requestabort flag. The solution will be aborted whenever the current active object is completed. It is also possible that active objects themselves implement an abortion mechanism, in which case abortion might occur even sooner.

#### `public Command RunningAsCommand()`

When the document is being solved inside the context of an executing Rhino command, this function will return the command instance. Under normal operation of Grasshopper in the traditional sense, this function will return null. The document executes as a command when inside of the grasshopper player or as a compiled grasshopper player based command. The way to run a GH_Document as a command is to call the GH_RhinoScriptInterface.RunAsCommand function

**Returns:** `Command` — executing command when running as a command; otherwise null

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Document_RunningAsCommand.htm)

#### `public void ScheduleSolution(int delay)`

Schedule a new solution after a specified time interval. The schedule is erased if another solution is triggered before the schedule is executed. Tight schedules override loose schedules. If this method is called during a solution, the schedule will be started after the current solution completes.

**Parameters:**
- `delay` (System.Int32) — The delay (in milliseconds) between the completion of this solution and the start of the scheduled solution. If the delay is 1ms or more, a timer will be used to trigger a new solution sometime in the future. If a delay of 0ms is used, then the next solution will run recursively within the current solution. You should not use a delay of zero milliseconds too often as it will cause the Rhino process to run out of stack-space. The SolutionDepth property can be used to see how deeply recursive the solution currently is.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Document_ScheduleSolution.htm)

#### `public void ScheduleSolution(int delay, GH_Document.GH_ScheduleDelegate callback)`

Schedule a new solution after a specified time interval. The schedule is erased if another solution is triggered before the schedule is executed. Tight schedules override loose schedules. If this method is called during a solution, the schedule will be started after the current solution completes.

**Parameters:**
- `delay` (System.Int32) — The delay (in milliseconds) between the completion of this solution and the start of the scheduled solution. If the delay is 1ms or more, a timer will be used to trigger a new solution sometime in the future. If a delay of 0ms is used, then the next solution will run recursively within the current solution. You should not use a delay of zero milliseconds too often as it will cause the Rhino process to run out of stack-space. The SolutionDepth property can be used to see how deeply recursive the solution currently is.
- `callback` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_Document.GH_ScheduleDelegate) — If not null, the callback will be called before the scheduled solution is triggered.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Document_ScheduleSolution_1.htm)

### Properties
- `AbortRequested` (Boolean, get) — Gets the abortrequested flag state.
- `ActiveDoc` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value indicating that this document was last active in a multi-doc aware Rhino.
- `AttributeCount` (Int32, get) — Returns the total number of attributes in the document. Both Normal and Hidden ones
- `Attributes` (IList<IGH_Attributes>, get) — Returns a list of all the attributes in this document. This includes attributes for nested objects such as input or output parameters.
- `Author` (IGH_Author, get) — Gets the author details associated with this document.
- `ConstantServer` (SortedDictionary<String,GH_Variant>, get) — Gets the dictionary of constants defined in this document.
- `ContainsCustomPreviewObjects` (Boolean, get) — Gets whether this document contains *any* custom preview objects.
- `Context` (GH_DocumentContext, get/set) — Gets or sets the context for this document. Note that the context is not a state so much as an action. Therefore it is not particularly useful to read the context after the fact, rather, context is mostly intended to be a setter, not a getter. When you set the context, all top-level document objects will be informed and the ContextChanged event will be raised.
- `DefaultPreviewColour` (Color, get/set) — Gets or sets the default preview colour for normal objects.
- `DefaultSelectedPreviewColour` (Color, get/set) — Gets or sets the default preview colour for normal objects.
- `DisplayName` (String, get) — Gets the display name for this document. The display name is highly context sensitive and is only supposed to give the user a good idea of which document this is and whether it has been modified.
- `DocumentID` (Guid, get) — Gets the document Id. The Id is assigned at construction and never changed. Note: clusters may choose to modify the Id of their internal documents.
- `Enabled` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets the enabled state for this document. Enabled documents respond to events and display their previews in the Rhino viewport.
- `EnableSolutions` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a global variable that indicates whether solutions are allowed to be recomputed. When this flag is off, the solver will be disabled and the event watchers should be silent.
- `EventServer` (GH_DocumentEventServer, get) — Gets the event server that belongs to this document. The Event server may be in an inactive state, but it is never null.
- `FilePath` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the file path of this document. If the file path is not set Nothing is returned.
- `IgnoredCount` (Int32, get) — Returns the total numbers of components currently in the Object Exception Ignore List.
- `IsFilePathDefined` (Boolean, get) — Returns True if the filename flag of this document has been set
- `IsModified` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets the modified flag. If the new value differs from the old value, the ModifiedChanged() event is raised.
- `ModifiedSubsidiaries` (Int32, get) — Gets the number of modified subsidiary documents.
- `Nested` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets whether this document is nested. A nested document is always part of some other document and is therefore unknown to the DocumentServer and the Canvas.
- `ObjectCount` (Int32, get) — Returns the total number of top-level objects in the document.
- `Objects` (IList<IGH_DocumentObject>, get) — Returns a list of all normal, top-level objects.
- `ObjectSpan` (TimeSpan, get) — Gets the total timespan for all objects.
- `Owner` (IGH_DocumentOwner, get/set) — Gets or sets the owner of this document. A document is typically owned when it is subsidiary to some object, such as a cluster.
- `PreviewBoundary` (GH_PreviewBoundary, get/set) — Gets or sets the preview boundary applied to this document. If a boundary has been set, only those objects which are inside the boundary will be previewed.
- `PreviewBoundingBox` (BoundingBox, get) — Gets the boundingbox used for previews.
- `PreviewColour` (Color, get/set) — Gets or sets the colour of unselected preview geometry.
- `PreviewColourSelected` (Color, get/set) — Gets or sets the colour of selected preview geometry.
- `PreviewCustomMeshParameters` (MeshingParameters, get/set) — Gets or sets the Custom Meshing Parameters for this document, these are only used when PreviewMeshType = GH_PreviewMesh.Custom
- `PreviewFilter` (GH_PreviewFilter, get/set) — Gets or set the preview filter.
- `PreviewMeshType` (GH_PreviewMesh, get/set) — Gets or sets the preview mesh type for this document.
- `PreviewMode` (GH_PreviewMode, get/set) — Gets or sets the preview display mode and makes sure the conduit instance of this class is set to match.
- `Profiler` (GH_ProfilerMode, get/set) — Gets or sets the profiler mode used in this document.
- `Properties` (GH_DocumentProperties, get) — The properties of this document
- `RaiseEvents` (Boolean, get/set) — If RaiseEvents is set to false, all the On....() methods of this document will stop raising events.
- `RemotePanelLayout` (RcpLayout, get) — Gets the Remote Control Panel layout for this document.
- `RhinoDocument` (RhinoDoc, get) — 
- `RuntimeID` (UInt64, get) — Gets the runtime ID of this document. RuntimeID is assigned once and never changed. It is also not deserialized so every document will always have a unique ID when loaded.
- `ScheduleDelay` (Int32, get) — Gets the delay for the next scheduled solution. If there is no solution scheduled the delay will be negative.
- `SelectedCount` (Int32, get) — Gets the number of selected objects
- `SelectionTopology` (GH_SelectionTopology, get) — Gets the selection topology of the document.
- `SolutionDepth` (Int32, get) — Gets the number of nested solutions currently running. If no solutions are running, the depth should be zero. If one solution is runnnig, the depth should be one. Higher depths are only possible if a new solution was scheduled from within an existing solution with delay=0ms.
- `SolutionHistory` (List<GH_SolutionSpan>, get) — Gets a list of the last 1000 solution timespans, from oldest to youngest.
- `SolutionSpan` (TimeSpan, get) — Gets the timespan for the most recent solution.
- `SolutionState` (GH_ProcessStep, get) — Gets the state of this document.
- `StateServer` (GH_StateServer, get) — Gets the list of stored states in this document
- `Thumbnail` (Bitmap, get/set) — Gets or sets the thumbnail bitmap for this document.
- `UndoServer` (GH_UndoServer, get) — Gets the UndoServer instance that belongs to this document.
- `UndoUtil` (GH_UndoUtil, get) — Gets a temporary undo utility instance that allows you to easily create standard undo events.
- `ValueTable` (GH_SettingsServer, get) — Gets the value table associated with this document.
- `IgnoredUnknowns` (HashSet<Guid>, get) — A collection of missing component IDs to ignore during file load operations. Erase this cache to re-enable all the load warnings.
- `ScheduleInactive` (Int32, get) — Defined the delay used to indicate the absence of a schedule.
- `ScheduleRecursive` (Int32, get) — Defines the delay used to indicate the schedule should be immedate and recursive.

## GH_DocumentAssociations (class)

(No description provided in vendor docs for Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_DocumentAssociations.)

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_DocumentAssociations.htm)

### Constructors
- `public GH_DocumentAssociations()` — Initializes a new instance of the GH_DocumentAssociations class

### Methods
#### `public void Associate(string rhinoFile, string grasshopperFile)`

Add an association between a Rhino and a Grasshopper file. If a similar association already exists, it will be overwritten. This function reads and writes to the disk, it is SLOW. Only call it when you KNOW something changed.

**Parameters:**
- `rhinoFile` (System.String) — Path to Rhino 3dm file.
- `grasshopperFile` (System.String) — Path to Grasshopper ghx or gh file.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_DocumentAssociations_Associate.htm)

#### `public List<string> GetGrasshopperAssociations(string rhinoFile)`

Find all the Grasshopper ghx files that have been associated with a given Rhino file.

**Parameters:**
- `rhinoFile` (System.String) — Rhino file path.

**Returns:** `List<String>` — A list of files, ordered chronologically (most recent association first)

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_DocumentAssociations_GetGrasshopperAssociations.htm)

#### `public List<string> GetRhinoAssociations(string grasshopperFile)`

Find all the Rhino 3dm files that have been associated with a given Grasshopper file.

**Parameters:**
- `grasshopperFile` (System.String) — Grasshopper file path.

**Returns:** `List<String>` — A list of files, ordered chronologically (most recent association first)

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_DocumentAssociations_GetRhinoAssociations.htm)

## GH_DocumentContext (enum)

Enumerates all possible (de)activation contexts for documents.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_DocumentContext.htm)

### Values
- `Unknown` = `0` — Specifies unknown context. This should never be used.
- `None` = `1` — Specifies unset state. This is only used for documents that have never been in a context.
- `Open` = `2` — Indicates the document was created anew from a file.
- `Close` = `3` — Indicates the document has been unloaded from memory.
- `Loaded` = `4` — Indicates the document has been loaded into the Canvas.
- `Unloaded` = `5` — Indicates the document has been unloaded from the Canvas.
- `Lock` = `6` — Indicates the document has been locked. This is only possible for nested documents.
- `Unlock` = `7` — Indicates the document has been unlocked. This is only possible for nested documents.

## GH_DocumentIO (class)

Provides basic IO operations for GH_Documents.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_DocumentIO.htm)

### Constructors
- `public GH_DocumentIO()` — Create a new instance of GH_DocumentIO without an internal document.
- `public GH_DocumentIO(GH_Document internal_doc)` — Create a new instance of GH_DocumentIO with an internal document.

### Methods
#### `public void ClearClipboard(GH_ClipboardType clipboard)`

Clear the Clipboard Text content. This function wipes both the Text and UnicodeText clipboard fields.

**Parameters:**
- `clipboard` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_ClipboardType)

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_DocumentIO_ClearClipboard.htm)

#### `public bool Copy(GH_ClipboardType clipboard)`

Copy the internal document into the clipboard.

**Parameters:**
- `clipboard` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_ClipboardType) — Specifies the clipboard type to use.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_DocumentIO_Copy.htm)

#### `public bool Copy(GH_ClipboardType clipboard, bool limitToSelection)`

Copy the internal document into the clipboard.

**Parameters:**
- `clipboard` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_ClipboardType) — Specifies the clipboard type to use.
- `limitToSelection` (System.Boolean) — If true, only the selected objects will be copied.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_DocumentIO_Copy_1.htm)

#### `public bool Copy(GH_ClipboardType clipboard, IEnumerable<Guid> guids)`

Copy the specified objects in the internal document into the clipboard.

**Parameters:**
- `clipboard` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_ClipboardType) — Specifies the clipboard type to use.
- `guids` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Guid>) — List of object ids to copy.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_DocumentIO_Copy_2.htm)

#### `public static Bitmap GetDocumentIcon(string filePath, Size size)`

Try and extract the icon image of a document. Not all gh/ghx files contain an icon so this function may return null. The document itself is not loaded and rendered, so the icon really has to be part of the file already.

**Parameters:**
- `filePath` (System.String) — Filepath to examine.
- `size` (System.Drawing.Size)

**Returns:** `Bitmap` — An icon bitmap on success or null on failure. Caller is responsible for disposing of the image.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_DocumentIO_GetDocumentIcon.htm)

#### `public static Bitmap GetDocumentThumbnail(string filePath)`

Try and extract the preview image of a document. Not all gh/ghx files contain a thumbnail so this function may return null. The document itself is not loaded and rendered, so the thumbnail really has to be part of the file already.

**Parameters:**
- `filePath` (System.String) — Filepath to examine.

**Returns:** `Bitmap` — A preview bitmap on success or null on failure. Caller is responsible for disposing of the image.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_DocumentIO_GetDocumentThumbnail.htm)

#### `public static DialogResult SubsidiaryDocumentSavePrompt(GH_Document document)`

Display a 'stuff has changed do you want to save' message box with Yes/No/Cancel options.

**Parameters:**

**Returns:** `DialogResult` — Yes, No, Cancel or None state.

#### `public static DialogResult SubsidiaryDocumentSavePrompt(string documentName, bool modified, int modifiedSubsidiaries)`

Display a 'stuff has changed do you want to save' message box with Yes/No/Cancel options.

**Parameters:**
- `documentName` (System.String) — Name of top level document.
- `modified` (System.Boolean) — Boolean indicating whether top-level document is modified.
- `modifiedSubsidiaries` (System.Int32) — Number of modified subsidiary documents.

**Returns:** `DialogResult` — Yes, No, Cancel or None state.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_DocumentIO_SubsidiaryDocumentSavePrompt_1.htm)

### Properties
- `DisableOverwriteProtection` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets whether the file overwrite protection dialog ought to be shown during risky saves.
- `Document` (GH_Document, get/set) — Gets or sets the internal document.
- `IsDocument` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether or not this instance of GH_DocumentIO contains an internal document.
- `LocalClipboardContent` (String, get/set) — Gets the contents of the local clipboard.

## GH_DocumentObject (class)

Standard implementation of IGH_DocumentObject.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_DocumentObject.htm)

### Constructors
- `protected GH_DocumentObject(IGH_InstanceDescription tag)` — Initializes a new instance of the GH_DocumentObject class
- `protected GH_DocumentObject(string name, string nickName, string description, string category, string subCategory)` — Initializes a new instance of the GH_DocumentObject class

### Methods

#### `public virtual void ExpirePreview(bool redraw)`

Call this function when you suspect that the preview has expired for this object. This will cause the display cache to be eradicated.

**Parameters:**
- `redraw` (System.Boolean)

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_DocumentObject_ExpirePreview.htm)

#### `public virtual void ExpireSolution(bool recompute)`

Call this function whenever you do something which expires the current solution. This will make sure all caches are erased, all downstream objects are expired and that the event is raised. The default implementation merely places a call to OnSolutionExpired(), override this function in derived classes to make sure you clear local data caches and expire downstream objects.

**Parameters:**
- `recompute` (System.Boolean) — If True, the solution will be recomputed straight away.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_DocumentObject_ExpireSolution.htm)

#### `protected internal virtual string HtmlHelp_Source()`

Return a String which contains HTML formatted source for the help topic. If you want to pass a URL that points to a remote page, then prefix the URL with a GOTO: tag, like so: GOTO:http://www.YourWebAddressHere.com

**Remarks:** You can use the GUI.GH_HtmlFormatter class to generate an HTML page which conforms to Grasshopper Help standards

**Returns:** `String` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_DocumentObject_HtmlHelp_Source.htm)

Utility function for inserting text boxes into menus.

**Parameters:**
- `menu` (System.Windows.Forms.ToolStripDropDown) — Menu to add a textbox to.
- `text` (System.String) — Content of textbox.
- `keydown` (Grasshopper.GUI.GH_MenuTextBox.KeyDownEventHandler) — Delegate for the KeyDown event.
- `textchanged` (Grasshopper.GUI.GH_MenuTextBox.TextChangedEventHandler) — Delegate for the TextChanged event.
- `enabled` (System.Boolean) — If true, the textbox will be enabled.
- `width` (System.Int32) — If larger than zero, we'll do our very best to grow the menu to the given width. No guarantees.
- `lockOnFocus` (System.Boolean) — If true, then a GotFocus event will lock the other menu items.

**Returns:** `ToolStripTextBox` — The appended text box item.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_DocumentObject_Menu_AppendTextItem_1.htm)

#### `public static bool Menu_MoveItem(ToolStripItem item, bool insertBelow, params string[] targets)`

Utility function for moving menu items.

**Parameters:**
- `item` (System.Windows.Forms.ToolStripItem) — Item to move, it must already be part of the dropdown menu.
- `insertBelow` (System.Boolean) — If true, the item will be inserted below the match, if false, above the match.
- `targets` (System.String[]) — Names of move to targets, the item will be inserted next to the first name, if that fails the second, etc. etc.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_DocumentObject_Menu_MoveItem.htm)

#### `public static bool Menu_MoveItem(ToolStripItem item, params string[] precedingItems)`

Utility function for moving menu items.

**Parameters:**
- `item` (System.Windows.Forms.ToolStripItem) — Item to move, it must already be part of a dropdown menu.
- `precedingItems` (System.String[]) — Names of preceding items, the item will be inserted below the first name, if that fails the second, etc. etc.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_DocumentObject_Menu_MoveItem_1.htm)

### Properties
- `Attributes` (IGH_Attributes, get/set) — Gets or sets the attributes that are associated with this object. Only set custom attributes if you know what you are doing.
- `Category` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the Category in which this object belongs. If HasCategory() returns false, this field has no meaning.
- `ComponentGuid` (Guid, get) — Returns a consistent ID for this object type. Every object must supply a unique and unchanging ID that is used to identify objects of the same type.
- `Description` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the description of the object. This field typically remains fixed during the lifetime of an object.
- `Exposure` (GH_Exposure, get) — Gets the exposure of this object in the Graphical User Interface. The default is to expose everywhere.
- `HasCategory` (Boolean, get) — Gets whether or not the Category field has been set.
- `HasSubCategory` (Boolean, get) — Gets whether or not the SubCategory field has been set.
- `Icon` (Bitmap, get) — Override this function to supply a custom icon (24x24 pixels). The result of this property is cached, so don't worry if icon retrieval is not very fast.
- `Icon_24x24` (Bitmap, get) — The icon associated with this object.
- `Icon_24x24_Locked` (Bitmap, get) — The greyscale icon of this object.
- `IconDisplayMode` (GH_IconDisplayMode, get/set) — Gets the current display mode of the object.
- `InstanceDescription` (String, get) — Gets a description of the current state of the object. This field is usually the same as the Description() field, but it might be variable when overridden.
- `InstanceGuid` (Guid, get) — Gets the ID of this runtime instance.
- `Keywords` (IEnumerable<String>, get) — Gets a list of additional keywords that describe the object. Typically this list is empty but you can override this property to aid in object searches.
- `Name` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the name of the object. This field typically remains fixed during the lifetime of an object.
- `NickName` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the nickname of the object. This field can be changed by the user.
- `Obsolete` (Boolean, get) — Gets whether this object is obsolete. Default implementation returns true if the class name contains the string "OBSOLETE" or if this class has been decorated with the ObsoleteAttribute. You are free to override this if you want, but I suggest adding the ObsoleteAttribute instead.
- `SubCategory` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the SubCategory in which this object belongs. If HasSubCategory() returns false, this field has no meaning.
- `m_attributes` (IGH_Attributes, get) —

## GH_DocumentRank (enum)

Enumerates all possible document rankings.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_DocumentRank.htm)

### Values
- `Unset` = `0` — Indicates no rank. Documents with unset rank are invalid.
- `TopLevel` = `1` — Top level ranking.
- `Subsidiary` = `2` — Subsidiary ranking indicates this document is part of another document.

## GH_DocumentServer (class)

Manages and maintains a collection of GH_Documents.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_DocumentServer.htm)

### Constructors
- `public GH_DocumentServer()` — Initializes a new instance of the GH_DocumentServer class

### Methods
#### `public void AddDocument(GH_Document document)`

Add a new document to the global list. If the document is already registered nothing will happen. This also applies to a different document which points to the same file path

**Parameters:**
- `document` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_Document) — Document to add.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_DocumentServer_AddDocument.htm)

- `success` (System.Boolean)

Add a new document to the global list. If there already is a document with the given path, it will be made active.

- `makeActive` (System.Boolean) — If true, the document will be loaded into the main canvas.

#### `public void RemoveDocument(GH_Document document)`

Removes a specific document from the global list. If the document isn't registered nothing will happen. If the document is owned by an IGH_DocumentOwner, the owner will be informed of the removal.

**Parameters:**
- `document` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_Document) — Document to remove.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_DocumentServer_RemoveDocument.htm)

#### `public bool SafeRemoveDocument(GH_Document document)`

Remove a document correctly from the server. This method shows user-interface prompts if there is unsaved data and will also save if needed.

**Parameters:**
- `document` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_Document) — Document to remove.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the document was removed, false if otherwise.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_DocumentServer_SafeRemoveDocument.htm)

### Properties
- `Document` (GH_Document, get) — Gets the document at the given index.
- `DocumentCount` (Int32, get) — Gets the number of documents stored in the global list.
- `DocumentNames` (List<String>, get) — Gets a string collection containing all the names of the documents in the global list.
- `ModifiedDocumentCount` (Int32, get) — Gets the total number of modified (unsaved) documents.

### Properties
- `FlatCollection` (IEnumerable<GH_Document>, get) — Gets a flat collection of all documents. The most subsidiary documents are at the start of the collection.
- `Root` (GH_DocumentTreeNode, get) — Gets the root node for this document.
- `RootDocument` (GH_Document, get) — Gets the document inside the root node of this tree.

Represents a single node in a document tree.

## GH_Exposure (enum)

Lists the different kinds of GUI exposure an object can have.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Exposure.htm)

### Values
- `hidden` = `-1` — Do not expose the object anywhere. This is useful for legacy components.
- `primary` = `2` — Expose the object in the first section on the toolbar.
- `secondary` = `4` — Expose the object in the second section on the toolbar.
- `tertiary` = `8` — Expose the object in the third section on the toolbar.
- `quarternary` = `16` — Expose the object in the fourth section on the toolbar.
- `quinary` = `32` — Expose the object in the fifth section on the toolbar.
- `senary` = `64` — Expose the object in the sixth section on the toolbar.
- `septenary` = `128` — Expose the object in the seventh section on the toolbar.
- `octonary` = `256` — Expose the object in the eighth section on the toolbar.
- `last` = `256` — This exposure will be the last subcategory available.
- `obscure` = `65536` — Mark the object as obscure. Use this option for components which are unlikely to be used often. You must combine obscure with one of the other flags (except hidden). Objects which are marked as Obscure will be the first to disappear when a ribbon resizes and they will always appear at the end of a panel.

## GH_ExternalFile (class)

Represents information about external files to be loaded.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ExternalFile.htm)

### Constructors
- `public GH_ExternalFile(string path)` — Create a new instance of the file info class

### Methods
#### `public bool IsIdenticalTo(GH_ExternalFile other)`

Compares two files for content. If the size and the hash or the other file are equal to this one, the files are considered identical.

**Parameters:**
- `other` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_ExternalFile)

**Returns:** `Boolean` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ExternalFile_IsIdenticalTo.htm)

### Properties
- `FileHash` (Guid, get) — Gets the file hash.
- `FileName` (String, get) — Gets the file name (without the extension).
- `FilePath` (String, get) — Gets the file path.
- `FileSize` (Int64, get) — Gets the file size (in bytes).
- `FileType` (GH_ExternalFileType, get) — Gets the file type as indicated by the extension.

## GH_ExternalFileType (enum)

Enumerates the possible external files that Grasshopper is interested in.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ExternalFileType.htm)

### Values
- `Unknown` = `0` — Unknown (invalid) file type.
- `Assembly` = `1` — File represents a GHA assembly.
- `UserObject` = `2` — File represents a GHUSER archive.
- `Cluster` = `3` — File represents a GHCLUSTER archive.

## GH_FileSystemEventServer (class)

Provides a easy-to-use way to keep tabs on files.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_FileSystemEventServer.htm)

### Methods
#### `public static void AddWatcher(Object owner, string path, GH_FileSystemEventServer.FileEvent callback)`

Add a new file watcher instance.

**Parameters:**
- `owner` (System.Object) — The object that owns this watcher.
- `path` (System.String) — The path of the file to watch.
- `callback` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_FileSystemEventServer.FileEvent) — Callback delegate used during file changes.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_FileSystemEventServer_AddWatcher.htm)

#### `public static void RemoveWatcher(Object owner)`

Remove all watchers that are linked to a specific object.

**Parameters:**
- `owner` (System.Object)

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_FileSystemEventServer_RemoveWatcher.htm)

#### `public static void RemoveWatcher(Object owner, string path)`

Remove all watchers that are linked to a specific object and a specific file path.

**Parameters:**
- `owner` (System.Object)
- `path` (System.String)

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_FileSystemEventServer_RemoveWatcher_1.htm)

### Properties
- `AllPaths` (IEnumerable<String>, get) — Gets a list of all the paths currently under surveillance.

## GH_FileSystemEventServer.FileEvent (delegate)

This delegate is called when changes to a certain file occur.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_FileSystemEventServer_FileEvent.htm)

## GH_FileWatcher (class)

Provides easy mechanisms to monitor changes to the local File System.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_FileWatcher.htm)

### Methods
#### `public static GH_FileWatcher CreateDirectoryWatcher(string folderPath, string filter, GH_FileWatcherEvents events, GH_FileWatcher.FileChanged delegate)`

Create a new FileSystem Watcher object aimed at a specific file.

**Parameters:**
- `folderPath` (System.String) — Folder to watch.
- `filter` (System.String) — Filter string for filenames.
- `events` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_FileWatcherEvents) — Events to watch for.
- `delegate` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_FileWatcher.FileChanged) — Callback delegate.

**Returns:** `GH_FileWatcher` — A new filewatcher aimed at the specified folder.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_FileWatcher_CreateDirectoryWatcher.htm)

#### `public static GH_FileWatcher CreateDirectoryWatcher(string folderPath, string filter, GH_FileWatcherEvents events, GH_FileWatcher.FileChangedSimple delegate)`

Create a new FileSystem Watcher object aimed at a specific file.

**Parameters:**
- `folderPath` (System.String) — Folder to watch.
- `filter` (System.String) — Filter string for filenames.
- `events` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_FileWatcherEvents) — Events to watch for.
- `delegate` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_FileWatcher.FileChangedSimple) — Callback delegate.

**Returns:** `GH_FileWatcher` — A new filewatcher aimed at the specified folder.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_FileWatcher_CreateDirectoryWatcher_1.htm)

#### `public static GH_FileWatcher CreateFileWatcher(string filePath, GH_FileWatcherEvents events, GH_FileWatcher.FileChanged delegate)`

Create a new FileSystem Watcher object aimed at a specific file.

**Parameters:**
- `filePath` (System.String) — File to watch.
- `events` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_FileWatcherEvents) — Events to watch for.
- `delegate` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_FileWatcher.FileChanged) — Callback delegate.

**Returns:** `GH_FileWatcher` — A new filewatcher aimed at the specified file.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_FileWatcher_CreateFileWatcher.htm)

#### `public static GH_FileWatcher CreateFileWatcher(string filePath, GH_FileWatcherEvents events, GH_FileWatcher.FileChangedSimple delegate)`

Create a new FileSystem Watcher object aimed at a specific file.

**Parameters:**
- `filePath` (System.String) — File to watch.
- `events` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_FileWatcherEvents) — Events to watch for.
- `delegate` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_FileWatcher.FileChangedSimple) — Callback delegate.

**Returns:** `GH_FileWatcher` — A new filewatcher aimed at the specified file.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_FileWatcher_CreateFileWatcher_1.htm)

#### `public void Dispose()`

Releases all resources used by the GH_FileWatcher

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_FileWatcher_Dispose.htm)

#### `protected virtual void Dispose(bool disposing)`

Releases the unmanaged resources used by the GH_FileWatcher and optionally releases the managed resources

**Parameters:**
- `disposing` (System.Boolean) — True to release both managed and unmanaged resources; false to release only unmanaged resources

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_FileWatcher_Dispose_1.htm)

#### `public void SetCustomUIThread(Control target)`

If you want to override the default Invoke behaviour, you must set InvokeOnDefaultThread to False and supply either a custom Control or a Form.

**Parameters:**
- `target` (System.Windows.Forms.Control) — GUI target object which will be used for Invoking File System Events.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_FileWatcher_SetCustomUIThread.htm)

#### `public void SetCustomUIThread(Form target)`

If you want to override the default Invoke behaviour, you must set InvokeOnDefaultThread to False and supply either a custom Control or a Form.

**Parameters:**
- `target` (System.Windows.Forms.Form) — GUI target object which will be used for Invoking File System Events.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_FileWatcher_SetCustomUIThread_1.htm)

### Properties
- `Active` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets the active flag on this watcher.
- `Buffer` (TimeSpan, get/set) — Gets or sets the time during which events are buffered. If multiple events for the same file occur within the buffered timespan, they will be merged into one event.
- `InvokeOnDefaultThread` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets invoke behaviour for events. If true, then the event delegate is called via the Grasshopper main UI thread. This is almost always what you need.
- `IsDisposed` (Boolean, get) — 
- `Path` (String, get) — Gets the file or folder path currently being watched.

## GH_FileWatcher.FileChanged (delegate)

Delegate method for file change callbacks.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_FileWatcher_FileChanged.htm)

## GH_FileWatcher.FileChangedSimple (delegate)

Delegate method for file change callbacks.

**Remarks:** See AlsoReferenceGrasshopper.Kernel Namespace

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_FileWatcher_FileChangedSimple.htm)

## GH_FileWatcherEvents (enum)

Enumerates all possible file watcher events. These can be combined.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_FileWatcherEvents.htm)

### Values
- `None` = `0` — No events are specified.
- `Changed` = `1` — The file is changed.
- `Renamed` = `2` — The file is renamed.
- `Created` = `4` — A file is created.
- `Deleted` = `8` — A file is deleted.
- `All` = `15` — Represents all possible events.

## GH_Filter (enum)

Enumerates all object Search filters.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Filter.htm)

### Values
- `DontCare` = `0` — Property is ignored.
- `Include` = `1` — Objects are requires to have this state in order to be included.
- `Exclude` = `2` — Objects are not allowed to have this state in order to be included.

#### `public static Font NewFont(FontFamily family, float size, FontStyle style)`

Safe font constructor.

**Parameters:**
- `family` (System.Drawing.FontFamily) — Font family.
- `size` (System.Single) — Size (in points).
- `style` (System.Drawing.FontStyle) — Style (if available).

**Returns:** `Font` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_FontServer_NewFont_4.htm)

#### `public static Font NewFont(string family, float size, FontStyle style)`

High level function for Font creation. We've ran into loads of trouble in the past with missing Fonts. Never create a font directly via GDI+, always use this method.

**Parameters:**
- `family` (System.String) — Font Family Name. If a font is found with the exact name, it will be loaded.
- `size` (System.Single) — Size of font.
- `style` (System.Drawing.FontStyle) — Font style, if the requested family does not support the requested style, the style flags will be ignored.

**Returns:** `Font` — A font that might or might not be what you asked for.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_FontServer_NewFont_5.htm)

#### `public static Font StringToFont(string text)`

Convert a font description string back into a font.

**Parameters:**
- `text` (System.String) — Text to parse.

**Returns:** `Font` — Font represented by the text, or null if no font could be created.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_FontServer_StringToFont.htm)

#### `public static int StringWidth(IEnumerable<string> text, Font font)`

Measure the maximum width of a collection of strings

**Parameters:**
- `text` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<String>) — The strings to measure
- `font` (System.Drawing.Font) — The font to measure with

**Returns:** `Int32` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_FontServer_StringWidth.htm)

#### `public static int StringWidth(string text, Font font)`

Measure the width of a string in pixels

**Parameters:**
- `text` (System.String) — The string to measure
- `font` (System.Drawing.Font) — The font to measure with

**Returns:** `Int32` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_FontServer_StringWidth_1.htm)

### Properties
- `Console` (Font, get) — FontFamily.GenericMonospace, 10pt, regular
- `ConsoleAdjusted` (Font, get) — FontFamily.GenericMonospace, 10pt, regular, adjusted to counter UI scaling.
- `ConsoleSmall` (Font, get) — FontFamily.GenericMonospace, 8pt, regular
- `ConsoleSmallAdjusted` (Font, get) — FontFamily.GenericMonospace, 8pt, regular, adjusted to counter UI scaling.
- `FamilyConsole` (FontFamily, get/set) — Gets or sets the FontFamily used for monospaced fonts in Grasshopper.
- `FamilyScript` (FontFamily, get/set) — Gets or sets the FontFamily used for scripted fonts in Grasshopper.
- `FamilyStandard` (FontFamily, get/set) — Gets or sets the FontFamily used for standard fonts in Grasshopper.
- `Large` (Font, get) — SystemFonts.CaptionFont, 10pt, bold
- `LargeAdjusted` (Font, get) — SystemFonts.CaptionFont, 10pt, bold, adjusted for logical pixel size.
- `Script` (Font, get) — FontFamily.GenericSansSerif, 10pt, italic
- `ScriptSmall` (Font, get) — FontFamily.GenericSansSerif, 8pt, italic
- `Small` (Font, get) — SystemFonts.CaptionFont, 6pt, regular
- `Standard` (Font, get) — SystemFonts.CaptionFont, 8pt, regular
- `StandardAdjusted` (Font, get) — SystemFonts.CaptionFont, 8pt, regular, adjusted for logical pixel size.
- `StandardBold` (Font, get) — SystemFonts.CaptionFont, 8pt, bold
- `StandardItalic` (Font, get) — SystemFonts.CaptionFont, 8pt, italic

#### `public static string FormatFilePath(string path, int maxLength)`

Format a filepath so that it doesn't exceed a string length. If the filepath filename is already longer than maxLength it will be returned intact.

**Parameters:**
- `path` (System.String) — Path for format.
- `maxLength` (System.Int32) — Maximum number of characters to use.

**Returns:** `String` — The formatted path.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Format_FormatFilePath.htm)

#### `public static string FormatFilePath(string path, int maxWidth, Font font)`

Format a filepath so that it doesn't exceed width. If the filepath filename is already longer than maxLength it will be returned intact.

**Parameters:**
- `path` (System.String) — Path to format.
- `maxWidth` (System.Int32) — Maximum allowed width (in pixels)
- `font` (System.Drawing.Font) — Font to use for measuring.

**Returns:** `String` — The formatted path.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_Format_FormatFilePath_1.htm)

## GH_GHALoadingEventArgs (class)

Event arguments used in the GH_GHALoaded event on GH_ComponentServer.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_GHALoadingEventArgs.htm)

### Properties
- `Assembly` (Assembly, get) — Gets the assembly that was loaded from the GHA file.
- `FileName` (String, get) — Gets the location of the gha file.
- `Icon` (Bitmap, get) — Gets the official icon of the GHA file.
- `Id` (Guid, get) — Gets the Id of the GHA file.
- `LoadingMechanism` (GH_LoadingMechanism, get) — Gets the mechanism that was used to load the file.
- `Name` (String, get) — Gets the official name of the GHA file.

## GH_GetterResult (enum)

Enumerates typical getter results.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_GetterResult.htm)

### Values
- `accept` = `0`
- `success` = `1`
- `cancel` = `2`

## GH_GuidConflictSolution (enum)

Contains a list of all possible Assembly GUID conflict solutions

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_GuidConflictSolution.htm)

### Values
- `Unset` = `0` — Indicates there is no solution picked yet
- `Replace` = `1` — Replace the loaded proxy with the conflicting proxy
- `ReplaceAll` = `2` — Replace all loaded proxies with all conflicting proxies in the current assembly
- `Skip` = `3` — Skip the conflicting proxy
- `SkipAll` = `4` — Skip all conflicting proxies in the current assembly

## GH_IconDisplayMode (enum)

Lists all display modes that individual objects can have.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_IconDisplayMode.htm)

### Values
- `application` = `0` — Inherit the mode from the application setting. This is the default setting.
- `icon` = `1` — Always display the icon.
- `name` = `2` — Always display the nickname.

## GH_InstanceDescription (class)

Base implementation of IGH_InstanceDescription. Consider deriving from this class rather than implementing IGH_InstanceDescription from scratch.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_InstanceDescription.htm)

### Methods
#### `public void CopyFrom(IGH_InstanceDescription other)`

Copy all fields (except the instance ID) from another instance description.

**Parameters:**
- `other` (Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_InstanceDescription) — Object to mimic.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_InstanceDescription_CopyFrom.htm)

#### `public void NewInstanceGuid()`

Generate a new random instance GUID

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_InstanceDescription_NewInstanceGuid.htm)

#### `public void NewInstanceGuid(Guid UUID)`

Set the instance ID to be a specific GUID. This is very dangerous, only use this function if you're 6"4' and your first name is David.

**Parameters:**
- `UUID` (System.Guid)

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_InstanceDescription_NewInstanceGuid_1.htm)

#### `public virtual bool Read(GH_IReader reader)`

Default deserialization. Only retrieves Name, NickName, Description and InstanceGuid fields. If you want to retrieve additional fields use ReadFull() instead. However, ReadFull() will only work if the archive was written using WriteFull().

**Parameters:**
- `reader` (GH_IO.Serialization.GH_IReader) — Object to deserialize with.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — Always True.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_InstanceDescription_Read.htm)

#### `public virtual bool ReadFull(GH_IReader reader)`

GH_InstanceDescription does not by default serialize all fields. Use this function to read all fields from the archive. This method is compatible with the default Write()/Read() operations.

**Parameters:**
- `reader` (GH_IO.Serialization.GH_IReader) — Writer for deserialization.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_InstanceDescription_ReadFull.htm)

#### `public virtual bool Write(GH_IWriter writer)`

Default serialization. Only stores Name, NickName, Description and InstanceGuid fields. If you want to store additional fields use WriteFull() instead.

**Parameters:**
- `writer` (GH_IO.Serialization.GH_IWriter) — Object to serialize with.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — Always True.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_InstanceDescription_Write.htm)

#### `public virtual bool WriteFull(GH_IWriter writer)`

GH_InstanceDescription does not by default serialize all fields. Use this function to write all fields to the archive. This method is compatible with the default Write()/Read() operations.

**Parameters:**
- `writer` (GH_IO.Serialization.GH_IWriter) — Writer for serialization.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_InstanceDescription_WriteFull.htm)

### Properties
- `Category` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the Category in which this object belongs. If HasCategory() returns false, this field has no meaning.
- `Description` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the description of the object. This field typically remains fixed during the lifetime of an object.
- `HasCategory` (Boolean, get) — Gets whether or not the Category field has been set.
- `HasSubCategory` (Boolean, get) — Gets whether or not the SubCategory field has been set.
- `InstanceDescription` (String, get) — Gets a description of the current state of the object. This field is usually the same as the Description() field, but it might be variable when overridden.
- `InstanceGuid` (Guid, get) — Gets the ID of this runtime instance.
- `Keywords` (IEnumerable<String>, get) — Gets a list of additional keywords that describe the object. Typically this list is empty but you can override this property to aid in object searches.
- `Name` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the name of the object. This field typically remains fixed during the lifetime of an object.
- `NickName` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the nickname of the object. This field can be changed by the user.
- `SubCategory` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the SubCategory in which this object belongs. If HasSubCategory() returns false, this field has no meaning.

## GH_LibraryLicense (enum)

Enumerates the possible library types for GHA assemblies.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_LibraryLicense.htm)

### Values
- `unset` = `0` — Unset library type. You should not return this value.
- `expired` = `1` — The license for this library has expired and it can no longer be used.
- `alpha` = `2` — This library is an Alpha release.
- `beta` = `3` — This library is a Beta release.
- `free` = `4` — This library is freeware.
- `opensource` = `5` — This library is distributed under an open source license.
- `trial` = `6` — This library is running in Trial mode.
- `commercial` = `7` — This library is running under a Commercial license.
- `educational` = `8` — This library is running under an Educational license.
- `developer` = `9` — This library is running under a Developer license.
- `reseller` = `10` — This library is running under a Reseller license.
- `other` = `11` — Undefined library type.

## GH_LineParser (class)

Provides basic functionality for the custom ReadFile component script parsing.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_LineParser.htm)

### Constructors
- `protected GH_LineParser()` — Initializes a new instance of the GH_LineParser class

### Methods
#### `public abstract Object ParseLine(string line, ref GH_LineParserAction action)`

Parse a line of text and convert it to custom data.

**Parameters:**
- `line` (System.String) — Line to parse.
- `action` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_LineParserAction) — Set the action to take after this function returns. The default is Include.

**Returns:** `Object` — The data represented by the line.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_LineParser_ParseLine.htm)

## GH_LineParserAction (enum)

Enumerates the possible actions a line parser can invoke.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_LineParserAction.htm)

### Values
- `Include` = `0` — Include the return code of the parser function.
- `Ignore` = `1` — Ignore this line of text and move on to the next line.
- `Abort` = `2` — Ignore this line of text and don't parse all subsequent lines.

## GH_ListUtil (class)

Provides utilities for dealing with list of Data from input parameters.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ListUtil.htm)

Count all object references in L

- `L` (System.Collections.Generic.List<T>) — List to parse

**Returns:** `Int32` — Number of proper objects in L

Count all null references in L

**Parameters:**
- `L` (System.Collections.Generic.List<T>) — List to parse

**Returns:** `Int32` — Number of nulls in L

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ListUtil_CountNullRefs__1.htm)

#### `public static bool EnsureNonNullCount<T>(List<T> L, int N)`

Tests a list to see if it contains sufficient non-null references.

**Parameters:**
- `L` (System.Collections.Generic.List<T>) — List to parse
- `N` (System.Int32)

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if L contains N or more non-null references.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ListUtil_EnsureNonNullCount__1.htm)

#### `public static int FirstNonNullItem<T>(List<T> L)`

Returns the index of the first non-null item in a list.

**Parameters:**
- `L` (System.Collections.Generic.List<T>) — List to parse.

**Returns:** `Int32` — The index of the first non-null item or -1 if no object reference could be found.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ListUtil_FirstNonNullItem__1.htm)

#### `public static List<T> RemoveNullRefs<T>(List<T> L)`

Remove all null references from a list of items.

**Parameters:**
- `L` (System.Collections.Generic.List<T>) — List to filter

**Returns:** `List<T>` — List containing only non-null items. References are shared with L

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ListUtil_RemoveNullRefs__1.htm)

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_LoadingAttribute.htm)

### Constructors
- `public GH_LoadingAttribute(GH_LoadingDemand demand)` — Initializes a new instance of the GH_LoadingAttribute class

## GH_LoadingDemand (enum)

Enumerates all assembly loading behaviour demands.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_LoadingDemand.htm)

### Values
- `None` = `-1` — Indicates no valid loading behaviour. Do not use this value in the GH_GHALoadingAttribute constructor.
- `Default` = `0` — Use the application settings.
- `ForceCOFF` = `1` — Force this assembly to be loaded using COFF byte arrays.
- `ForceDirect` = `2` — Force this assembly to be loaded directly from the disk.

## GH_LoadingInstruction (enum)

Enumerates all external GHA loading process instructions.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_LoadingInstruction.htm)

### Values
- `Proceed` = `0` — Proceed with GHA loading.
- `Abort` = `1` — Abort the GHA loading, all your components will be skipped.

## GH_LoadingMechanism (enum)

Enumerates all assembly loading mechanisms.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_LoadingMechanism.htm)

### Values
- `Unknown` = `-1` — Assembly was loaded with an unknown mechanism.
- `COFF` = `1` — Assembly was loaded from a memory byte-array representation of the file.
- `Direct` = `2` — Assembly was loaded directly from disk.

## GH_MatchLevel (enum)

All possible string matching schemes.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_MatchLevel.htm)

### Values
- `None` = `0` — No match is performed.
- `Exact` = `1` — String must match the key exactly.
- `Start` = `2` — String must start with the key.
- `WordExact` = `4` — String must contain word that is an exact match with the key.
- `WordStart` = `8` — String must contain a word that starts with the key.
- `Contains` = `16` — String must contain the key.
- `Fuzzy` = `32` — String must contain word that is somewhat similar to the key.
- `AllClean` = `31` — Represents all match types except fuzzy matching.
- `All` = `63` — Represents all match types.

## GH_MatchWeight (enum)

Weighting factors of string matches.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_MatchWeight.htm)

### Values
- `Exact` = `100`
- `Start` = `100`
- `WordExact` = `100`
- `WordStart` = `100`
- `Contains` = `10`
- `Fuzzy` = `10`

## GH_MemoryBitmap (class)

Utility class for ultra-fast bitmap sampling and filters.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_MemoryBitmap.htm)

### Constructors
- `public GH_MemoryBitmap(Bitmap bitmap)` — Default constructor.
- `public GH_MemoryBitmap(Bitmap bitmap, WrapMode wrap)` — Constructor with Wrap mode override.

### Methods
#### `public byte A(int x, int y)`

Gets the alpha channel value at the given pixel coordinates.

**Parameters:**
- `x` (System.Int32) — X coordinate (column) of pixel. Must be on image interior.
- `y` (System.Int32) — Y coordinate (column) of pixel. Must be on image interior.

**Returns:** `Byte` — Value of alpha channel at given pixel or 255 if the alpha channel is not defined.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_MemoryBitmap_A.htm)

#### `public byte B(int x, int y)`

Gets the blue channel value at the given pixel coordinates.

**Parameters:**
- `x` (System.Int32) — X coordinate (column) of pixel. Must be on image interior.
- `y` (System.Int32) — Y coordinate (column) of pixel. Must be on image interior.

**Returns:** `Byte` — Value of Blue channel at given pixel.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_MemoryBitmap_B.htm)

#### `public Color Colour(int x, int y)`

Gets the colour at the given pixel coordinates.

**Parameters:**
- `x` (System.Int32) — X coordinate (column) of pixel. Must be on image interior.
- `y` (System.Int32) — Y coordinate (column) of pixel. Must be on image interior.

**Returns:** `Color` — Colour at given pixel.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_MemoryBitmap_Colour.htm)

#### `public void Colour(int x, int y, Color col)`

Sets the colour at the given pixel coordinates.

**Parameters:**
- `x` (System.Int32) — X coordinate (column) of pixel. Must be on image interior.
- `y` (System.Int32) — Y coordinate (column) of pixel. Must be on image interior.
- `col` (System.Drawing.Color) — Color to assign.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_MemoryBitmap_Colour_1.htm)

#### `public void CopyChannel(GH_BitmapChannel source, GH_BitmapChannel target)`

Copy the contents of one channel into another.

**Parameters:**
- `source` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_BitmapChannel) — Source channel (compound channels are not allowed).
- `target` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_BitmapChannel) — Target channel (compound channels are not allowed).

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_MemoryBitmap_CopyChannel.htm)

#### `public void Filter_AlphaEdges(Padding padding, byte alpha_override)`

Set the alpha component on all edges to a specific value.

**Parameters:**
- `padding` (System.Windows.Forms.Padding) — Width of alpha band along all edges. Must be a valid padding construct.
- `alpha_override` (System.Byte) — Value of alpha to apply.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_MemoryBitmap_Filter_AlphaEdges.htm)

#### `public void Filter_Blur()`

Blur the image in both directions.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_MemoryBitmap_Filter_Blur.htm)

#### `public void Filter_Blur(int N)`

Blur the image N times in both directions.

**Parameters:**
- `N` (System.Int32) — Number of times to perform the blurring operation.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_MemoryBitmap_Filter_Blur_1.htm)

#### `public void Filter_Blur(int interior_weight, int exterior_weight)`

Blur the image in both directions.

**Parameters:**
- `interior_weight` (System.Int32) — Weighting factor for pixels at the center of the convolution matrix.
- `exterior_weight` (System.Int32) — Weighting factor for pixels at the edges of the convolution matrix.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_MemoryBitmap_Filter_Blur_2.htm)

#### `public void Filter_Blur(int N, int interior_weight, int exterior_weight)`

Blur the image N times in both directions.

**Parameters:**
- `N` (System.Int32) — Number of times to perform the blurring operation.
- `interior_weight` (System.Int32) — Weighting factor for pixels at the center of the convolution matrix.
- `exterior_weight` (System.Int32) — Weighting factor for pixels at the edges of the convolution matrix.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_MemoryBitmap_Filter_Blur_3.htm)

#### `public void Filter_BlurAlpha(int N, int interior_weight, int exterior_weight)`

Blur the alpha channel of the image N times in both directions.

**Parameters:**
- `N` (System.Int32) — Number of times to perform the blurring operation.
- `interior_weight` (System.Int32) — Weighting factor for pixels at the center of the convolution matrix.
- `exterior_weight` (System.Int32) — Weighting factor for pixels at the edges of the convolution matrix.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_MemoryBitmap_Filter_BlurAlpha.htm)

#### `public void Filter_Contrast(GH_BitmapChannel channel, double factor)`

Adjust the contrast of a specific channel.

**Parameters:**
- `channel` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_BitmapChannel) — Channel(s) to modify.
- `factor` (System.Double) — Contrast boost factor. A factor of 1.0 will not affect contrast. Lower values will lessen contrast and higher values will increase contrast.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_MemoryBitmap_Filter_Contrast.htm)

#### `public void Filter_DitherPattern(Color colour)`

Create a checkerboard dither pattern.

**Parameters:**
- `colour` (System.Drawing.Color) — Colour value to set at dithered pixels.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_MemoryBitmap_Filter_DitherPattern.htm)

#### `public void Filter_DropShadow(Size offset, int blur, double darkness)`

Add a drop-shadow to the image. Image is not grown, so if you want the drop shadow to extend beyond the image boundaries, you have to add padding to the image first.

**Parameters:**
- `offset` (System.Drawing.Size) — Number of pixels (x and y) to offset the drop shadow.
- `blur` (System.Int32) — Amount of blurring for the shadow.
- `darkness` (System.Double) — Darkness factor for the shadow.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_MemoryBitmap_Filter_DropShadow.htm)

#### `public void Filter_Dullify()`

Reduces contrast based on opacity. This filter pulls all colours towards dull grey, without wrecking drop-shadows.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_MemoryBitmap_Filter_Dullify.htm)

#### `public void Filter_Equalize(GH_BitmapChannel channel, byte value)`

Set all the values in a specific channel to a specific value.

**Parameters:**
- `channel` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_BitmapChannel) — Channel(s) to equalize.
- `value` (System.Byte) — Value to equalize with.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_MemoryBitmap_Filter_Equalize.htm)

Convert the image to a greyscale shadow of its former self.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_MemoryBitmap_Filter_GreyScale.htm)

Convert the image to a greyscale shadow of its former self.

**Parameters:**
- `amount` (System.Double) — Greyscale factor. 1.0 equals complete greyscale, 0.0 equals no greyscale, -1.0 equals inverse greyscale

Convert the image to a greyscale representation of the hues.
