- `writer` (GH_IO.Serialization.GH_IWriter)

**Returns:** `Boolean` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_PersistentParam_1_Write.htm)

#### `public virtual bool WriteFull(GH_IWriter writer)`

GH_InstanceDescription does not by default serialize all fields. Use this function to write all fields to the archive. This method is compatible with the default Write()/Read() operations.

**Parameters:**
- `writer` (GH_IO.Serialization.GH_IWriter) — Writer for serialization.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_InstanceDescription_WriteFull.htm)

### Properties
- `Access` (GH_ParamAccess, get/set) — Gets or sets the Access level for this parameter.
- `Attributes` (IGH_Attributes, get/set) — Gets or sets the attributes that are associated with this object. Only set custom attributes if you know what you are doing.
- `Category` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the Category in which this object belongs. If HasCategory() returns false, this field has no meaning.
- `ComponentGuid` (Guid, get) — Returns a consistent ID for this object type. Every object must supply a unique and unchanging ID that is used to identify objects of the same type.
- `DataMapping` (GH_DataMapping, get/set) — Gets or sets the data mapping of this Parameter.
- `DataType` (GH_ParamData, get) — Gets the type of data stored in this parameter.
- `Description` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the description of the object. This field typically remains fixed during the lifetime of an object.
- `Exposure` (GH_Exposure, get) — Gets the exposure of this object in the Graphical User Interface. The default is to expose everywhere.
- `HasCategory` (Boolean, get) — Gets whether or not the Category field has been set.
- `HasProxySources` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether or not this parameter maintains proxy sources. Proxy sources are used during file IO, when actual sources might not be available yet. Once an IO operation has been completed there should be no more proxy sources.
- `HasSubCategory` (Boolean, get) — Gets whether or not the SubCategory field has been set.
- `Icon` (Bitmap, get) — (Overrides GH_DocumentObject.Icon.)
- `Icon_24x24` (Bitmap, get) — The icon associated with this object.
- `Icon_24x24_Locked` (Bitmap, get) — The greyscale icon of this object.
- `IconCapableUI` (Boolean, get) — By default the NickName menu item supports the Icon Mode override toggle. If your UI is not capable of displaying icons, then override this property and return False.
- `IconDisplayMode` (GH_IconDisplayMode, get/set) — Gets the current display mode of the object.
- `InstanceDescription` (String, get) — Gets the description of this instance. The default description is about the amount and source of the local values.
- `InstanceGuid` (Guid, get) — Gets the ID of this runtime instance.
- `IsDataProvider` (Boolean, get) — (Inherited from GH_Param<T>.)
- `IsPrincipal` (GH_PrincipalState, get) — Gets whether this parameter is a principal parameter. Principal parameters are maintained by components and are not part of the IGH_Param interface.
- `Keywords` (IEnumerable<String>, get) — Gets a list of additional keywords that describe the object. Typically this list is empty but you can override this property to aid in object searches.
- `Kind` (GH_ParamKind, get) — Gets the parameter kind. The kind is evaluated lazily and cached.
- `Locked` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets the enabled flag of this object. Disabled objects are ignored during solutions.
- `MutableNickName` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value that enables Nick name changes through the menu. The default is TRUE.
- `Name` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the name of the object. This field typically remains fixed during the lifetime of an object.
- `NickName` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the nickname of the object. This field can be changed by the user.
- `Obsolete` (Boolean, get) — Gets whether this object is obsolete. Default implementation returns true if the class name contains the string "OBSOLETE" or if this class has been decorated with the ObsoleteAttribute. You are free to override this if you want, but I suggest adding the ObsoleteAttribute instead.
- `Optional` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets whether or not this parameter is considered optional by the owner component. Empty, non-optional parameters prevent the component from being solved.
- `PersistentData` (GH_Structure<T>, get) — Gets the persistent data stored in this parameter. If you modify the persistent data, be sure to call the: OnObjectChanged(GH_ObjectEventType.PersistentData) event.
- `PersistentDataCount` (Int32, get) — Gets the number of persistent data items stored in this parameter.
- `Phase` (GH_SolutionPhase, get/set) — Gets or sets the solution phase this object is currenly in.
- `ProcessorTime` (TimeSpan, get) — (Inherited from GH_Param<T>.)
- `ProxySourceCount` (Int32, get) — Gets the number of proxy sources for this parameter. Proxy sources are used during file IO, when actual sources might not be available yet. Once an IO operation has been completed there should be no more proxy sources.
- `Recipients` (IList<IGH_Param>, get) — Gets a list of all the recipients of this parameter. I.e. a recipient has this parameter as one of the sources. The Recipient list is maintained by the parameter, do not modify it yourself.
- `Reverse` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets the data reverse modifier of this parameter.
- `RuntimeMessageLevel` (GH_RuntimeMessageLevel, get) — Returns the worst case flag for the current object If the object contains at least 1 error, the result is Error.If the object contains at least 1 warning, the result is Warning.If the object contains at least 1 message, the result is Remark.If the object contains no errors, no warnings and no messages, the result is Blank.
- `Simplify` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets the simplify modifier for this parameter.
- `SourceCount` (Int32, get) — Gets the number of sources for this parameter.
- `Sources` (IList<IGH_Param>, get) — Gets a list of source parameters. Do not modify this list, if you wish to add or remove sources, use dedicated functions like AddSource() and RemoveSource() instead.
- `StateTags` (GH_StateTagList, get) — Gets all the StateTags that are associated with this parameter. A state tag is a visual feedback icon that represents specific internal settings.
- `SubCategory` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the SubCategory in which this object belongs. If HasSubCategory() returns false, this field has no meaning.
- `Type` (Type, get) — Gets the Framework Type descriptor for the stored Data.
- `TypeName` (String, get) — (Overrides GH_Param<T>.TypeName.)
- `VolatileData` (IGH_Structure, get) — (Inherited from GH_Param<T>.)
- `VolatileDataCount` (Int32, get) — (Inherited from GH_Param<T>.)
- `WireDisplay` (GH_ParamWireDisplay, get/set) — Gets or sets the wire display style for this parameter. Wire display only affects the wires connected to the parameter input.
- `m_attributes` (IGH_Attributes, get) — (Inherited from GH_DocumentObject.)
- `m_data` (GH_Structure<T>, get) — Contains the runtime data for this parameter, also known as "Volatile" data.

## GH_PickBox (enum)

Represents the different ways in which picking rectangles can behave.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_PickBox.htm)

### Values
- `Window` = `0` — The picking box must completely surround the object box.
- `Crossing` = `1` — The picking box must only partially intersect the object box.

## GH_PingDocumentEventArgs (class)

Event arguments passed during PingDocument events.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_PingDocumentEventArgs.htm)

### Constructors
- `public GH_PingDocumentEventArgs()` — Initializes a new instance of the GH_PingDocumentEventArgs class

### Properties
- `Document` (GH_Document, get/set) — If you're a GH_Document that owns this object, you must fill out this field. This is the only way for objects to know which document they belong to.
- `m_document` (GH_Document, get) — 

## GH_PluginFolderType (enum)

Enumerates the possible folder types for GH_AssemblyFolderInfo

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_PluginFolderType.htm)

### Values
- `Core` = `0` — The Core folder is the one where native Grasshopper GHA files are installed.
- `Default` = `1` — The Default folder is where Grasshopper puts 3rd party GHA files.
- `RhiFolder` = `2` — A RhiFolder folder is created by an RHI installer package.
- `UserFolder` = `3` — A UserFolder is created by the user via the _GrasshopperDeveloperSettings command.

## GH_PreviewArgs (class)

This class is passed to Objects that implement IGH_PreviewObject during drawing operations.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_PreviewArgs.htm)

### Properties
- `DefaultCurveThickness` (Int32, get) — Gets the curve thickness as defined by the viewport display scheme.
- `Display` (DisplayPipeline, get) — Gets the Pipeline that is being used to draw the current preview.
- `Document` (GH_Document, get) — Gets the Grasshopper document that is currently drawing the preview.
- `MeshingParameters` (MeshingParameters, get) — Gets the meshing parameters to be used during meshing breps.
- `ShadeMaterial` (DisplayMaterial, get) — Gets the document default material for unselected Faces.
- `ShadeMaterial_Selected` (DisplayMaterial, get) — Gets the document default material for selected Faces.
- `Viewport` (RhinoViewport, get) — Gets the Viewport in which the current preview is drawn.
- `WireColour` (Color, get) — Gets the document default colour for unselected Wires.
- `WireColour_Selected` (Color, get) — Gets the document default colour for selected Wires.

## GH_PreviewFilter (enum)

Lists all possible Grasshopper preview filters.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_PreviewFilter.htm)

### Values
- `None` = `0` — Specifies no filtering is applied to the preview, i.e. all objects are drawn.
- `Selected` = `1` — Only selected objects are included in the preview.

## GH_PreviewMesh (enum)

Enumerates possible meshing parameters for documents.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_PreviewMesh.htm)

### Values
- `None` = `0` — No preview meshes.
- `LowQuality` = `1` — Rhino default for coarse meshes.
- `HighQuality` = `2` — Rhino default for smooth meshes.
- `Document` = `3` — Same meshing parameters as the current document.
- `Custom` = `4` — Custom meshing parameters.

## GH_PreviewMeshArgs (class)

This class is passed to objects that implement IGH_PreviewData during mesh drawing operations.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_PreviewMeshArgs.htm)

### Constructors
- `public GH_PreviewMeshArgs(RhinoViewport vp, DisplayPipeline pl, DisplayMaterial mat, MeshingParameters meshParams)` — Initializes a new instance of the GH_PreviewMeshArgs class

### Properties
- `Material` (DisplayMaterial, get) — Gets the material in which meshes ought to be drawn.
- `MeshingParameters` (MeshingParameters, get) — Gets the meshing parameters which ought to be used for meshing breps and surfaces. If this property returns a null reference, meshes are supposed to be omitted.
- `Pipeline` (DisplayPipeline, get) — Gets the Display Pipeline we're currently drawing with.
- `Viewport` (RhinoViewport, get) — Gets the Viewport in which we're currently drawing.

## GH_PreviewMode (enum)

Lists all possible Grasshopper preview modes.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_PreviewMode.htm)

### Values
- `Disabled` = `0` — Preview is not drawn in the Rhino viewports.
- `Wireframe` = `1` — Only wireframe preview is drawn in the Rhino Viewports.
- `Shaded` = `2` — Wireframe and shaded previews are drawn in the Rhino Viewports.

## GH_PrincipalState (enum)

Enumerates the different possible states in which the masterness of a parameter can occur.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_PrincipalState.htm)

### Values
- `CannotBePrincipal` = `0` — This parameter cannot be a principal parameter. It is not a valid component input.
- `IsPrincipal` = `1` — This parameter is the current principal parameter as defined by the component which owns it.
- `IsNotPrincipal` = `2` — This parameter is not the current principal parameter, but it could be.

## GH_ProcessStep (enum)

Enumerates all possible solution states in which the document can be.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ProcessStep.htm)

### Values
- `PreProcess` = `0` — The document is ready for a new solution
- `Process` = `1` — The document is currently calculating a new solution, no changes are allowed during this phase
- `PostProcess` = `2` — The document has completed a solution, and nothing has changed since.
- `Aborted` = `3` — The last solution has been aborted before it reached completion.

## GH_ProfilerMode (enum)

Lists all profiler modes.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ProfilerMode.htm)

### Values
- `None` = `0` — Indicates no profiling is required.
- `Processor` = `1` — Indicates processor time is being measured.
- `Memory` = `2` — Indicates memory footprint is being measured.

## GH_RelevantObjectFilter (enum)

Enumerates the possible filters to use in relevant object searches.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_RelevantObjectFilter.htm)

### Values
- `None` = `0` — Blank filter. When combined with another filter it is ignored.
- `Groups` = `1` — Include group objects in the search.
- `InputGrips` = `2` — Include input grips in the search.
- `OutputGrips` = `4` — Include output grips in the search.
- `Grips` = `6` — Include both input and outpu grips in the search.
- `Attributes` = `8` — Include attribute bounds in the search.
- `Wires` = `1024` — Include wires (currently not yet supported)
- `All` = `2147483647` — Search all possible objects.

## GH_RenderArgs (class)

This class is passed to objects that implement IGH_RenderAwareData or IGH_RenderAwareObject during render queue operations.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_RenderArgs.htm)

### Constructors
- `[ObsoleteAttribute] public GH_RenderArgs(RhinoDoc doc, ViewportInfo vp, RenderPrimitiveList geometry, Guid id, Dictionary<int, RenderMaterial> materialCache)` — Create new render arguments.
- `[ObsoleteAttribute] public GH_RenderArgs(RhinoDoc doc, ViewportInfo vp, RenderPrimitiveList geometry, Guid id, RenderMaterial matNormal, RenderMaterial matSelected)` — Create new render arguments.

### Methods
#### `public Transform PopModelTransform()`



**Returns:** `Transform` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_RenderArgs_PopModelTransform.htm)

#### `public void PushModelTransform(Transform transform)`



**Parameters:**
- `transform` (Transform)

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_RenderArgs_PushModelTransform.htm)

### Properties
- `Document` (RhinoDoc, get) — Gets the rhino document from whence the render originated.
- `MaterialNormal` (RenderMaterial, get) — Gets the material hint to use for unselected objects.
- `MaterialSelected` (RenderMaterial, get) — Gets the material hint to use for selected objects.
- `ModelTransform` (Transform, get) — Gets the current model transform.
- `PluginId` (Guid, get) — Gets the render plugin id that is about to render this geometry.
- `Viewport` (ViewportInfo, get) — Gets the viewport details involved in this rendering.
- `ViewportName` (String, get) — Gets the viewport name involved in this rendering.

## GH_RuntimeMessage (class)

Contains all fields that together define a RuntimeMessage. Runtime Messages are created and recorded by document objects during Grasshopper solutions.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_RuntimeMessage.htm)

### Constructors
- `public GH_RuntimeMessage(string nMessage, GH_RuntimeMessageLevel nType = GH_RuntimeMessageLevel.Remark, string nSource = null)` — Initializes a new instance of the GH_RuntimeMessage class

### Properties
- `Event` (DateTime, get) — Gets the time at which the message was recorded.
- `HasSource` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether or not this message has a source.
- `Message` (String, get) — Gets the contents of the message.
- `Source` (String, get) — Gets a string describing the source object that created the message.
- `Type` (GH_RuntimeMessageLevel, get) — Gets the type qualifier of the message.

## GH_RuntimeMessageLevel (enum)

Indicates the severity of a runtime message.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_RuntimeMessageLevel.htm)

### Values
- `Blank` = `0` — No warnings or errors were recorded during solver-time processes
- `Remark` = `255` — One of more messages (but no warnings and no errors) were recorded during solver-time processes.
- `Warning` = `10` — One or more warnings (but no errors) were recorded during solver-time processes
- `Error` = `20` — One or more errors (and possibly any number of warnings) were recorded during solver-time processes.

## GH_SelectionState (enum)

Enumerates the possible effects of a specific selection operation.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_SelectionState.htm)

### Values
- `Changed` = `0`
- `Unchanged` = `1`

## GH_SelectionTopology (enum)

Enumerates the possible selection topologies.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_SelectionTopology.htm)

### Values
- `None` = `0` — No selected objects.
- `Convex` = `1` — Selection set is convex, meaning unselected objects are not sandwiched between selected objects.
- `Concave` = `2` — Selection set is concave, meaning unselected objects are sandwiched between selected objects.

## GH_SettingsServer (class)

A single instance of this class is maintained at module level under the accessor "Settings".

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_SettingsServer.htm)

- `public GH_SettingsServer(string databaseName, bool loadSettings)` — Create a new instance of the GH_SettingsServer class. The server will be associated with a settings xml file in the %ApplicationData%\Grasshopper\ directory.

## GH_SolutionEventArgs (class)

Event arguments used for Document Solution events.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_SolutionEventArgs.htm)

### Properties
- `Document` (GH_Document, get) — Gets the document that raised the event, should be the same as the "sender" parameter in the event handler.
- `Duration` (TimeSpan, get) — Gets the duration for this solution. The duration is a more accurate measure than EndTime-StartTime.
- `StartTime` (DateTime, get) — Gets the time when the current solution began.

## GH_SolutionExpiredEventArgs (class)

Arguments for solution events.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_SolutionExpiredEventArgs.htm)

### Constructors
- `public GH_SolutionExpiredEventArgs(bool eRecompute)` — Initializes a new instance of the GH_SolutionExpiredEventArgs class

### Properties
- `Recompute` (Boolean, get) — 

## GH_SolutionMode (enum)

Enumerates all solution modes.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_SolutionMode.htm)

### Values
- `Default` = `0` — Represents the standard mode, error messages are displayed in custom forms.
- `CommandLine` = `1` — Represents reduced feedback mode. All messages are send to the Rhino command history, which is thread safe.
- `Silent` = `2` — Represents null feedback mode. No messages are send to any GUI.

## GH_SolutionPhase (enum)

The Phase flags are used to track object states during solutions.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_SolutionPhase.htm)

### Values
- `Blank` = `0` — There is no data available in this object. This object will be solved next time a solution begins.
- `Collecting` = `1` — This object is currently in the process of collecting data
- `Collected` = `2` — This object has finished collecting all data and is ready for computation.
- `Computing` = `3` — This object is currently in the process of computing new data
- `Computed` = `4` — This object has successfully completed data computation and it will be skipped in the next solution unless it expires in the meantime. This is the final step in a successful solution cycle.
- `Failed` = `10` — This object has failed to successfully complete the data computation stage. A new attempt at solving it will be made when the next solution starts. This is the final step in an unsuccessful solution cycle.

## GH_SolutionSpan (struct)

Represents the time interval for a GH_Document solution.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_SolutionSpan.htm)

### Constructors
- `public GH_SolutionSpan(DateTime t0, DateTime t1)` — Creates a new instance of the GH_SolutionSpan structure.

### Properties
- `EndTime` (DateTime, get) — Gets the time at which this solution was finished.
- `StartTime` (DateTime, get) — Gets the time at which this solution was started.

## GH_State (class)

Represents a single recorded document state. A state contains values for an arbitrary set of objects inside the document.

## GH_StateTagLayoutDirection (enum)

Enumerates the possible layout directions for parameter state tags.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_StateTagLayoutDirection.htm)

### Values
- `Left` = `0` — Floating and Input parameters should be layed out towards the left.
- `Right` = `1` — Output parameters should be layed out towards the right.

Call this function when you suspect that the preview has expired for this object. This will cause the display cache to be eradicated.

**Parameters:**
- `redraw` (System.Boolean)

#### `public override void ExpireSolution(bool recompute)`

Informs the document that owns this object that the solution has expired. The current object will be set to BLANK as a result. This method is recursive, it will also expire any and all objects which depend on this object. If you want a less destructive expiration, consider using ClearData(). If this object is already Blank, you should consider not expiring it.

**Parameters:**
- `recompute` (System.Boolean) — If True, the document will be instructed to solve the new state.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ActiveObject_ExpireSolution.htm)

### Properties
- `Attributes` (IGH_Attributes, get/set) — Gets or sets the attributes that are associated with this object. Only set custom attributes if you know what you are doing.
- `CancelToken` (CancellationToken, get) — 
- `Category` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the Category in which this object belongs. If HasCategory() returns false, this field has no meaning.
- `ClippingBox` (BoundingBox, get) — Gets the clipping box for all preview geometry drawn by this component and all associated parameters.
- `ComponentGuid` (Guid, get) — Returns a consistent ID for this object type. Every object must supply a unique and unchanging ID that is used to identify objects of the same type.
- `Description` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the description of the object. This field typically remains fixed during the lifetime of an object.
- `Exposure` (GH_Exposure, get) — Gets the exposure of this object in the Graphical User Interface. The default is to expose everywhere.
- `HasCategory` (Boolean, get) — Gets whether or not the Category field has been set.
- `HasSubCategory` (Boolean, get) — Gets whether or not the SubCategory field has been set.
- `HelpDescription` (String, get) — Override this method if you want the help topic content to be more than the Component description.
- `Hidden` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets the hidden flag for this component. Does not affect Hidden flags on parameters associated with this component.
- `Icon` (Bitmap, get) — Override this function to supply a custom icon (24x24 pixels). The result of this property is cached, so don't worry if icon retrieval is not very fast.
- `Icon_24x24` (Bitmap, get) — The icon associated with this object.
- `Icon_24x24_Locked` (Bitmap, get) — The greyscale icon of this object.
- `IconCapableUI` (Boolean, get) — By default the NickName menu item supports the Icon Mode override toggle. If your UI is not capable of displaying icons, then override this property and return False.
- `IconDisplayMode` (GH_IconDisplayMode, get/set) — Gets the current display mode of the object.
- `InConstructor` (Boolean, get) — Gets the constructor flag for this instance. If InConstructor = True, it means the component is still registering parameters and the layout is not yet complete.
- `InPreSolve` (Boolean, get/set) — Is set to True by the framework right before a "use tasks" pass is made on SolveIntance calls. When True, SolveInstance should be using Tasks to start calculations on input data. When False, SolveInstance should be setting the output data by either using the result of a task or directly computing the results.
- `InstanceDescription` (String, get) — (Overrides GH_Component.InstanceDescription.)
- `InstanceGuid` (Guid, get) — Gets the ID of this runtime instance.
- `IsBakeCapable` (Boolean, get) — (Inherited from GH_Component.)
- `IsDataProvider` (Boolean, get) — Components are never Data providers. Only the output parameters can be considered to be providers.
- `IsPreviewCapable` (Boolean, get) — If a single parameter is PreviewCapable, so is the component. Override this property if you need special Preview flags.
- `IsValidPrincipalParameterIndex` (Boolean, get) — Gets whether the MasterParameterIndex property identifies a valid parameter.
- `Keywords` (IEnumerable<String>, get) — Gets a list of additional keywords that describe the object. Typically this list is empty but you can override this property to aid in object searches.
- `Locked` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets the Locked state for this Component. This also sets locked states for all input and output parameters.
- `Message` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets a custom message to be displayed underneath the component. This message is not serialized and should be assigned on every solution anew.
- `MutableNickName` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value that enables Nick name changes through the menu. The default is TRUE.
- `Name` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the name of the object. This field typically remains fixed during the lifetime of an object.
- `NickName` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the nickname of the object. This field can be changed by the user.
- `Obsolete` (Boolean, get) — Gets whether this object is obsolete. Default implementation returns true if the class name contains the string "OBSOLETE" or if this class has been decorated with the ObsoleteAttribute. You are free to override this if you want, but I suggest adding the ObsoleteAttribute instead.
- `Params` (GH_ComponentParamServer, get) — Gets the parameter manager object for this component.
- `Phase` (GH_SolutionPhase, get/set) — Gets or sets the solution phase this object is currenly in.
- `PrincipalParameterIndex` (Int32, get/set) — Gets or sets the principal parameter index override. Negative indices or indices larger than or equal to the input parameter count will be ignored and regular principal parameter detection will kick in.
- `ProcessorTime` (TimeSpan, get) — Gets the total processor time (profiler) spend by this component and all input parameters.
- `RunCount` (Int32, get) — Gets a value indicating how many times the SolveInstance method was called on this component during the last solution. This property will return -1 if no valid runcount is available.
- `RuntimeMessageLevel` (GH_RuntimeMessageLevel, get) — Returns the worst case runtime warning level of me and all my parameters
- `SubCategory` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the SubCategory in which this object belongs. If HasSubCategory() returns false, this field has no meaning.
- `TaskList` (List<Task<T>>, get) — 
- `UseTasks` (Boolean, get/set) — Returns True if the component is set to support tasks. Components can have this feature turned on/off. Note that even if UseTasks returns True, there is a chance that a multipass task based solution may not be invoked depending on if multiple calls need to be made during enumeration. You must pay attention to the value of InPreSolve during SolveInstance. The set version of this property can be called by the framework if we want to provide a technique to globally turn on/off task support for all components in a definition.
- `m_attributes` (IGH_Attributes, get) — (Inherited from GH_DocumentObject.)

### Events

## GH_TimeKind (enum)

Enumerates the possible kinds that a System.DateTime value can imply.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_TimeKind.htm)

### Values
- `Unset` = `0` — Unset kind.
- `Mixed` = `1` — Mixed indicates there isn't a single clear answer.
- `PureTime` = `2` — DateTime value implies a pure time (no date part).
- `PureDate` = `3` — DateTime value implies a pute date (no time part).
- `DateAndTime` = `4` — DateTime value implies both a date and a time component.

## GH_TypeLib (class)

Utility module that caches a number of oft-used types and type-ids for quick access. Do not change any of the values in this module or you'll wreck casting and conversion logic.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_TypeLib.htm)

## GH_UndoOperation (enum)

Enumerates all possible types of undo operation.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_UndoOperation.htm)

### Values
- `ClearUndoStack` = `0` — The stack with all undo records has been cleared.
- `ClearRedoStack` = `1` — The stack with all redo records has been cleared. This always happens when a new undo record is added.
- `RecordAdded` = `2` — A new undo record is added to the list. There will be a ClearRedoStack event after this one.
- `RecordRemoved` = `3` — A specific record has been deleted. This is not a typical operation and is usually due to user intervention. Removal can occur from both the undo and the redo stack.
- `Undo` = `4` — An undo operation was successfully completed. This means a record has been moved from the undo stack to the redo stack.
- `Redo` = `5` — A redo operation was successfully completed. This means a record has been moved from the redo stack back onto the undo stack. This does not count as a RecordAdded operation.

## GH_UpgradeUtil (class)

Exposes several utility functions for migrating parameters and wires between components useful while upgrading components.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_UpgradeUtil.htm)

### Constructors
- `public GH_UpgradeUtil()` — Initializes a new instance of the GH_UpgradeUtil class

### Methods
#### `public static bool MigrateInputParameters(IGH_Component source, IGH_Component target)`

Migrate all input parameters.

**Parameters:**
- `source` (Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_Component) — Source component.
- `target` (Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_Component) — Target component.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_UpgradeUtil_MigrateInputParameters.htm)

#### `public static bool MigrateInputParameters(IGH_Component source, IGH_Component target, int[] index0, int[] index1)`

Migrate a bunch of input parameters.

**Parameters:**
- `source` (Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_Component) — Source component.
- `target` (Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_Component) — Target component.
- `index0` (System.Int32[]) — List of input indices to migrate.
- `index1` (System.Int32[]) — Matching list of target indices.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_UpgradeUtil_MigrateInputParameters_1.htm)

#### `public static bool MigrateOutputParameters(IGH_Component source, IGH_Component target)`

Migrate all output parameters.

**Parameters:**
- `source` (Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_Component) — Source component.
- `target` (Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_Component) — Target component.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_UpgradeUtil_MigrateOutputParameters.htm)

#### `public static bool MigrateOutputParameters(IGH_Component source, IGH_Component target, int[] index0, int[] index1)`

Migrate a bunch of output parameters.

**Parameters:**
- `source` (Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_Component) — Source component.
- `target` (Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_Component) — Target component.
- `index0` (System.Int32[]) — List of output indices to migrate.
- `index1` (System.Int32[]) — Matching list of target indices.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_UpgradeUtil_MigrateOutputParameters_1.htm)

#### `public static int MigrateRecipients(IEnumerable<IGH_Param> recipients, IGH_Param target)`

Utility method for bulk assigning a bunch of sources.

**Parameters:**
- `recipients` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<IGH_Param>)
- `target` (Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_Param)

**Returns:** `Int32` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_UpgradeUtil_MigrateRecipients_1.htm)

#### `public static int MigrateRecipients(IGH_Param from, IGH_Param to)`

Migrate all recipients from A to B, while maintaining order.

**Parameters:**
- `from` (Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_Param) — Parameter to move recipients from.
- `to` (Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_Param) — Parameter to move recipients to.

**Returns:** `Int32` — Number of recipients migrated.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_UpgradeUtil_MigrateRecipients.htm)

#### `public static int MigrateSources(IEnumerable<IGH_Param> sources, IGH_Param target)`

Utility method for bulk assigning a bunch of sources.

**Parameters:**
- `sources` (System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<IGH_Param>)
- `target` (Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_Param)

**Returns:** `Int32` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_UpgradeUtil_MigrateSources_1.htm)

#### `public static int MigrateSources(IGH_Param from, IGH_Param to)`

Migrate all sources from A to B, while maintaining order.

**Parameters:**
- `from` (Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_Param) — Parameter to move sources from.
- `to` (Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_Param) — Parameter to move sources to.

**Returns:** `Int32` — Number of sources migrated.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_UpgradeUtil_MigrateSources.htm)

#### `public static void ReplaceInputParameters(IGH_Component source, IGH_Component target)`

Move all input parameters from one component to another. Only use this function if the target component can handle the input layout of the source component.

**Parameters:**
- `source` (Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_Component) — Component to move parameters from.
- `target` (Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_Component) — Component to move parameters to.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_UpgradeUtil_ReplaceInputParameters.htm)

#### `public static void ReplaceOutputParameters(IGH_Component source, IGH_Component target)`

Move all output parameters from one component to another. Only use this function if the target component can handle the output layout of the source component.

**Parameters:**
- `source` (Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_Component) — Component to move parameters from.
- `target` (Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_Component) — Component to move parameters to.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_UpgradeUtil_ReplaceOutputParameters.htm)

#### `public static IGH_Component SwapComponents(IGH_Component oldComponent, Guid newComponentGuid)`

Replace an existing component with a different one. All parameters (input and output) will be migrated.

**Parameters:**
- `oldComponent` (Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_Component) — Component current in a GH_Document.
- `newComponentGuid` (System.Guid) — ComponentGuid of the component to add.

**Returns:** `IGH_Component` — Component that was added.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_UpgradeUtil_SwapComponents_2.htm)

#### `public static IGH_Component SwapComponents(IGH_Component oldComponent, Guid newComponentGuid, bool migrateParameters)`

Replace an existing component with a different one. All parameters (input and output) will be migrated.

**Parameters:**
- `oldComponent` (Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_Component) — Component current in a GH_Document.
- `newComponentGuid` (System.Guid) — ComponentGuid of the component to add.
- `migrateParameters` (System.Boolean) — If true, all parameters will be moved from old to new.

**Returns:** `IGH_Component` — Component that was added.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_UpgradeUtil_SwapComponents_3.htm)

#### `public static bool SwapComponents(IGH_Component oldComponent, IGH_Component newComponent)`

Replace an existing component with a different one. All parameters (input and output) will be migrated.

**Parameters:**
- `oldComponent` (Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_Component) — Component current in a GH_Document.
- `newComponent` (Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_Component) — Component to swap.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_UpgradeUtil_SwapComponents.htm)

#### `public static bool SwapComponents(IGH_Component oldComponent, IGH_Component newComponent, bool migrateParameters)`

Replace an existing component with a different one. All parameters (input and output) will be migrated.

**Parameters:**
- `oldComponent` (Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_Component) — Component current in a GH_Document.
- `newComponent` (Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_Component) — Component to swap.
- `migrateParameters` (System.Boolean) — If true, all parameters will be moved from old to new.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_UpgradeUtil_SwapComponents_1.htm)

## GH_UserObject (class)

Represents a custom user object. A User Object is an existing IGH_DocumentObject with overridden icon, instance description and exposure.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_UserObject.htm)

### Constructors
- `public GH_UserObject()` — Empty constructor
- `public GH_UserObject(string source_file)` — Create a new user object from an existing *.ghuser file.

### Methods
#### `public void Clear()`

Destroy all data except the file path and the ID.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_UserObject_Clear.htm)

#### `public void CreateDefaultPath(bool avoid_conflict)`

Set the Path field for this user object to the default. The default file name is derived from the Description.Name field. This method also ensures the UserObject directory exists.

**Parameters:**
- `avoid_conflict` (System.Boolean) — If true, a unique file path is assigned.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_UserObject_CreateDefaultPath.htm)

#### `public IGH_DocumentObject InstantiateObject()`

Instantiate a new IGH_DocumentObject from this User Object.

**Returns:** `IGH_DocumentObject` — The IGH_DocumentObject instance defined inside the UserObject file or null on error.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_UserObject_InstantiateObject.htm)

#### `public void ReadFromFile()`

Force an update of the local data from the file. The Path field must be set for this to work.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_UserObject_ReadFromFile.htm)

#### `public bool SaveToFile()`

Save the current User Object state to a file. Any existing file will be overwritten.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_UserObject_SaveToFile.htm)

#### `public void SetDataFromObject(IGH_DocumentObject obj)`

Set the Data field content of ths UserObject from a Document object instance.

**Parameters:**
- `obj` (Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_DocumentObject) — Object to assign

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_UserObject_SetDataFromObject.htm)

### Properties
- `BaseGuid` (Guid, get/set) — Gets or sets the ComponentID of the IGH_DocumentObject that functions as the base class for this User Object.
- `Data` (Byte[], get/set) — Gets or sets the serialized data. This data is used to deserialize a new instance of the base class.
- `Description` (GH_InstanceDescription, get/set) — Gets or sets the Instance Description override for this User Object.
- `Exposure` (GH_Exposure, get/set) — Gets or sets the Exposure of this User Object.
- `Guid` (Guid, get/set) — Gets or sets the Guid of this User Object. The Guid is created anew during construction and is not stored in the User Object File. You should never have to set this field.
- `Icon` (Bitmap, get/set) — Gets or sets the icon associated with this User Object. If the icon hasn't been overridden, then a Default icon is returned.
- `Path` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the file path this User Object is loaded from or saved to.

## GH_ValueProxy<T> (class)

Represents the abstract base implementation of the IGH_ValueProxy interface.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ValueProxy_1.htm)

### Constructors
- `protected GH_ValueProxy(T nValue, string nProxyName, bool bIsStringProxy)` — Default constructor for the base class. All derived classes must call this constructor.

### Methods
#### `public IGH_ValueProxy Duplicate()`



**Returns:** `IGH_ValueProxy` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ValueProxy_1_Duplicate.htm)

#### `public abstract GH_ValueProxy<T> Duplicate_T()`



**Returns:** `GH_ValueProxy<T>` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ValueProxy_1_Duplicate_T.htm)

#### `public virtual bool FromString(string in)`

Performs the String->Value parsing. The default implementation always returns False.

**Remarks:** The result of the parse is stored in the local value pointer. If this function returns False there is no guarantee the local value is still valid.

**Parameters:**
- `in` (System.String) — The String to parse

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True in cae of success, False in case of failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ValueProxy_1_FromString.htm)

**Parameters:**
- `in` (System.String)

**Returns:** `String` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ValueProxy_1_MutateString.htm)

#### `public override string ToString()`

Performs a default ToString operation on the local value. If the value is Nothing, an empty String is returned.

**Remarks:** Implementors: override this function if you need custom formatting logic.

**Returns:** `String` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_GH_ValueProxy_1_ToString.htm)

### Properties

- `m_IsStringProxy` (Boolean, get) — 
- `m_Name` (String, get) — 
- `m_UserString` (String, get) — 
- `m_Valid` (Boolean, get) — 
- `m_Value` (T, get) —

## IGH_ActiveObject (interface)

Base interface for all objects on the canvas that participate in solutions

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_ActiveObject.htm)

### Methods
#### `void AddRuntimeMessage(GH_RuntimeMessageLevel Type, string Message)`

Add a new message to this object. Valid message type flags are Warning and Error. If the Message string is empty or zero-length no message is added.

**Parameters:**
- `Type` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_RuntimeMessageLevel) — Type of message. Only Warnings and Errors are recorded.
- `Message` (System.String) — Content of message.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_ActiveObject_AddRuntimeMessage.htm)

#### `void AddedToDocument(GH_Document document)`

This method will be called when an object is added to a document.

**Parameters:**
- `document` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_Document) — Document that now owns this object.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DocumentObject_AddedToDocument.htm)

#### `bool AppendMenuItems(ToolStripDropDown menu)`

This function is called when a context menu is about to be displayed. Override it to set custom items.

**Parameters:**
- `menu` (System.Windows.Forms.ToolStripDropDown) — Menu object to populate.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DocumentObject_AppendMenuItems.htm)

#### `void ClearData()`

This function is called whenever the object needs to clear all solution data. This usually amounts to wiping volatile caches and messages. This function will not affect any other objects, but it will set the Phase flag to Blank

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_ActiveObject_ClearData.htm)

#### `void ClearRuntimeMessages()`

Clear all message lists.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_ActiveObject_ClearRuntimeMessages.htm)

#### `void CollectData()`

This function is called whenever the object is required to collect all data. Either from Persistent records, from source params or whatever. This step is only performed if the phase flag is Blank or Failed. Upon completion, the phase flag will be set to Collected

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_ActiveObject_CollectData.htm)

#### `void ComputeData()`

This function is called whenever the object is required to generate new data. This step is only performed by some objects and only when the Phase flag is Collected. Upon completion, the Phase will be Computed. If this object throws exceptions, it is the responsibility of the caller to set the Phase flag to Failed.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_ActiveObject_ComputeData.htm)

#### `void CreateAttributes()`

Create new default attributes for this object. You should only call this function when the Attributes() field is null.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DocumentObject_CreateAttributes.htm)

#### `bool DependsOn(IGH_ActiveObject PotentialSource)`

Solve the inheritance relationship between this object and a potential parental object.

**Parameters:**
- `PotentialSource` (Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_ActiveObject)

**Returns:** `Boolean` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_ActiveObject_DependsOn.htm)

#### `void DocumentContextChanged(GH_Document document, GH_DocumentContext context)`

This method will be called when the document that owns this object moves into a different context.

**Parameters:**
- `document` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_Document) — Document that owns this object.
- `context` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_DocumentContext) — The reason for this event.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DocumentObject_DocumentContextChanged.htm)

#### `void ExpirePreview(bool redraw)`

Call this function when you suspect that the preview has expired for this object. This will cause the display cache to be eradicated.

**Parameters:**
- `redraw` (System.Boolean)

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DocumentObject_ExpirePreview.htm)

#### `void ExpireSolution(bool recompute)`

Call this function whenever you do something which expires the current solution. This will make sure all caches are erased, all downstream objects are expired and that the event is raised. The default implementation merely places a call to OnSolutionExpired(), override this function in derived classes to make sure you clear local data caches and expire downstream objects.

**Parameters:**
- `recompute` (System.Boolean) — If True, the solution will be recomputed straight away.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DocumentObject_ExpireSolution.htm)

#### `void IsolateObject()`

Destroy all connections to other objects.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DocumentObject_IsolateObject.htm)

#### `void MovedBetweenDocuments(GH_Document oldDocument, GH_Document newDocument)`

This method will be called when an object is moved from one document to another.

**Parameters:**
- `oldDocument` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_Document) — Document that used to own this object.
- `newDocument` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_Document) — Document that now owns ths object.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DocumentObject_MovedBetweenDocuments.htm)

#### `void OnAttributesChanged()`

Raises the AttributesChanged event on the toplevel object.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DocumentObject_OnAttributesChanged.htm)

#### `void OnDisplayExpired(bool redraw)`

Raises the DisplayExpired event on the toplevel object.

**Parameters:**
- `redraw` (System.Boolean) — If True, the canvas will be immediately redrawn.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DocumentObject_OnDisplayExpired.htm)

#### `void OnObjectChanged(GH_ObjectChangedEventArgs e)`

Raises the ObjectChanged event on the current (!not the top level!) object.

**Parameters:**
- `e` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_ObjectChangedEventArgs) — Fully constructed event arguments.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DocumentObject_OnObjectChanged.htm)

- `eventType` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_ObjectEventType) — Type of change.

**Parameters:**
- `customEvent` (System.String) — Custom change type name.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DocumentObject_OnObjectChanged_3.htm)

#### `void OnObjectChanged(string customEvent, Object tag)`

Raises the ObjectChanged event on the current (!not the top level!) object.

- `customEvent` (System.String) — Custom change type name.
- `tag` (System.Object) — Tag to apply to the event arguments.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DocumentObject_OnObjectChanged_4.htm)

#### `GH_Document OnPingDocument()`

Raise the PingDocument Event on the toplevel object and try to find the document which owns this object.

**Returns:** `GH_Document` — The document which owns this object if successful, or nothing if no document owns this object.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DocumentObject_OnPingDocument.htm)

Raises the PreviewExpired event on the toplevel object.

**Parameters:**
- `redraw` (System.Boolean) — If True, the viewports will be immediately redrawn.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DocumentObject_OnPreviewExpired.htm)

Raises the SolutionExpired event on the toplevel object.

**Parameters:**
- `recompute` (System.Boolean) — If True, the solution will be immediately recalculated.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DocumentObject_OnSolutionExpired.htm)

Record an entire undo record.

Test whether this object is compliant with a given Rhino version.

- `exeServiceRelease` (System.Int32) — Rhino minor (service) release.
