#### `void TriggerAutoSave()`

Triggers the AutoSave function on the owner document with the object_changed flag.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DocumentObject_TriggerAutoSave.htm)

#### `void TriggerAutoSave(GH_AutoSaveTrigger trigger)`

Triggers the AutoSave function on the owner document with a custom flag.

**Parameters:**
- `trigger` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_AutoSaveTrigger) — Reason for the autosave operation. It is possible that a user has decided to avoid autosave events for specific types, so if you can, try and provide a correct trigger flag.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DocumentObject_TriggerAutoSave_1.htm)

#### `void TriggerAutoSave(GH_AutoSaveTrigger trigger, Guid id)`

Triggers the AutoSave function on the owner document with a custom flag.

**Parameters:**
- `trigger` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_AutoSaveTrigger) — Reason for the autosave operation. It is possible that a user has decided to avoid autosave events for specific types, so if you can, try and provide a correct trigger flag.
- `id` (System.Guid) — ID of autosave event. Consecutive autosave requests with the same ID will be ignored.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DocumentObject_TriggerAutoSave_2.htm)

#### `void TriggerAutoSave(Guid id)`

Triggers the AutoSave function on the owner document with the object_changed flag.

**Parameters:**
- `id` (System.Guid) — ID of autosave event. Consecutive autosave requests with the same ID will be ignored.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DocumentObject_TriggerAutoSave_3.htm)

### Properties
- `Attributes` (IGH_Attributes, get/set) — Gets or sets the attributes that are associated with this object. Only set custom attributes if you know what you are doing.
- `ComponentGuid` (Guid, get) — Returns a consistent ID for this object type. Every object must supply a unique and unchanging ID that is used to identify objects of the same type.
- `Exposure` (GH_Exposure, get) — Gets the exposure of this object in the Graphical User Interface.
- `Icon_24x24` (Bitmap, get) — The icon associated with this object.
- `Icon_24x24_Locked` (Bitmap, get) — The greyscale icon of this object.
- `IconDisplayMode` (GH_IconDisplayMode, get/set) — Gets the current display mode of the object.
- `IsDataProvider` (Boolean, get) — Gets whether or not this object is capable of emitting data.
- `Locked` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets the locked flag of this object. Locked objects are ignored during solutions.
- `MutableNickName` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value that enables Nick name changes through the menu. The default is TRUE.
- `Obsolete` (Boolean, get) — Gets whether this object is Obsolete.
- `Phase` (GH_SolutionPhase, get/set) — Gets the solution phase in which this object is currently stuck.
- `ProcessorTime` (TimeSpan, get) — Gets the most recent measured processor time.
- `RuntimeMessageLevel` (GH_RuntimeMessageLevel, get) — Gets the worst-case level of all messages. I.e. if only warnings have been recorded, the level will be ::warning. If even a single error exists, the level will be ::error.

### Events
#### `AttributesChanged` (`Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_DocumentObject.AttributesChangedEventHandler`)

**Signature:** `event IGH_DocumentObject.AttributesChangedEventHandler AttributesChanged`

Raised whenever the number or kind of attributes changes. This event is handled by GH_Documents who subsequently wipe their attribute caches.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/E_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DocumentObject_AttributesChanged.htm)

#### `DisplayExpired` (`Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_DocumentObject.DisplayExpiredEventHandler`)

**Signature:** `event IGH_DocumentObject.DisplayExpiredEventHandler DisplayExpired`

Raised whenever the display (on the Canvas) of a certain object becomes invalid.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/E_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DocumentObject_DisplayExpired.htm)

#### `ObjectChanged` (`Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_DocumentObject.ObjectChangedEventHandler`)

**Signature:** `event IGH_DocumentObject.ObjectChangedEventHandler ObjectChanged`

Raised whenever some property of the object changes that would affect remote instances.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/E_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DocumentObject_ObjectChanged.htm)

#### `PingDocument` (`Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_DocumentObject.PingDocumentEventHandler`)

**Signature:** `event IGH_DocumentObject.PingDocumentEventHandler PingDocument`

Raised whenever an object needs to know which GH_Document it belongs to.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/E_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DocumentObject_PingDocument.htm)

#### `PreviewExpired` (`Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_DocumentObject.PreviewExpiredEventHandler`)

**Signature:** `event IGH_DocumentObject.PreviewExpiredEventHandler PreviewExpired`

Raised whenever the display (in the Rhino viewports) of a certain object becomes invalid.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/E_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DocumentObject_PreviewExpired.htm)

#### `SolutionExpired` (`Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_DocumentObject.SolutionExpiredEventHandler`)

**Signature:** `event IGH_DocumentObject.SolutionExpiredEventHandler SolutionExpired`

Raised whenever the solution of a certain object becomes invalid.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/E_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DocumentObject_SolutionExpired.htm)

## IGH_Attributes (interface)

Base interface for all Attributes. Attributes are the visual portion of GH_DocumentObjects. They handle display, mouse events, layout, context menus etc. etc. You should consider inheriting from GH_Attributes or some other abstract class instead of implementing this interface from scratch.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_Attributes.htm)

### Methods
#### `void AppendToAttributeTree(List<IGH_Attributes> attributes)`

Recursively append these attributes and all child attributes to the attribute list.

**Parameters:**
- `attributes` (System.Collections.Generic.List<IGH_Attributes>) — List to append to.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_Attributes_AppendToAttributeTree.htm)

#### `void ExpireLayout()`

Expires the entire layout of the attributes. When overridden, implementer must place a call to the base class ExpireLayout().

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_Attributes_ExpireLayout.htm)

#### `bool InvalidateCanvas(GH_Canvas canvas, GH_CanvasMouseEvent e)`

If the mouse location should cause a canvas invalidation then return true. You only need to override this function if you draw objects that are dependant on cursor positions outside the bounds of the attributes.

**Parameters:**
- `canvas` (Grasshopper.GUI.Canvas.GH_Canvas) — Canvas to potentially invalidate.
- `e` (Grasshopper.GUI.GH_CanvasMouseEvent) — Event arguments for canvas mousemove.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the canvas ought to be invalidated.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_Attributes_InvalidateCanvas.htm)

#### `bool IsMenuRegion(PointF point)`

Determines whether a point is available for context menu popups. By default, IsMenuRegion calls IsPickRegion(PointF).

**Parameters:**
- `point` (System.Drawing.PointF) — Point location to test.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_Attributes_IsMenuRegion.htm)

#### `bool IsPickRegion(PointF point)`

Determines whether a point is within the pickable region for this object. By default, any point inside the Bounds is treated as pickable.

**Parameters:**
- `point` (System.Drawing.PointF) — Point location to test.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_Attributes_IsPickRegion.htm)

#### `bool IsPickRegion(RectangleF box, GH_PickBox method)`

Determines whether a rectangle is a valid pick region for this object. By default, the picking rectangle is intersected with the Bounds rectangle.

**Parameters:**
- `box` (System.Drawing.RectangleF) — Selection box.
- `method` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_PickBox) — Selection method.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_Attributes_IsPickRegion_1.htm)

#### `bool IsTooltipRegion(PointF canvasPoint)`

Determine whether the specified pixel should result in a tooltip when the cursor hovers over it.

**Parameters:**
- `canvasPoint` (System.Drawing.PointF) — Point in Canvas coordinates.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_GUI_Canvas_IGH_TooltipAwareObject_IsTooltipRegion.htm)

#### `void NewInstanceGuid()`

Generate a new instance GUID for the owner object.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_Attributes_NewInstanceGuid.htm)

#### `void NewInstanceGuid(Guid newID)`

Generate a new instance GUID for the owner object. Do not use this overload unless you're > 1.95m and called David.

**Parameters:**
- `newID` (System.Guid) — New ID to assign.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_Attributes_NewInstanceGuid_1.htm)

#### `void PerformLayout()`

Recompute the layout for these attributes. This function is automatically called during Drawing operations, so you typically don't have to.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_Attributes_PerformLayout.htm)

#### `bool Read(GH_IReader reader)`

This method is called whenever the instance is required to deserialize itself.

**Parameters:**
- `reader` (GH_IO.Serialization.GH_IReader) — Reader object to deserialize from.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_GH_IO_GH_ISerializable_Read.htm)

#### `void RenderToCanvas(GH_Canvas canvas, GH_CanvasChannel channel)`

Render these attributes into a Canvas control.

**Parameters:**
- `canvas` (Grasshopper.GUI.Canvas.GH_Canvas) — Canvas to draw on.
- `channel` (Grasshopper.GUI.Canvas.GH_CanvasChannel) — Current drawing channel.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_Attributes_RenderToCanvas.htm)

#### `GH_ObjectResponse RespondToKeyDown(GH_Canvas sender, KeyEventArgs e)`

(Inherited from IGH_ResponsiveObject.)

**Parameters:**
- `sender` (Grasshopper.GUI.Canvas.GH_Canvas)
- `e` (System.Windows.Forms.KeyEventArgs)

**Returns:** `GH_ObjectResponse` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_GUI_Canvas_IGH_ResponsiveObject_RespondToKeyDown.htm)

#### `GH_ObjectResponse RespondToKeyUp(GH_Canvas sender, KeyEventArgs e)`

(Inherited from IGH_ResponsiveObject.)

**Parameters:**
- `sender` (Grasshopper.GUI.Canvas.GH_Canvas)
- `e` (System.Windows.Forms.KeyEventArgs)

**Returns:** `GH_ObjectResponse` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_GUI_Canvas_IGH_ResponsiveObject_RespondToKeyUp.htm)

#### `GH_ObjectResponse RespondToMouseDoubleClick(GH_Canvas sender, GH_CanvasMouseEvent e)`

This function will be called whenever the left button is double-clicked over the canvas. If you are active, you will be the only object who gets called.

**Parameters:**
- `sender` (Grasshopper.GUI.Canvas.GH_Canvas) — Base canvas.
- `e` (Grasshopper.GUI.GH_CanvasMouseEvent) — Mouse event arguments.

**Returns:** `GH_ObjectResponse` — All Response flags are valid.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_GUI_Canvas_IGH_ResponsiveObject_RespondToMouseDoubleClick.htm)

#### `GH_ObjectResponse RespondToMouseDown(GH_Canvas sender, GH_CanvasMouseEvent e)`

This function will be called whenever a mouse button is pressed over the canvas. If you are active, you will be the only object who gets called. If you are inactive, you might get called if nobody on top of you decides to become active.

**Parameters:**
- `sender` (Grasshopper.GUI.Canvas.GH_Canvas) — Base canvas.
- `e` (Grasshopper.GUI.GH_CanvasMouseEvent) — Mouse event arguments.

**Returns:** `GH_ObjectResponse` — All Response flags are valid.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_GUI_Canvas_IGH_ResponsiveObject_RespondToMouseDown.htm)

#### `GH_ObjectResponse RespondToMouseMove(GH_Canvas sender, GH_CanvasMouseEvent e)`

This function will be called when the mouse moves across the canvas. If you are active, you will be the only object who gets called. If you are inactive, you might get called if nobody on top of you decides to become active.

**Parameters:**
- `sender` (Grasshopper.GUI.Canvas.GH_Canvas) — Base canvas.
- `e` (Grasshopper.GUI.GH_CanvasMouseEvent) — Mouse event arguments.

**Returns:** `GH_ObjectResponse` — All Response flags are valid.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_GUI_Canvas_IGH_ResponsiveObject_RespondToMouseMove.htm)

#### `GH_ObjectResponse RespondToMouseUp(GH_Canvas sender, GH_CanvasMouseEvent e)`

This function will be called whenever a mouse button is released over the canvas. If you are active, you will be the only object who gets called. If you are inactive, you will not be called at all for MouseUp events.

**Parameters:**
- `sender` (Grasshopper.GUI.Canvas.GH_Canvas) — Base canvas.
- `e` (Grasshopper.GUI.GH_CanvasMouseEvent) — Mouse event arguments.

**Returns:** `GH_ObjectResponse` — All Response flags are valid.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_GUI_Canvas_IGH_ResponsiveObject_RespondToMouseUp.htm)

#### `void SetupTooltip(PointF canvasPoint, GH_TooltipDisplayEventArgs e)`

This function is called when a tooltip it about to be displayed.

**Parameters:**
- `canvasPoint` (System.Drawing.PointF) — The pixel coordinate on the canvas of the cursor.
- `e` (Grasshopper.GUI.GH_TooltipDisplayEventArgs) — Tooltip display arguments.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_GUI_Canvas_IGH_TooltipAwareObject_SetupTooltip.htm)

#### `bool Write(GH_IWriter writer)`

This method is called whenever the instance is required to serialize itself.

**Parameters:**
- `writer` (GH_IO.Serialization.GH_IWriter) — Writer object to serialize with.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_GH_IO_GH_ISerializable_Write.htm)

### Properties
- `AllowMessageBalloon` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether these attributes allow warning and error boxes to be drawn on top of them.
- `Bounds` (RectangleF, get/set) — Gets the rectangle that contains the active content of the attributes. Typically the Contents determine the active area for menus, tooltips etc. Attributes are not supposed to draw objects beyond the Bounds.
- `DocObject` (IGH_DocumentObject, get) — Gets the owner object of these attributes.
- `GetTopLevel` (IGH_Attributes, get) — Gets the top level attributes of these attributes.
- `HasInputGrip` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether or not these attributes have an input grip.
- `HasOutputGrip` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether or not these attributes have an output grip.
- `InputGrip` (PointF, get) — Gets the input grip location for these attributes. If HasInputGrip equals False, this point is meaningless.
- `InstanceGuid` (Guid, get) — Gets the instance ID for these attributes.
- `IsTopLevel` (Boolean, get) — Gets whether these attributes are top_level (i.e. no Parent attributes)
- `OutputGrip` (PointF, get) — Gets the output grip location for these attributes. If HasOutputGrip equals False, this point is meaningless.
- `Parent` (IGH_Attributes, get/set) — Gets or sets the parent attributes. Top level attributes do not have parents.
- `PathName` (String, get) — Get a description of the location of these attributes within the local attribute tree.
- `Pivot` (PointF, get/set) — Gets the pivot for these attributes. The pivot controls the general placement of the attributes. If you want to move the attributes, change the pivot location.
- `Selected` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets the selected state of the top-level attributes.
- `TooltipEnabled` (Boolean, get) — Gets the tooltip enabled value. If False, no further tooltip functions will be called.

## IGH_Author (interface)

Provides of collection of basic author details.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_Author.htm)

### Properties
- `Address` (String, get) — Gets the address of the author.
- `Company` (String, get) — Gets the Company name of the author.
- `Copyright` (String, get) — Gets the copyright message for these details.
- `Email` (String, get) — Gets the email of the author.
- `IsEmpty` (Boolean, get) — Gets whether any data has been assigned to this object.
- `Name` (String, get) — Gets the name of the author.
- `Phone` (String, get) — Gets the phone number of the author.
- `Website` (String, get) — Gets the website of the author.

## IGH_BakeAwareData (interface)

Implement this interface in your IGH_Goo data type if you want to be included in baking operations.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_BakeAwareData.htm)

### Methods
#### `bool BakeGeometry(RhinoDoc doc, ObjectAttributes att, out Guid obj_guid)`

Bake an object in the given Rhino document.

**Parameters:**
- `doc` (RhinoDoc) — Document to bake into.
- `att` (ObjectAttributes) — Attributes to bake with (should not be null).
- `obj_guid` (System.Guid) — The id of the baked object.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success. If False, obj_guid and obj_inst are not guaranteed to be valid pointers.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_BakeAwareData_BakeGeometry.htm)

## IGH_BakeAwareObject (interface)

Implement this interface on your IGH_ActiveObject if you want to participate in the Bake GUI.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_BakeAwareObject.htm)

### Methods
#### `void BakeGeometry(RhinoDoc doc, List<Guid> obj_ids)`

Bake all the goemetry in this object in the given Rhino document.

**Parameters:**
- `doc` (RhinoDoc) — Document to bake into.
- `obj_ids` (System.Collections.Generic.List<Guid>) — All bake objects IDs will be appended to this list.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_BakeAwareObject_BakeGeometry_1.htm)

#### `void BakeGeometry(RhinoDoc doc, ObjectAttributes att, List<Guid> obj_ids)`

Bake all the goemetry in this object in the given Rhino document.

**Parameters:**
- `doc` (RhinoDoc) — Document to bake into.
- `att` (ObjectAttributes) — Attributes to use for baked geometry.
- `obj_ids` (System.Collections.Generic.List<Guid>) — All bake objects IDs will be appended to this list.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_BakeAwareObject_BakeGeometry.htm)

### Properties
- `IsBakeCapable` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether or not the object can potentially Bake data in its current state.

## IGH_Component (interface)

Base interface for all GH_Component types. Do not implement this interface from scratch, inherit from GH_Component instead.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_Component.htm)

### Methods
#### `void AddRuntimeMessage(GH_RuntimeMessageLevel Type, string Message)`

Add a new message to this object. Valid message type flags are Warning and Error. If the Message string is empty or zero-length no message is added.

**Parameters:**
- `Type` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_RuntimeMessageLevel) — Type of message. Only Warnings and Errors are recorded.
- `Message` (System.String) — Content of message.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_ActiveObject_AddRuntimeMessage.htm)

#### `void BakeGeometry(RhinoDoc doc, List<Guid> obj_ids)`

Bake all the goemetry in this object in the given Rhino document.

**Parameters:**
- `doc` (RhinoDoc) — Document to bake into.
- `obj_ids` (System.Collections.Generic.List<Guid>) — All bake objects IDs will be appended to this list.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_BakeAwareObject_BakeGeometry_1.htm)

#### `void BakeGeometry(RhinoDoc doc, ObjectAttributes att, List<Guid> obj_ids)`

Bake all the goemetry in this object in the given Rhino document.

**Parameters:**
- `doc` (RhinoDoc) — Document to bake into.
- `att` (ObjectAttributes) — Attributes to use for baked geometry.
- `obj_ids` (System.Collections.Generic.List<Guid>) — All bake objects IDs will be appended to this list.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_BakeAwareObject_BakeGeometry.htm)

#### `void ClearData()`

This function is called whenever the object needs to clear all solution data. This usually amounts to wiping volatile caches and messages. This function will not affect any other objects, but it will set the Phase flag to Blank

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_ActiveObject_ClearData.htm)

#### `void ClearRuntimeMessages()`

Clear all message lists.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_ActiveObject_ClearRuntimeMessages.htm)

#### `void CollectData()`

This function is called whenever the object is required to collect all data. Either from Persistent records, from source params or whatever. This step is only performed if the phase flag is Blank or Failed. Upon completion, the phase flag will be set to Collected

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_ActiveObject_CollectData.htm)

#### `void ComputeData()`

This function is called whenever the object is required to generate new data. This step is only performed by some objects and only when the Phase flag is Collected. Upon completion, the Phase will be Computed. If this object throws exceptions, it is the responsibility of the caller to set the Phase flag to Failed.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_ActiveObject_ComputeData.htm)

#### `bool DependsOn(IGH_ActiveObject PotentialSource)`

Solve the inheritance relationship between this object and a potential parental object.

**Parameters:**
- `PotentialSource` (Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_ActiveObject)

**Returns:** `Boolean` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_ActiveObject_DependsOn.htm)

#### `void DrawViewportMeshes(IGH_PreviewArgs args)`

Implement this function to draw all shaded meshes. If the viewport does not support shading, this function will not be called.

**Parameters:**
- `args` (Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_PreviewArgs) — Draw operation arguments.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_PreviewObject_DrawViewportMeshes.htm)

#### `void DrawViewportWires(IGH_PreviewArgs args)`

Implement this function to draw all wire and point geometry previews.

**Parameters:**
- `args` (Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_PreviewArgs) — Draw operation arguments.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_PreviewObject_DrawViewportWires.htm)

#### `void RegisterRemoteIDs(GH_GuidTable id_list)`

If this object depends on Rhino Objects, you must register the UUIDs of those objects. Failure to do so will result in faulty event handling.

**Parameters:**
- `id_list` (GH_GuidTable) — The object ID database Grasshopper is responsive to.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_ActiveObject_RegisterRemoteIDs.htm)

#### `IList<string> RuntimeMessages(GH_RuntimeMessageLevel level)`

Gets the list of cached runtime messages that were recorded during solver-time processes.

**Parameters:**
- `level` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_RuntimeMessageLevel) — Runtime message type to retrieve.

**Returns:** `IList<String>` — A list of runtime message strings.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_ActiveObject_RuntimeMessages.htm)

#### `bool SDKCompliancy(int exeVersion, int exeServiceRelease)`

Test whether this object is compliant with a given Rhino version.

**Parameters:**
- `exeVersion` (System.Int32) — Rhino major release (4, 5, ...).
- `exeServiceRelease` (System.Int32) — Rhino minor (service) release.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if this object is compliant with the given Rhino release.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_ActiveObject_SDKCompliancy.htm)

### Properties
- `ClippingBox` (BoundingBox, get) — Gets the clipping box for this object. The clipping box is typically the union of the boundingboxes of all the geometry that is drawn by this object.
- `Hidden` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value indicating whether or not this object is currently drawing preview flags.
- `InConstructor` (Boolean, get) — Gets the constructor flag for this instance. If InConstructor = True, it means the component is still registering parameters and the layout is not yet complete.
- `IsBakeCapable` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether or not the object can potentially Bake data in its current state.
- `IsDataProvider` (Boolean, get) — Gets whether or not this object is capable of emitting data.
- `IsPreviewCapable` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether or not this object (in its current state) can draw geometric previews at all.
- `IsValidMasterParameterIndex` (Boolean, get) — Gets whether the MasterParameterIndex property identifies a valid parameter.
- `Locked` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets the locked flag of this object. Locked objects are ignored during solutions.
- `MasterParameterIndex` (Int32, get/set) — Gets or sets the master parameter index override. Negative indices or indices larger than or equal to the input parameter count will be ignored and regular master parameter detection will kick in.
- `Message` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets a custom message to be displayed underneath the component.
- `MutableNickName` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value that enables Nick name changes through the menu. The default is TRUE.
- `Params` (GH_ComponentParamServer, get) — Gets the Params object that managed input and output parameters.
- `Phase` (GH_SolutionPhase, get/set) — Gets the solution phase in which this object is currently stuck.
- `ProcessorTime` (TimeSpan, get) — Gets the most recent measured processor time.
- `RunCount` (Int32, get) — Gets a value indicating how many times the SolveInstance method was called on this component during the last solution. This property will return -1 if no valid runcount is available.
- `RuntimeMessageLevel` (GH_RuntimeMessageLevel, get) — Gets the worst-case level of all messages. I.e. if only warnings have been recorded, the level will be ::warning. If even a single error exists, the level will be ::error.

## IGH_ContextualComponent (interface)

Base interface for contextual components. This makes accessing non-strongly-typed functionality easier.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_ContextualComponent.htm)

### Properties
- `Archive` (GH_Archive, get) — Gets instance of embedded Grasshopper GH_Archive

## IGH_ContextualParameter (interface)

Base interface for contextual parameters. This makes accessing non-strongly-typed functionality easier.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_ContextualParameter.htm)

### Methods
#### `void AssignContextualData(IEnumerable data)`

Assign a collection of values as contextual data.

**Parameters:**
- `data` (System.Collections.IEnumerable) — Data to assign. Should be of expected type.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_ContextualParameter_AssignContextualData.htm)

#### `bool AutoAssignContextualData(GH_ParameterContext context)`

Use the in-build getter UI to assign data to the parameter.

**Parameters:**
- `context` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_ParameterContext)

**Returns:** `Boolean` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_ContextualParameter_AutoAssignContextualData.htm)

### Properties
- `AtLeast` (Int32, get) — Minimum number of inputs to request
- `AtMost` (Int32, get) — Maximum number of inputs to request.
- `ContextualData` (IEnumerable<Object>, get) — Iterate over all specified contextual values.
- `Immediate` (Boolean, get) — Gets whether this parameter triggers immediate updates from inside its getter. Immediacy is important for a smooth experience, but it maybe disabled if the expected recalculation time becomes excessive.
- `Prompt` (String, get) — Gets the prompt to be displayed while asking the user to enter data. The prompt string may contain '{0}' fields which will be populated with the amount of values picked so far.

## IGH_ContextualParameter2 (interface)

Version 2 of Base interface for contextual parameters. This makes accessing non-strongly-typed functionality easier.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_ContextualParameter2.htm)

### Methods
#### `void AssignContextualData(IEnumerable data)`

Assign a collection of values as contextual data.

**Parameters:**
- `data` (System.Collections.IEnumerable) — Data to assign. Should be of expected type.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_ContextualParameter_AssignContextualData.htm)

#### `bool AutoAssignContextualData(GH_ParameterContext context)`

Use the in-build getter UI to assign data to the parameter.

**Parameters:**
- `context` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_ParameterContext)

**Returns:** `Boolean` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_ContextualParameter_AutoAssignContextualData.htm)

#### `void ClearContextualData()`

Clear previously assigned collection of values as contextual data

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_ContextualParameter2_ClearContextualData.htm)

### Properties
- `AtLeast` (Int32, get) — Minimum number of inputs to request
- `AtMost` (Int32, get) — Maximum number of inputs to request.
- `ContextualData` (IEnumerable<Object>, get) — Iterate over all specified contextual values.
- `Immediate` (Boolean, get) — Gets whether this parameter triggers immediate updates from inside its getter. Immediacy is important for a smooth experience, but it maybe disabled if the expected recalculation time becomes excessive.
- `Prompt` (String, get) — Gets the prompt to be displayed while asking the user to enter data. The prompt string may contain '{0}' fields which will be populated with the amount of values picked so far.

## IGH_DataAccess (interface)

Provides access to component parameters, in order to retrieve and assign data during solutions.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DataAccess.htm)

### Methods
#### `void AbortComponentSolution()`

Call this method if you wish to stop solving this component.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DataAccess_AbortComponentSolution.htm)

#### `bool BlitData<Q>(int paramIndex, GH_Structure<Q> tree, bool overwrite) where Q : IGH_Goo`

Creates a direct copy of an existing data structure. The existing generic instance must of the exact same type as the target parameter.

**Parameters:**
- `paramIndex` (System.Int32) — Index of output parameter.
- `tree` (Grasshopper.Kernel.Data.GH_Structure<Q>) — Parameter whose data to blit
- `overwrite` (System.Boolean) — If true, any existing data will be removed.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DataAccess_BlitData__1.htm)

#### `void DisableGapLogic()`

Call this function if you want to disable the gap null logic for the current iteration.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DataAccess_DisableGapLogic.htm)

#### `void DisableGapLogic(int paramIndex)`

Call this function if you want to disable the gap null logic for a specific output parameter.

**Parameters:**
- `paramIndex` (System.Int32) — Index of output parameter to disable.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DataAccess_DisableGapLogic_1.htm)

#### `bool GetData<T>(int index, ref T destination)`

Retrieve data from an input parameter during GH_Component.SolveInstance().

**Parameters:**
- `index` (System.Int32)
- `destination` (T)

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DataAccess_GetData__1.htm)

#### `bool GetData<T>(string name, ref T destination)`

Retrieve data from an input parameter during GH_Component.SolveInstance().

**Remarks:** Parameter access by index is faster, consider using the other overload if possible.

**Parameters:**
- `name` (System.String)
- `destination` (T)

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DataAccess_GetData__1_1.htm)

#### `bool GetDataList<T>(int index, List<T> list)`

Retrieve an entire list of data from an input parameter during GH_Component.SolveInstance().

**Parameters:**
- `index` (System.Int32)
- `list` (System.Collections.Generic.List<T>)

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DataAccess_GetDataList__1.htm)

#### `bool GetDataList<T>(string name, List<T> list)`

Retrieve an entire list of data from an input parameter during GH_Component.SolveInstance().

**Remarks:** Parameter access by index is faster, consider using the other overload if possible.

**Parameters:**
- `name` (System.String)
- `list` (System.Collections.Generic.List<T>)

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DataAccess_GetDataList__1_1.htm)

#### `bool GetDataTree<T>(int index, out GH_Structure<T> tree) where T : IGH_Goo`

Retrieve an entire data tree from an input parameter during GH_Component.SolveInstance().

**Parameters:**
- `index` (System.Int32)
- `tree` (Grasshopper.Kernel.Data.GH_Structure<T>)

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DataAccess_GetDataTree__1.htm)

#### `bool GetDataTree<T>(string name, out GH_Structure<T> tree) where T : IGH_Goo`

Retrieve an entire data tree from an input parameter during GH_Component.SolveInstance().

**Remarks:** Parameter access by index is faster, consider using the other overload if possible.

**Parameters:**
- `name` (System.String)
- `tree` (Grasshopper.Kernel.Data.GH_Structure<T>)

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success, false on failure.

Increments the iteration count by 1.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DataAccess_IncrementIteration.htm)

#### `int ParameterTargetIndex(int paramIndex)`

Get the target index for the specified output parameter.

**Parameters:**
- `paramIndex` (System.Int32) — Index of output parameter.

**Returns:** `Int32` —

Get the target path for the specified output parameter.

- `paramIndex` (System.Int32) — Index of output parameter.

**Returns:** `GH_Path` —

#### `bool SetData(int paramIndex, Object data)`

Stores data in an output parameter during GH_Component.SolveInstance(). Use this function only for setting individual data items. If you want to set lists of data, you *must* call SetDataList() instead.

**Parameters:**
- `paramIndex` (System.Int32) — Index of the output parameter at which to store the data.
- `data` (System.Object) — Data to store. Data will be converted if necessary, but it will not be duplicated automatically.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DataAccess_SetData.htm)

#### `bool SetData(int paramIndex, Object data, int itemIndexOverride)`

Expert user function. Stores data in an output parameter during GH_Component.SolveInstance(). Use this function only for setting individual data items. If you want to set lists of data, you *must* call SetDataList() instead.

**Parameters:**
- `paramIndex` (System.Int32) — Index of the output parameter at which to store the data.
- `data` (System.Object) — Data to store. Data will be converted if necessary, but it will not be duplicated automatically.
- `itemIndexOverride` (System.Int32) — Typically the item index is a result of Data matching logic. If you want to override the item index, then call this function.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DataAccess_SetData_1.htm)

#### `bool SetData(string paramName, Object data)`

Stores data in an output parameter during GH_Component.SolveInstance().

**Remarks:** Parameter access by index is faster, consider using the other overload if possible.

**Parameters:**
- `paramName` (System.String) — Name of the output parameter at which to store the data.
- `data` (System.Object) — Data to store. Data will be converted if necessary, but it will not be duplicated automatically.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DataAccess_SetData_2.htm)

#### `bool SetDataList(int paramIndex, IEnumerable data)`

Stores a list of data in an output parameter during GH_Component.SolveInstance().

**Parameters:**
- `paramIndex` (System.Int32) — Index of the output parameter at which to store the list.
- `data` (System.Collections.IEnumerable) — Data to store. Data will be converted if necessary, but it will not be duplicated automatically.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DataAccess_SetDataList.htm)

#### `bool SetDataList(int paramIndex, IEnumerable data, int listIndexOverride)`

Expert user function. Stores a list of data in an output parameter during GH_Component.SolveInstance().

**Parameters:**
- `paramIndex` (System.Int32) — Index of the output parameter at which to store the list.
- `data` (System.Collections.IEnumerable) — Data to store. Data will be converted if necessary, but it will not be duplicated automatically.
- `listIndexOverride` (System.Int32) — Typically the branch index is a result of Data matching logic. If you want to override the branch index, then call this function.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DataAccess_SetDataList_1.htm)

#### `bool SetDataList(string paramName, IEnumerable data)`

Stores data in an output parameter during GH_Component.SolveInstance().

**Remarks:** Parameter access by index is faster, consider using the other overload if possible.

**Parameters:**
- `paramName` (System.String) — Name of the parameter at which to store the data.
- `data` (System.Collections.IEnumerable) — Data to store. Data will be converted if necessary, but it will not be duplicated automatically.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DataAccess_SetDataList_2.htm)

Folds a utility tree representation into this tree.

**Parameters:**
- `paramIndex` (System.Int32) — Index of output parameter to merge with.
- `tree` (Grasshopper.Kernel.Data.IGH_DataTree) — Tree to merge

Folds a utility tree representation into this tree.

**Parameters:**
- `paramIndex` (System.Int32) — Index of output parameter to merge with.
- `tree` (Grasshopper.Kernel.Data.IGH_Structure) — Tree to merge

#### `int Util_CountNonNullRefs<T>(List<T> L)`

Count all object references in L

**Parameters:**
- `L` (System.Collections.Generic.List<T>) — List to parse

**Returns:** `Int32` — Number of proper objects in L

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DataAccess_Util_CountNonNullRefs__1.htm)

#### `int Util_CountNullRefs<T>(List<T> L)`

Count all null references in L

**Parameters:**
- `L` (System.Collections.Generic.List<T>) — List to parse

**Returns:** `Int32` — Number of nulls in L

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DataAccess_Util_CountNullRefs__1.htm)

#### `bool Util_EnsureNonNullCount<T>(List<T> L, int N)`

Tests a list to see if it contains sufficient non-null references.

**Parameters:**
- `L` (System.Collections.Generic.List<T>) — List to parse
- `N` (System.Int32)

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if L contains N or more non-null references.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DataAccess_Util_EnsureNonNullCount__1.htm)

#### `int Util_FirstNonNullItem<T>(List<T> L)`

Returns the index of the first non-null item in a list.

**Parameters:**
- `L` (System.Collections.Generic.List<T>) — List to parse.

**Returns:** `Int32` — The index of the first non-null item or -1 if no object reference could be found.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DataAccess_Util_FirstNonNullItem__1.htm)

#### `List<T> Util_RemoveNullRefs<T>(List<T> L)`

Remove all null references from a list of items.

**Parameters:**
- `L` (System.Collections.Generic.List<T>) — List to filter

**Returns:** `List<T>` — List containing only non-null items. References are shared with L

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DataAccess_Util_RemoveNullRefs__1.htm)

### Properties
- `Iteration` (Int32, get) — Gets the current iteration count. The first time the SolveInstance() function is called on a component during a solution the Iteration counter will be zero. It will be incremented by one for every subsequent call.

## IGH_DebugDescription (interface)

Implement this interface to participate in Debug Logging.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DebugDescription.htm)

### Methods
#### `void AppendToDebugLog(GH_DebugDescriptionWriter writer)`

Write important debugging data to a debug writer object.

**Parameters:**
- `writer` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_DebugDescriptionWriter) — Object to write to.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DebugDescription_AppendToDebugLog.htm)

## IGH_DocumentObject (interface)

Base interface for objects that want to play on the Canvas. Do not implement this interface from scratch. Either inherit from GH_DocumentObject or one of the other partially finished types such as GH_ActiveObject, GH_Param(Of T) or GH_Component.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DocumentObject.htm)

### Methods
#### `void AddedToDocument(GH_Document document)`

This method will be called when an object is added to a document.

**Parameters:**
- `document` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_Document) — Document that now owns this object.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DocumentObject_AddedToDocument.htm)

#### `bool AppendMenuItems(ToolStripDropDown menu)`

This function is called when a context menu is about to be displayed. Override it to set custom items.

**Parameters:**
- `menu` (System.Windows.Forms.ToolStripDropDown) — Menu object to populate.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DocumentObject_AppendMenuItems.htm)

#### `void CreateAttributes()`

Create new default attributes for this object. You should only call this function when the Attributes() field is null.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DocumentObject_CreateAttributes.htm)

#### `void DocumentContextChanged(GH_Document document, GH_DocumentContext context)`

This method will be called when the document that owns this object moves into a different context.

**Parameters:**
- `document` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_Document) — Document that owns this object.
- `context` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_DocumentContext) — The reason for this event.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DocumentObject_DocumentContextChanged.htm)

#### `void ExpirePreview(bool redraw)`

Call this function when you suspect that the preview has expired for this object. This will cause the display cache to be eradicated.

**Parameters:**
- `redraw` (System.Boolean)

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DocumentObject_ExpirePreview.htm)

#### `void ExpireSolution(bool recompute)`

Call this function whenever you do something which expires the current solution. This will make sure all caches are erased, all downstream objects are expired and that the event is raised. The default implementation merely places a call to OnSolutionExpired(), override this function in derived classes to make sure you clear local data caches and expire downstream objects.

**Parameters:**
- `recompute` (System.Boolean) — If True, the solution will be recomputed straight away.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DocumentObject_ExpireSolution.htm)

#### `void IsolateObject()`

Destroy all connections to other objects.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DocumentObject_IsolateObject.htm)

#### `void MovedBetweenDocuments(GH_Document oldDocument, GH_Document newDocument)`

This method will be called when an object is moved from one document to another.

**Parameters:**
- `oldDocument` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_Document) — Document that used to own this object.
- `newDocument` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_Document) — Document that now owns ths object.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DocumentObject_MovedBetweenDocuments.htm)

#### `void RemovedFromDocument(GH_Document document)`

This method will be called when an object is removed from a document.

**Parameters:**
- `document` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_Document) — Document that now no longer owns this object.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DocumentObject_RemovedFromDocument.htm)

#### `void TriggerAutoSave()`

Triggers the AutoSave function on the owner document with the object_changed flag.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DocumentObject_TriggerAutoSave.htm)

#### `void TriggerAutoSave(GH_AutoSaveTrigger trigger)`

Triggers the AutoSave function on the owner document with a custom flag.

**Parameters:**
- `trigger` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_AutoSaveTrigger) — Reason for the autosave operation. It is possible that a user has decided to avoid autosave events for specific types, so if you can, try and provide a correct trigger flag.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DocumentObject_TriggerAutoSave_1.htm)

#### `void TriggerAutoSave(GH_AutoSaveTrigger trigger, Guid id)`

Triggers the AutoSave function on the owner document with a custom flag.

**Parameters:**
- `trigger` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_AutoSaveTrigger) — Reason for the autosave operation. It is possible that a user has decided to avoid autosave events for specific types, so if you can, try and provide a correct trigger flag.
- `id` (System.Guid) — ID of autosave event. Consecutive autosave requests with the same ID will be ignored.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DocumentObject_TriggerAutoSave_2.htm)

#### `void TriggerAutoSave(Guid id)`

Triggers the AutoSave function on the owner document with the object_changed flag.

**Parameters:**
- `id` (System.Guid) — ID of autosave event. Consecutive autosave requests with the same ID will be ignored.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DocumentObject_TriggerAutoSave_3.htm)

### Properties
- `Attributes` (IGH_Attributes, get/set) — Gets or sets the attributes that are associated with this object. Only set custom attributes if you know what you are doing.
- `Category` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the Category in which this object belongs. If HasCategory() returns false, this field has no meaning.
- `ComponentGuid` (Guid, get) — Returns a consistent ID for this object type. Every object must supply a unique and unchanging ID that is used to identify objects of the same type.
- `Description` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the description of the object. This field typically remains fixed during the lifetime of an object.
- `Exposure` (GH_Exposure, get) — Gets the exposure of this object in the Graphical User Interface.
- `HasCategory` (Boolean, get) — Gets whether or not the Category field has been set.
- `HasSubCategory` (Boolean, get) — Gets whether or not the SubCategory field has been set.
- `Icon_24x24` (Bitmap, get) — The icon associated with this object.
- `Icon_24x24_Locked` (Bitmap, get) — The greyscale icon of this object.
- `IconDisplayMode` (GH_IconDisplayMode, get/set) — Gets the current display mode of the object.
- `InstanceDescription` (String, get) — Gets a description of the current state of the object. This field is usually the same as the Description() field, but it might be variable when overridden.
- `InstanceGuid` (Guid, get) — Gets the ID of this runtime instance.
- `Keywords` (IEnumerable<String>, get) — Gets a list of additional keywords that describe the object. Typically this list is empty but you can override this property to aid in object searches.
- `Name` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the name of the object. This field typically remains fixed during the lifetime of an object.
- `NickName` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the nickname of the object. This field can be changed by the user.
- `Obsolete` (Boolean, get) — Gets whether this object is Obsolete.
- `SubCategory` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the SubCategory in which this object belongs. If HasSubCategory() returns false, this field has no meaning.

### Events
#### `AttributesChanged` (`Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_DocumentObject.AttributesChangedEventHandler`)

**Signature:** `event IGH_DocumentObject.AttributesChangedEventHandler AttributesChanged`

Raised whenever the number or kind of attributes changes. This event is handled by GH_Documents who subsequently wipe their attribute caches.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/E_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DocumentObject_AttributesChanged.htm)

#### `DisplayExpired` (`Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_DocumentObject.DisplayExpiredEventHandler`)

**Signature:** `event IGH_DocumentObject.DisplayExpiredEventHandler DisplayExpired`

Raised whenever the display (on the Canvas) of a certain object becomes invalid.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/E_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DocumentObject_DisplayExpired.htm)

#### `ObjectChanged` (`Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_DocumentObject.ObjectChangedEventHandler`)

**Signature:** `event IGH_DocumentObject.ObjectChangedEventHandler ObjectChanged`

Raised whenever some property of the object changes that would affect remote instances.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/E_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DocumentObject_ObjectChanged.htm)

#### `PingDocument` (`Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_DocumentObject.PingDocumentEventHandler`)

**Signature:** `event IGH_DocumentObject.PingDocumentEventHandler PingDocument`

Raised whenever an object needs to know which GH_Document it belongs to.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/E_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DocumentObject_PingDocument.htm)

#### `PreviewExpired` (`Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_DocumentObject.PreviewExpiredEventHandler`)

**Signature:** `event IGH_DocumentObject.PreviewExpiredEventHandler PreviewExpired`

Raised whenever the display (in the Rhino viewports) of a certain object becomes invalid.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/E_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DocumentObject_PreviewExpired.htm)

#### `SolutionExpired` (`Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_DocumentObject.SolutionExpiredEventHandler`)

**Signature:** `event IGH_DocumentObject.SolutionExpiredEventHandler SolutionExpired`

Raised whenever the solution of a certain object becomes invalid.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/E_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_DocumentObject_SolutionExpired.htm)

This method will be called whenever the owner document was closed.

**Parameters:**

This method will be called whenever the owned document is modified.

- `document` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_Document) — The modified document.

## IGH_EventConsumer (interface)

Implement this interface if you want to inter-operate with a GH_EventServer.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_EventConsumer.htm)

### Methods
#### `bool IsRelevantEvent(RhinoObject obj)`

Whenever a new object event is handled, this function will be called to determine whether it will cause an update.

**Parameters:**
- `obj` (RhinoObject) — Object that is the pivot of the event.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the object should cause an update, false if not.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_EventConsumer_IsRelevantEvent.htm)

#### `void PartialExpiration(GH_CustomEventServer server)`

This method will be called by the GH_CustomEventServer to indicate one or more interesting object events can now be processed.

**Parameters:**
- `server` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_CustomEventServer) — Server that called this method.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_EventConsumer_PartialExpiration.htm)

#### `void TotalExpiration(GH_CustomEventServer server)`

This method will be called by the GH_CustomEventServer to indicate all interesting object events can now be processed.

**Parameters:**
- `server` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_CustomEventServer) — Server that called this method.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_EventConsumer_TotalExpiration.htm)

## IGH_InitCodeAware (interface)

Implement this interface in your IGH_DocumentObject class if you want to handle initialization codes from Canvas Search boxes.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_InitCodeAware.htm)

### Methods
#### `void SetInitCode(string code)`

This method will be called when the user inserts an object via the Canvas Search popup and supplies an "=XXXX" string init code. Do not throw exceptions in this method! Either handle the code correctly or ignore it.

**Parameters:**
- `code` (System.String) — The portion after the first "=" symbol.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_InitCodeAware_SetInitCode.htm)

## IGH_InstanceDescription (interface)

Generate a new random instance GUID

Set the instance ID to be a specific GUID. This is very dangerous, only use this function if you're 6"4' and called David.

**Parameters:**
- `UUID` (System.Guid)

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_InstanceDescription_NewInstanceGuid_1.htm)

This method is called whenever the instance is required to deserialize itself.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_GH_IO_GH_ISerializable_Read.htm)

- `writer` (GH_IO.Serialization.GH_IWriter) — Writer object to serialize with.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success, false on failure.

### Properties
- `Category` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the Category in which this object belongs. If HasCategory() returns false, this field has no meaning.
- `Description` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the description of the object. This field typically remains fixed during the lifetime of an object.
- `HasCategory` (Boolean, get) — Gets whether or not the Category field has been set.
- `HasSubCategory` (Boolean, get) — Gets whether or not the SubCategory field has been set.
- `InstanceDescription` (String, get) — Gets a description of the current state of the object. This field is usually the same as the Description() field, but it might be variable when overridden.
- `InstanceGuid` (Guid, get) — Gets the ID of this runtime instance.
- `Keywords` (IEnumerable<String>, get) — Gets a list of additional keywords that describe the object. Typically this list is empty but you can override this property to aid in object searches.
- `Name` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the name of the object. This field typically remains fixed during the lifetime of an object.
- `NickName` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the nickname of the object. This field can be changed by the user.
- `SubCategory` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the SubCategory in which this object belongs. If HasSubCategory() returns false, this field has no meaning.

## IGH_InstanceGuidDependent (interface)

Implement this interface in your IGH_DocumentObject class if you want to be informed when the InstanceGuids of objects in the document are mutated.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_InstanceGuidDependent.htm)

### Methods
#### `void InstanceGuidsChanged(SortedDictionary<Guid, Guid> map)`

This method will be called by the GH_Document that owns this object after it mutates some or all of it's object InstanceIds.

**Parameters:**
- `map` (System.Collections.Generic.SortedDictionary<Guid,Guid>) — A translation dictionary of old InstanceID keys (no longer existent) and new InstanceID values.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_InstanceGuidDependent_InstanceGuidsChanged.htm)

## IGH_ObjectProxy (interface)

Provides proxy data for all Grasshopper components and parameters.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_ObjectProxy.htm)

### Methods
#### `IGH_DocumentObject CreateInstance()`

Create a new instance from the target object.

**Returns:** `IGH_DocumentObject` — An IGH_DocumentObject instance on success or null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_ObjectProxy_CreateInstance.htm)

#### `IGH_ObjectProxy DuplicateProxy()`

Create a duplicate of this proxy.

**Returns:** `IGH_ObjectProxy` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_ObjectProxy_DuplicateProxy.htm)

### Properties
- `Desc` (IGH_InstanceDescription, get) — Gets the Instance Description associated with this proxy.
- `Exposure` (GH_Exposure, get/set) — Gets or sets the Exposure of the target object. Only set the exposure if you know what you are doing.
- `Guid` (Guid, get) — Gets the Guid of the target object. In the case of Compiled Objects, the Guid represents the ComponentID, in the case of User Objects, the Guid is a hash of the file path.
- `Icon` (Bitmap, get) — Gets the icon for this object.
- `Kind` (GH_ObjectType, get) — Gets the kind of the Proxy target object.
- `LibraryGuid` (Guid, get) — Gets the ID of the library (GHA file) that defines this object.
- `Location` (String, get) — Gets the location of the target object. If the proxy represents a Compiled Object, the location refers to the file path of the assembly, in the case of User Objects, the location refers to the object file.
- `Obsolete` (Boolean, get) — Gets whether this proxy represents an obsolete object.
- `SDKCompliant` (Boolean, get) — Gets whether this object is compliant with the current Rhino version.
- `Type` (Type, get) — Gets the Type for the target object. If the proxy references a User Object, the Type member will be null.

## IGH_Param (interface)

Base interface for all Parameter types in Grasshopper. Do not implement this interface from scratch, derive from GH_Param or GH_PersistentParam instead.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_Param.htm)

### Methods
#### `void AddRuntimeMessage(GH_RuntimeMessageLevel Type, string Message)`

Add a new message to this object. Valid message type flags are Warning and Error. If the Message string is empty or zero-length no message is added.

**Parameters:**
- `Type` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_RuntimeMessageLevel) — Type of message. Only Warnings and Errors are recorded.
- `Message` (System.String) — Content of message.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_ActiveObject_AddRuntimeMessage.htm)

#### `void AddSource(IGH_Param source)`

Append a new Source parameter to the end of the Sources list. Sources provide this parameter with data at runtime.

**Parameters:**
- `source` (Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_Param) — Source to append.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_Param_AddSource.htm)

#### `void AddSource(IGH_Param source, int index)`

Insert a new Source parameter into the Sources list. Sources provide this parameter with data at runtime.

**Parameters:**
- `source` (Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_Param) — Source to append.
- `index` (System.Int32) — Insertion index of source.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_Param_AddSource_1.htm)

#### `bool AddVolatileData(GH_Path path, int index, Object data)`

Inserts an item of volatile data into the data structure.

**Parameters:**
- `path` (Grasshopper.Kernel.Data.GH_Path) — The branch path of the data. If the path doesn't exist yet, it will be created.
- `index` (System.Int32) — The item index of the data. If the path doesn't contain the index yet, it will be enlarged to encompass the index.
- `data` (System.Object) — The data to store.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success, False on failure. If the data cannot be converted, the topology will remain unmolested.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_Param_AddVolatileData.htm)

#### `bool AddVolatileDataList(GH_Path path, IEnumerable list)`

Inserts a list of items into the data structure.

**Parameters:**
- `path` (Grasshopper.Kernel.Data.GH_Path) — The branch path of the data. If the path doesn't exist yet, it will be created.
- `list` (System.Collections.IEnumerable) — The data list to store.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_Param_AddVolatileDataList.htm)

#### `bool AddVolatileDataTree(IGH_Structure tree)`

Insert an entire data tree into this parameter.

**Parameters:**
- `tree` (Grasshopper.Kernel.Data.IGH_Structure) — Data tree to insert.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_Param_AddVolatileDataTree.htm)

#### `void ClearData()`

This function is called whenever the object needs to clear all solution data. This usually amounts to wiping volatile caches and messages. This function will not affect any other objects, but it will set the Phase flag to Blank

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_ActiveObject_ClearData.htm)

#### `void ClearProxySources()`

Remove all proxy sources without attempting to relink them.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_Param_ClearProxySources.htm)

#### `void ClearRuntimeMessages()`

Clear all message lists.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_ActiveObject_ClearRuntimeMessages.htm)

#### `void CollectData()`

This function is called whenever the object is required to collect all data. Either from Persistent records, from source params or whatever. This step is only performed if the phase flag is Blank or Failed. Upon completion, the phase flag will be set to Collected

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_ActiveObject_CollectData.htm)

#### `void ComputeData()`

This function is called whenever the object is required to generate new data. This step is only performed by some objects and only when the Phase flag is Collected. Upon completion, the Phase will be Computed. If this object throws exceptions, it is the responsibility of the caller to set the Phase flag to Failed.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_ActiveObject_ComputeData.htm)

#### `void CreateProxySources()`

Convert all proper source parameters into proxy sources.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_Param_CreateProxySources.htm)

#### `bool DependsOn(IGH_ActiveObject PotentialSource)`

Solve the inheritance relationship between this object and a potential parental object.

**Parameters:**
- `PotentialSource` (Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_ActiveObject)

**Returns:** `Boolean` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_ActiveObject_DependsOn.htm)

#### `void RegisterRemoteIDs(GH_GuidTable id_list)`

If this object depends on Rhino Objects, you must register the UUIDs of those objects. Failure to do so will result in faulty event handling.

**Parameters:**
- `id_list` (GH_GuidTable) — The object ID database Grasshopper is responsive to.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_ActiveObject_RegisterRemoteIDs.htm)

#### `bool RelinkProxySources(GH_Document document)`

Attempt to replace all proxy sources with real sources. Proxy sources are used during file IO, when actual sources might not be available yet. Once an IO operation has been completed there should be no more proxy sources.

**Parameters:**
- `document` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_Document)

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success, false on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_Param_RelinkProxySources.htm)

#### `void RemoveAllSources()`

Remove all sources from this parameter.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_Param_RemoveAllSources.htm)

#### `void RemoveEffects()`

Remove all post-process effects.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_Param_RemoveEffects.htm)

#### `void RemoveSource(Guid source_id)`

Remove the specified source from this parameter.

**Parameters:**
- `source_id` (System.Guid) — InstanceID of source to remove.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_Param_RemoveSource_1.htm)

#### `void RemoveSource(IGH_Param source)`

Remove the specified source from this parameter.

**Parameters:**
- `source` (Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_Param) — Source to remove.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_Param_RemoveSource.htm)

#### `void ReplaceSource(Guid old_source_id, IGH_Param new_source)`

Replace an existing source with a new one. If the old_source does not exist in this parameter, nothing happens.

**Parameters:**
- `old_source_id` (System.Guid) — Source to replace.
- `new_source` (Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_Param) — Source to replace with.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_Param_ReplaceSource_1.htm)

#### `void ReplaceSource(IGH_Param old_source, IGH_Param new_source)`

Replace an existing source with a new one. If the old_source does not exist in this parameter, nothing happens.

**Parameters:**
- `old_source` (Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_Param) — Source to replace.
- `new_source` (Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_Param) — Source to replace with.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_Param_ReplaceSource.htm)

#### `IList<string> RuntimeMessages(GH_RuntimeMessageLevel level)`

Gets the list of cached runtime messages that were recorded during solver-time processes.

**Parameters:**
- `level` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_RuntimeMessageLevel) — Runtime message type to retrieve.

**Returns:** `IList<String>` — A list of runtime message strings.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_ActiveObject_RuntimeMessages.htm)

#### `bool SDKCompliancy(int exeVersion, int exeServiceRelease)`

Test whether this object is compliant with a given Rhino version.

**Parameters:**
- `exeVersion` (System.Int32) — Rhino major release (4, 5, ...).
- `exeServiceRelease` (System.Int32) — Rhino minor (service) release.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if this object is compliant with the given Rhino release.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_ActiveObject_SDKCompliancy.htm)

### Properties
- `Access` (GH_ParamAccess, get/set) — Gets or sets the Access level for this parameter.
- `DataMapping` (GH_DataMapping, get/set) — Gets or sets the data mapping of this Parameter.
- `DataType` (GH_ParamData, get) — Gets the type of the current data state.
- `HasProxySources` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether or not this parameter maintains proxy sources. Proxy sources are used during file IO, when actual sources might not be available yet. Once an IO operation has been completed there should be no more proxy sources.
- `IsDataProvider` (Boolean, get) — Gets whether or not this object is capable of emitting data.
- `Kind` (GH_ParamKind, get) — Gets the parameter kind. The kind is evaluated lazily and cached.
- `Locked` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets the locked flag of this object. Locked objects are ignored during solutions.
- `MutableNickName` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value that enables Nick name changes through the menu. The default is TRUE.
- `Optional` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets whether or not this parameter is considered optional by the owner component. Empty, non-optional parameters prevent the component from being solved.
- `Phase` (GH_SolutionPhase, get/set) — Gets the solution phase in which this object is currently stuck.
- `ProcessorTime` (TimeSpan, get) — Gets the most recent measured processor time.
- `ProxySourceCount` (Int32, get) — Gets the number of proxy sources for this parameter. Proxy sources are used during file IO, when actual sources might not be available yet. Once an IO operation has been completed there should be no more proxy sources.
- `Recipients` (IList<IGH_Param>, get) — Gets a list of all the recipients of this parameter. I.e. a recipient has this parameter as one of the sources. The Recipient list is maintained by the parameter, do not modify it yourself.
- `Reverse` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets the data reverse modifier of this parameter.
- `RuntimeMessageLevel` (GH_RuntimeMessageLevel, get) — Gets the worst-case level of all messages. I.e. if only warnings have been recorded, the level will be ::warning. If even a single error exists, the level will be ::error.
- `Simplify` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets the simplify modifier for this parameter.
- `SourceCount` (Int32, get) — Gets the number of sources for this parameter.
- `Sources` (IList<IGH_Param>, get) — Gets a list of source parameters. Do not modify this list, if you wish to add or remove sources, use dedicated functions like AddSource() and RemoveSource instead.
- `StateTags` (GH_StateTagList, get) — Gets all the StateTags that are associated with this parameter. A state tag is a visual feedback icon that represents specific internal settings.
- `Type` (Type, get) — Gets the Framework Type descriptor for the stored Data.
- `TypeName` (String, get) — Gets a human readable description of the data stored in this parameter.
- `VolatileData` (IGH_Structure, get) — Gets the instance of the volatile data tree stored in this parameter.
- `VolatileDataCount` (Int32, get) — Gets the total number of volatile data items stored in this parameter.
- `WireDisplay` (GH_ParamWireDisplay, get/set) — Gets or sets the wire display style for this parameter. Wire display only affects the wires connected to the parameter input.

## IGH_PreviewArgs (interface)

Preview display arguments for IGH_PreviewObjects.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_PreviewArgs.htm)

### Properties
- `DefaultCurveThickness` (Int32, get) — Gets the curve thickness as defined by the viewport display scheme.
- `Display` (DisplayPipeline, get) — Gets the Pipeline that is being used to draw the current preview.
- `Document` (GH_Document, get) — Gets the Grasshopper document that is currently drawing the preview.
- `MeshingParameters` (MeshingParameters, get) — Gets the meshing parameters to be used during meshing breps.
- `ShadeMaterial` (DisplayMaterial, get) — Gets the document default material for unselected Faces.
- `ShadeMaterial_Selected` (DisplayMaterial, get) — Gets the document default material for selected Faces.
- `Viewport` (RhinoViewport, get) — Gets the Viewport in which the current preview is drawn.
- `WireColour` (Color, get) — Gets the document default colour for unselected Wires.
- `WireColour_Selected` (Color, get) — Gets the document default colour for selected Wires.

## IGH_PreviewData (interface)
