#### `void DrawViewportMeshes(GH_PreviewMeshArgs args)`

Implement this function to draw all shaded meshes. If the viewport does not support shading, this function will not be called.

**Parameters:**
- `args` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_PreviewMeshArgs) — Drawing arguments.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_PreviewData_DrawViewportMeshes.htm)

#### `void DrawViewportWires(GH_PreviewWireArgs args)`

Implement this function to draw all wire and point previews.

**Parameters:**
- `args` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_PreviewWireArgs) — Drawing arguments.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_PreviewData_DrawViewportWires.htm)

### Properties
- `ClippingBox` (BoundingBox, get) — Gets the clipping box for this data. The clipping box is typically the same as the boundingbox.

## IGH_PreviewObject (interface)

All IGH_DocumentObjects that wish to participate in Viewport redraws must implement this interface.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_PreviewObject.htm)

### Methods
#### `void DrawViewportMeshes(IGH_PreviewArgs args)`

Implement this function to draw all shaded meshes. If the viewport does not support shading, this function will not be called.

**Parameters:**
- `args` (Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_PreviewArgs) — Draw operation arguments.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_PreviewObject_DrawViewportMeshes.htm)

#### `void DrawViewportWires(IGH_PreviewArgs args)`

Implement this function to draw all wire and point geometry previews.

**Parameters:**
- `args` (Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_PreviewArgs) — Draw operation arguments.

### Properties
- `ClippingBox` (BoundingBox, get) — Gets the clipping box for this object. The clipping box is typically the union of the boundingboxes of all the geometry that is drawn by this object.
- `Hidden` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value indicating whether or not this object is currently drawing preview flags.
- `IsPreviewCapable` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether or not this object (in its current state) can draw geometric previews at all.

## IGH_StateTag (interface)

A StateTag is a visual feedback mechanism that reflects a certain local setting of an Object.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_StateTag.htm)

### Methods
#### `void Render(Graphics graphics)`

Render this tag into a graphics surface.

**Parameters:**
- `graphics` (System.Drawing.Graphics) — Graphics object to draw to.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_StateTag_Render.htm)

### Properties
- `Description` (String, get) — Gets the description of this state tag.
- `Icon` (Bitmap, get) — Gets the 16 by 16 pixel icon associated with this state tag.
- `Name` (String, get) — Gets the name field of this state tag.
- `Stage` (Rectangle, get/set) — Gets or sets the Stage rectangle for this state tag. Stages are assigned during Attribute Layout operations by owner objects.
- `StateDescription` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the state description of this tag. The state description appears in the Tooltip info field.

## IGH_TaskCapableComponent (interface)

GH_Component that can choose to support the use of Tasks to compute solutions When UseTasks returns true, the component has it's SolveInstance enumeration called twice. The first pass (InPreSolve=True) is intended solely for collecting data and starting calculation tasks. The second pass (InPreSolve=False) takes results or the tasks and sets the output.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_TaskCapableComponent.htm)

### Methods
#### `void RequestTaskCancellation()`

Called by the framework when solving has been interrupted and tasks do not need to complete.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_TaskCapableComponent_RequestTaskCancellation.htm)

### Properties
- `InConstructor` (Boolean, get) — Gets the constructor flag for this instance. If InConstructor = True, it means the component is still registering parameters and the layout is not yet complete.
- `InPreSolve` (Boolean, get/set) — Is set to True by the framework right before a "use tasks" pass is made on SolveIntance calls. When True, SolveInstance should be using Tasks to start calculations on input data. When False, SolveInstance should be setting the output data by either using the result of a task or directly computing the results.
- `IsValidMasterParameterIndex` (Boolean, get) — Gets whether the MasterParameterIndex property identifies a valid parameter.
- `MasterParameterIndex` (Int32, get/set) — Gets or sets the master parameter index override. Negative indices or indices larger than or equal to the input parameter count will be ignored and regular master parameter detection will kick in.
- `Message` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets a custom message to be displayed underneath the component.
- `Params` (GH_ComponentParamServer, get) — Gets the Params object that managed input and output parameters.
- `RunCount` (Int32, get) — Gets a value indicating how many times the SolveInstance method was called on this component during the last solution. This property will return -1 if no valid runcount is available.
- `UseTasks` (Boolean, get/set) — Returns True if the component is set to support tasks. Components can have this feature turned on/off. Note that even if UseTasks returns True, there is a chance that a multipass task based solution may not be invoked depending on if multiple calls need to be made during enumeration. You must pay attention to the value of InPreSolve during SolveInstance. The set version of this property can be called by the framework if we want to provide a technique to globally turn on/off task support for all components in a definition.

## IGH_UpgradeObject (interface)

Create a class with an empty constructor that implements this interface if you want to provide an upgrade mechanism.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_UpgradeObject.htm)

### Methods
#### `IGH_DocumentObject Upgrade(IGH_DocumentObject target, GH_Document document)`

Upgrade an existing object.

**Parameters:**
- `target` (Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_DocumentObject) — Object to upgrade.
- `document` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_Document) — Document that contains the object.

**Returns:** `IGH_DocumentObject` — The newly created object on success, null on failure.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_UpgradeObject_Upgrade.htm)

### Properties
- `UpgradeFrom` (Guid, get) — Gets the ComponentGuid of the old object (the object to be updated).
- `UpgradeTo` (Guid, get) — Gets the ComponentGuid of the new object (the object that will be inserted).
- `Version` (DateTime, get) — Return a DateTime object that indicates when this upgrade mechanism was written, so that it becomes possible to distinguish competing upgrade mechanisms.

## IGH_ValueProxy (interface)

(No description provided in vendor docs for Grasshopper.Kernel.IGH_ValueProxy.)

### Methods
#### `IGH_ValueProxy Duplicate()`

Create an exact duplicate of the type.

**Remarks:** Implementors: Do not maintain references to class instances. Copy EVERYTHING.

**Returns:** `IGH_ValueProxy` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_ValueProxy_Duplicate.htm)

#### `bool FromString(string in)`

If IsParsable(), then attempts to convert the string to a generic type value

**Parameters:**
- `in` (System.String) — The String to parse

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success, false on failure. This method should not throw exceptions.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_ValueProxy_FromString.htm)

#### `string MutateString(string in)`

Munge a string to remove obvious errors on account of the user.

**Parameters:**
- `in` (System.String)

**Returns:** `String` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_ValueProxy_MutateString.htm)

#### `string ToString()`

Returns a String description of the current value.

**Remarks:** If the implementation IsParsable(), then this representation must be in parsable format.

**Returns:** `String` —

### Properties
- `IsParsable` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether or not the type can be instantiated from a String
- `IsValid` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether or not the current value is valid
- `ProxyName` (String, get) — Gets a human-friendly name description of the template type
- `ProxyType` (Type, get) — Gets the System.Type description of the template type
- `ProxyValue` (Object, get) — Gets an instance of the local template type object
- `UserString` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the user-defined string that describes this proxy This only really makes sense if the Proxy is Parsable.

## IGH_VariableParameterComponent (interface)

Implement this interface in your component if you want to enable variable parameter UI.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_VariableParameterComponent.htm)

### Methods
#### `bool CanInsertParameter(GH_ParameterSide side, int index)`

This function will get called before an attempt is made to insert a parameter. Since this method is potentially called on Canvas redraws, it must be fast.

**Parameters:**
- `side` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_ParameterSide) — Parameter side.
- `index` (System.Int32) — Insertion index of parameter. Index=0 means the parameter will be in the topmost spot.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — Return True if your component supports a variable parameter at the given location.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_VariableParameterComponent_CanInsertParameter.htm)

#### `bool CanRemoveParameter(GH_ParameterSide side, int index)`

This function will get called before an attempt is made to remove a parameter. Since this method is potentially called on Canvas redraws, it must be fast.

**Parameters:**
- `side` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_ParameterSide) — Parameter side.
- `index` (System.Int32) — Removal index of parameter.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — Return True if your component supports removing a parameter at the given location.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_VariableParameterComponent_CanRemoveParameter.htm)

#### `IGH_Param CreateParameter(GH_ParameterSide side, int index)`

This function will be called when a new parameter is about to be inserted. You must provide a valid parameter or insertion will be skipped. You do not, repeat not, need to insert the parameter yourself.

**Parameters:**
- `side` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_ParameterSide) — Parameter side.
- `index` (System.Int32) — Insertion index of parameter. Index=0 means the parameter will be in the topmost spot.

**Returns:** `IGH_Param` — A valid IGH_Param instance to be inserted.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_VariableParameterComponent_CreateParameter.htm)

#### `bool DestroyParameter(GH_ParameterSide side, int index)`

This function will be called when a parameter is about to be removed. You do not need to do anything, but this would be a good time to remove any event handlers that might be attached to the parameter in question.

**Parameters:**
- `side` (Grasshopper.Kernel.GH_ParameterSide) — Parameter side.
- `index` (System.Int32) — Removal index of parameter.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — Return True if the parameter in question can indeed be removed. Note, this is only in emergencies, typically the CanRemoveParameter function should return false if the parameter in question is not removable.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_VariableParameterComponent_DestroyParameter.htm)

#### `void VariableParameterMaintenance()`

This method will be called when a closely related set of variable parameter operations completes. This would be a good time to ensure all Nicknames and parameter properties are correct. This method will also be called upon IO operations such as Open, Paste, Undo and Redo.

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IGH_VariableParameterComponent_VariableParameterMaintenance.htm)

- `TooltipEnabled` (Boolean, get) — Gets the tooltip enabled value. If False, no further tooltip functions will be called.

## IRcpAwareObject (interface)

Implement this interface in a parameter or component if you want to be publishable on the remote control panel.

[Vendor docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/T_Grasshopper_Kernel_IRcpAwareObject.htm)

### Methods
#### `IRcpItem PublishRcpItem()`

Emit a new object to be inserted on a Remote Control Panel.

**Returns:** `IRcpItem` — 

[Docs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mcneel/grasshopper-api-docs/gh-pages/api/grasshopper/html/M_Grasshopper_Kernel_IRcpAwareObject_PublishRcpItem.htm)
